title: Git pre-commit hooks with Vite+ staged linting
description:
## Context

Developer tooling epic (greppa-3mf) set up lint/format configuration. Now codify those checks as git pre-commit hooks so violations are caught before they reach the repo. Uses Vite+'s built-in hook system (`vp config` + `vp staged`), which is already bundled in `vite-plus@0.1.14` â€” no new dependencies needed.

## Requirements

- [ ] `vp config` runs automatically after `pnpm install` via `prepare` lifecycle script
- [ ] Pre-commit hook runs `vp staged` to format and lint staged files only (no typecheck)
- [ ] `staged` config in `vite.config.ts` maps TS/TSX files to `vp lint --fix` + `vp fmt --write`, and other files to `vp fmt --write` only
- [ ] `.vite-hooks/pre-commit` is version-controlled; `.vite-hooks/_/` (dispatch scripts) is gitignored
- [ ] Hooks work across grove-managed worktrees (grove already runs `pnpm install` on `add`)
- [ ] Hooks can be bypassed with `VITE_GIT_HOOKS=0`

## Design decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Hook manager | Vite+ built-in (`vp config`) | Already bundled; creates dispatch scripts + sets `core.hooksPath`; no extra deps |
| Pre-commit scope | Staged files only via `vp staged` | Fast; lint-staged semantics bundled in vite-plus |
| Checks | Format + lint, no typecheck | tsc is whole-project; too slow for pre-commit; better in CI |
| Commit-msg hook | None | User preference; conventional format enforced by convention not tooling |
| Auto-fix | Enabled (`--fix`, `--write`) | Auto-fix formatting and lint issues on commit for zero-friction DX |

## Success criteria

1. `git config core.hooksPath` returns `.vite-hooks/_` after `pnpm install`
2. Committing a file with a lint error fails the pre-commit hook
3. Committing a file with a fixable format issue auto-fixes and succeeds
4. `VITE_GIT_HOOKS=0 git commit` bypasses hooks
5. New grove worktree gets hooks via `pnpm install` -> `prepare` -> `vp config`
design: test
close_reason: All success criteria verified: hooks path set, lint errors block commits, format auto-fixes on commit, VITE_GIT_HOOKS=0 bypass works, grove worktrees inherit hooks via prepare script.

title: Configure vp staged hooks and prepare script
description:
Wire up Vite+ hook infrastructure and staged linting config.

## Steps

1. Add `"prepare": "vp config"` script to root `package.json`
2. Add `staged` config to `vite.config.ts`:
   - `*.{ts,tsx}` -> `['vp lint --fix', 'vp fmt --write']`
   - `*.{json,md,yaml,css}` -> `'vp fmt --write'`
3. Run `pnpm install` to trigger `prepare` -> `vp config`
4. Verify `.vite-hooks/pre-commit` exists with `vp staged` content
5. Verify `.vite-hooks/_/` dispatch scripts are generated and gitignored

## Files to modify

- `/home/sqve/code/personal/greppa/main/package.json` â€” add `prepare` script
- `/home/sqve/code/personal/greppa/main/vite.config.ts` â€” add `staged` config

## Files created by `vp config` (not manually)

- `.vite-hooks/pre-commit` â€” user hook script (version-controlled)
- `.vite-hooks/_/*` â€” dispatch scripts (gitignored via `_/.gitignore`)

## Verification

- `git config core.hooksPath` returns `.vite-hooks/_`
- `ls .vite-hooks/pre-commit` exists
- Stage a TS file, commit â€” hook runs fmt + lint
- `VITE_GIT_HOOKS=0 git commit --allow-empty -m "test: bypass"` succeeds

title: Text selection in side-by-side diff spans both columns
description: Selecting text in the right side of the side-by-side diff also highlights text in the left column. The table layout allows cross-cell selection â€” need user-select isolation between the two sides so each half can be copied independently.

title: Component playground
description:
## Context

Greppa's `packages/ui` has 4 components (Badge, EmptyState, Tabs, Tree) with more planned. There is no way to visually iterate on components in isolation. STACK.md originally specified Ladle, but Ladle is effectively unmaintained (no commits since Jan 2025, untriaged issues piling up). A lightweight Vite playground app inside `packages/ui` replaces the need for any external component isolation tool.

## Design summary

A plain Vite React app at `packages/ui/playground/` that imports components, renders their variants, and supports theme switching. Co-located `*.preview.tsx` files next to each component define what to render. Auto-discovered via `import.meta.glob`.

## Requirements

1. **Playground Vite app** â€” `packages/ui/playground/` with `index.html`, `main.tsx`, `App.tsx`, `App.module.css`. Runs via `pnpm --filter @greppa/ui dev`.
2. **Preview files** â€” co-located `*.preview.tsx` next to each component. Each exports a default component rendering all meaningful variants/states. Auto-discovered via `import.meta.glob('../src/**/*.preview.tsx')`.
3. **Theme switching** â€” toolbar dropdown that sets `document.documentElement.dataset.theme`. Lists all available themes (catppuccin-latte, catppuccin-mocha, plus any added later). Tokens update instantly via CSS custom properties.
4. **Sidebar navigation** â€” lists all discovered components by name (derived from file path). Click to view in preview pane.
5. **Preview files for existing components** â€” Badge, EmptyState, Tabs, Tree each get a `*.preview.tsx` showing all variants and states.
6. **Update STACK.md** â€” replace Ladle reference with the playground approach.

## Anti-patterns

- Do not add external component isolation tools (Storybook, Ladle, Histoire).
- Do not add interactive prop controls, documentation generation, or visual regression testing â€” these are future concerns.
- Do not introduce new dependencies â€” use existing React, Vite, CSS Modules.
- Do not put playground code in `src/` â€” it must not ship in the library bundle.

## Success criteria

- `pnpm --filter @greppa/ui dev` starts the playground and opens in a browser.
- Sidebar lists all components with preview files.
- Clicking a component shows its preview with all variants rendered.
- Theme dropdown switches between catppuccin-latte and catppuccin-mocha; all components re-theme instantly.
- All four existing components (Badge, EmptyState, Tabs, Tree) have preview files.
- STACK.md no longer references Ladle.
- Playground files are excluded from the library build/exports.
- All existing tests pass.

title: Scaffold playground Vite app with theme switching
description:
## What

Create the playground Vite app shell at `packages/ui/playground/` with:
- `index.html` entry point
- `main.tsx` React root (imports `tokens.css` and theme CSS)
- `App.tsx` layout: toolbar (theme picker) + sidebar + preview pane
- `App.module.css` for playground-specific styles
- `dev` script in `packages/ui/package.json` pointing to `vite playground/`
- A single preview file (`Badge.preview.tsx`) to validate the auto-discovery pattern

## Acceptance criteria

- `pnpm --filter @greppa/ui dev` starts Vite and serves the playground.
- Theme dropdown lists catppuccin-latte and catppuccin-mocha; switching updates all tokens instantly.
- Sidebar shows "Badge" (from the auto-discovered preview file).
- Clicking "Badge" renders all Badge variants in the preview pane.
- Playground files do not affect `packages/ui` exports or build output.

## Expanded plan

### 1. Add `@vitejs/plugin-react` devDep + `dev` script to `packages/ui/package.json`
**File:** `packages/ui/package.json`
- Add `"@vitejs/plugin-react": "6.0.1"` to devDependencies (same version as web)
- Add `"scripts": { "dev": "vp dev playground/" }`

### 2. Create `packages/ui/playground/index.html`
**File:** `packages/ui/playground/index.html`
- Minimal HTML, same FOUC-prevention theme script as web's `index.html`
- `<div id="root">` + `<script type="module" src="./main.tsx">`

### 3. Create `packages/ui/playground/main.tsx`
**File:** `packages/ui/playground/main.tsx`
- Import `../src/tokens.css` (brings in all token + theme CSS)
- Import `./App`
- `createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!).render(<App />)`

### 4. Create `packages/ui/playground/App.tsx`
**File:** `packages/ui/playground/App.tsx`
- Auto-discover previews: `import.meta.glob('../src/**/*.preview.tsx', { eager: true })`
- Extract component names from file paths (e.g., `Badge` from `../src/components/Badge/Badge.preview.tsx`)
- State: `selectedComponent` (string | null), defaults to first
- Layout:
  - **Toolbar**: theme `<select>` with catppuccin-latte/catppuccin-mocha options. onChange sets `document.documentElement.dataset.theme` and persists to localStorage key `gr-theme`. Read initial from `document.documentElement.dataset.theme`.
  - **Sidebar**: `<nav>` listing component names as buttons. Active state on selected.
  - **Preview pane**: renders the selected preview's default export.
- Use CSS Modules (`App.module.css`) for layout.

### 5. Create `packages/ui/playground/App.module.css`
**File:** `packages/ui/playground/App.module.css`
- Three-area grid: toolbar (top full-width), sidebar (left), preview (right)
- Full viewport height, no scrollbar on body
- Sidebar: fixed width, vertical scroll
- Preview: overflow auto, padding
- Use existing `--gr-*` tokens for colors

### 6. Create `packages/ui/src/components/Badge/Badge.preview.tsx`
**File:** `packages/ui/src/components/Badge/Badge.preview.tsx`
- Default export component rendering all 4 Badge variants: added, deleted, modified, renamed
- Each with visible label text

### 7. Create `packages/ui/vite.config.ts`
**File:** `packages/ui/vite.config.ts`
- Minimal config: `import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'; import { defineConfig } from 'vite-plus'; export default defineConfig({ plugins: [react()] })`
- No build config needed (playground only, not library)

### 8. Exclude playground from tsconfig
**File:** `packages/ui/tsconfig.json`
- `include` already only has `["src"]` â€” playground is outside src, so it's already excluded from declaration emit. No change needed.

### 9. Run `pnpm install` to install new devDep

### 10. Verify
- `pnpm --filter @greppa/ui dev` starts without errors
- Existing tests still pass: `pnpm test`

title: Add preview files for EmptyState, Tabs, and Tree
description:
## What

Create `*.preview.tsx` files for the remaining three components so the playground sidebar lists all four.

## Files to create

1. `packages/ui/src/components/EmptyState/EmptyState.preview.tsx` â€” render with title, description, and action slot
2. `packages/ui/src/components/Tabs/Tabs.preview.tsx` â€” render with 2â€“3 tabs showing content switching
3. `packages/ui/src/components/Tree/Tree.preview.tsx` â€” render with nested items, expandable folders

## Acceptance criteria

- Sidebar lists Badge, EmptyState, Tabs, Tree.
- Each preview renders all meaningful variants/states of the component.
- No changes to component source files.

## Notes from task 1

- Preview files must `export default` a component.
- Auto-discovery glob: `../src/**/*.preview.tsx` â€” the filename before `.preview.tsx` becomes the sidebar label.

title: Per-worktree isolated development environments
description:
## Problem

Multiple agents (and humans) work in git worktrees simultaneously. Each worktree needs its own server, web dev server, and database â€” but ports are hardcoded (API: 4400, Vite proxy: 4400), so only one worktree can run at a time.

## Requirements

1. Running `pnpm dev` in any worktree starts both server and web dev server on non-conflicting ports
2. Two worktrees can run `pnpm dev` simultaneously without port conflicts
3. The "main" worktree uses API port 4400 and Vite port 5173
4. `grove add <branch>` creates a worktree with `.env.local` already configured
5. Server accepts `--port` flag override, falls back to `API_PORT` env var, falls back to 4400
6. `.env.local` and `.data/` are gitignored
7. Same worktree directory name always produces the same ports (deterministic)

## Success criteria

1. `pnpm dev` in any worktree starts server + web dev server on unique, non-conflicting ports
2. Two worktrees running `pnpm dev` simultaneously have no port conflicts
3. "main" worktree preserves API port 4400 and Vite port 5173
4. `grove add <branch>` produces a worktree with `.env.local` pre-configured
5. `--port` flag > `API_PORT` env var > 4400 default (priority chain works)
6. `.env.local` and `.data/` are in `.gitignore`
7. Running `scripts/worktree-env.ts` twice on same worktree produces identical output

## Solution

A TypeScript script that deterministically assigns ports per worktree using FNV-1a hashing. Generates `.env.local` per worktree; all tools read from env vars.

## Architecture

### Port resolver script (`scripts/worktree-env.ts`)
- Detects worktree root via `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`
- Hashes the worktree directory basename using FNV-1a
- API port range: 3100â€“3999, Vite dev port range: 5100â€“5999
- Special case: directory named "main" â†’ API 4400, Vite 5173 (preserves current convention)
- Writes `.env.local` at worktree root with: `API_PORT`, `DEV_PORT`, `DB_PATH`
- `DB_PATH` = `<worktreeRoot>/.data/app.db`
- Run via `pnpm run env:setup` (uses tsx)

### Grove integration (`.grove.toml`)
- Add hook runs `pnpm run env:setup` after `pnpm install`
- Preserve `.env.local` so it survives worktree operations

### Vite config (`packages/web/vite.config.ts`)
- Use `loadEnv()` to read `API_PORT` and `DEV_PORT` from `.env.local`
- Dynamic proxy: `http://localhost:${API_PORT}`
- Dynamic dev server port from `DEV_PORT`

### Server CLI (`packages/cli/src/commands/serve.ts`)
- Read `API_PORT` env var as default when no `--port` flag given
- Fall back to 4400 if neither env var nor flag present

### SQLite isolation
- DB path from `DB_PATH` env var (not yet wired â€” DB layer doesn't exist yet)
- Each worktree gets its own `.data/app.db`

### Dev command (`pnpm dev` at root)
- Runs `env:setup` first (if `.env.local` missing)
- Starts server + Vite dev server in parallel

### Gitignore
- Add `.env.local` and `.data/` entries

## Key decisions
- FNV-1a hash: deterministic, same worktree name always gets same ports
- TypeScript via tsx: no bash scripts in a TS project
- Env vars via loadEnv(): avoids Vite configLoader workspace import issue (vitejs/vite#5370)
- 900-port ranges: ample space for 6+ concurrent worktrees with negligible collision risk
- "main" special case: preserves existing port 4400 habit

## Architecture

- Writes `.env.local` at worktree root with: `API_PORT`, `DEV_PORT`, `DB_PATH`
- `DB_PATH` = `<worktreeRoot>/.data/app.db`
- Run via `pnpm run env:setup` (uses tsx)

### Grove integration (`.grove.toml`)

- Preserve `.env.local` so it survives worktree operations

### Vite config (`packages/web/vite.config.ts`)
- Use `loadEnv()` to read `API_PORT` and `DEV_PORT` from `.env.local`
- Dynamic proxy: `http://localhost:${API_PORT}`

- Read `API_PORT` env var as default when no `--port` flag given

## Outcome

**Completed:** 2026-03-26
**Tasks:** 3 tasks completed
**Summary:** Built a deterministic port resolver (`scripts/worktreeEnv.ts`) using FNV-1a hashing that generates `.env.local` per worktree. Wired server CLI and Vite config to read ports from env vars with proper fallback chains. Root `pnpm dev` runs env:check then starts both processes in parallel via `pnpm -r --parallel`. Added `strictPort: true` to Vite config so port conflicts fail loudly rather than silently incrementing.
**Verification:** All tests passing (13/13), linter clean, typecheck clean, all 7 success criteria met with evidence.

title: Create worktree-env.ts port resolver script
description:
## Goal

Create `scripts/worktree-env.ts` â€” the core port resolver that generates `.env.local` per worktree with deterministic ports and DB path.

## Behaviors

1. FNV-1a hash of a string returns a consistent 32-bit unsigned integer
2. Hash mapped to API port range 3100â€“3999 and Vite port range 5100â€“5999
3. Basename "main" bypasses hash and returns API 4400, Vite 5173
4. Worktree root detected via `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`
5. DB path resolves to `<worktreeRoot>/.data/app.db`
6. `.env.local` written at worktree root with `API_PORT`, `DEV_PORT`, `DB_PATH`
7. Running the script twice on the same worktree produces identical `.env.local`

## Success criteria

- `pnpm run env:setup` generates a valid `.env.local` with API_PORT, DEV_PORT, DB_PATH
- Running it twice produces identical output (deterministic)
- "main" directory gets ports 4400/5173
- Other directory names get deterministic ports in their respective ranges
- `.env.local` and `.data/` added to `.gitignore`

## Details

- Implement FNV-1a hash function (pure TS, no dependencies)
- Map hash to port ranges using modulo
- Add `env:setup` script to root `package.json`: `"env:setup": "tsx scripts/worktree-env.ts"`

title: Wire server and Vite to read ports from env vars
description:
## Goal

Make server CLI and Vite dev server read ports from environment variables so they use worktree-specific values from `.env.local`.

## Behaviors

1. Server CLI: `--port` flag > `API_PORT` env var > 4400 default
2. Vite config: proxy target reads `API_PORT`, dev server port reads `DEV_PORT`
3. Vite uses `loadEnv()` to load `.env.local` values
4. Grove hook runs `pnpm run env:setup` after `pnpm install` on worktree add

## Success criteria

- `serve --port 9999` uses 9999
- `serve` without flag uses `API_PORT` env var if set, else 4400
- Vite dev server starts on `DEV_PORT` and proxies to `API_PORT`
- `grove add <branch>` runs env:setup as part of its hook chain

## Details

- `packages/cli/src/commands/serve.ts`: change `Flag.withDefault(4400)` to read `API_PORT` env var
- `packages/web/vite.config.ts`: use `loadEnv()` for `API_PORT`/`DEV_PORT`
- `.grove.toml`: append `pnpm run env:setup` to add hook
- `.env.local` must be in `.grove.toml` preserve patterns

title: Wire pnpm dev to run env:setup then start server and Vite in parallel
description:
## Goal

Replace the placeholder `pnpm dev` script with a command that runs `env:setup` (if `.env.local` missing), then starts server + Vite dev server in parallel.

## Behaviors

1. `pnpm dev` runs `env:setup` first if `.env.local` doesn't exist
2. Then starts server and Vite dev server in parallel
3. Both read ports from env vars set up by `env:setup`

## Success criteria

- `pnpm dev` starts both server and Vite dev server
- Each process uses ports from `.env.local`
- Ctrl+C stops both processes

## Details

- Root `package.json` `dev` script: run env:setup then start both processes
- Options: `npm-run-all`, `concurrently`, or turbo/vp built-in
- Check what vp (vite-plus) offers for parallel dev

title: Add hello world backend endpoint
description:
## Context

Add a `/api/hello` endpoint to the server package that returns a greeting message. Follows the same Effect HttpApi pattern established by the health endpoint.

## Requirements

- [ ] GET `/api/hello` returns `{ message: "Hello, World!" }` with 200 status
- [ ] Endpoint defined as its own `HttpApiGroup` in a dedicated file
- [ ] Registered in the top-level `Api` composition
- [ ] Handlers wired in `Http.ts`
- [ ] Integration test passing via `HttpRouter.toWebHandler` (same pattern as health test)

## Design decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Separate file | `HelloApi.ts` | Matches `HealthApi.ts` pattern â€” one file per API group |
| Response shape | `{ message: string }` | Simple, demonstrates string response vs health's literal |

## Acceptance criteria

1. `vp test` passes with new hello endpoint test
2. `curl http://localhost:4400/api/hello` returns `{"message":"Hello, World!"}` with 200
3. No regressions on existing health endpoint

title: Implement /api/hello endpoint with test
description:
## What

Add a hello world GET endpoint at `/api/hello` returning `{ message: "Hello, World!" }`.

## Files to modify/create

- **Create** `packages/server/src/HelloApi.ts` â€” endpoint declaration
- **Edit** `packages/server/src/Api.ts` â€” add `HelloApi` to composition
- **Edit** `packages/server/src/Http.ts` â€” add `HelloHandlers` layer + provide to `ApiRoutes`
- **Edit** `packages/server/src/Http.test.ts` â€” add integration test

## Implementation notes

Follow exact patterns from `HealthApi.ts` / `Http.ts`:
- `HttpApiGroup.make("hello").add(HttpApiEndpoint.get("getHello", "/api/hello", { success: ... }))`
- `HttpApiBuilder.group(Api, "hello", (handlers) => ...)`
- `Layer.provide` both `HealthHandlers` and `HelloHandlers` to `ApiRoutes`
- Test with `handler(new Request("http://localhost/api/hello"))` asserting status 200 and JSON body

## Acceptance criteria

- `vp test` passes
- Manual: `vp dev` then `curl http://localhost:4400/api/hello` returns `{"message":"Hello, World!"}`

title: Add hello world backend endpoint
description:
Add a GET /api/hello endpoint that returns { message: "Hello, world!" }.

## Success criteria

- GET /api/hello returns 200 with JSON body { message: "Hello, world!" }
- Endpoint follows existing patterns: HttpApiGroup in its own file, added to Api, handler in Http.ts
- Tests pass: endpoint returns expected response, unknown routes still 404
- vp check passes (lint, format, typecheck)

## Constraints

- Follow the HealthApi pattern exactly (separate ApiGroup file, Schema-typed response)
- No new dependencies
- Single task â€” this is trivial scope

title: Add GET /api/hello endpoint with HelloApi group
description:
Add a hello world endpoint following the HealthApi pattern.

## Implementation plan

1. Create packages/server/src/HelloApi.ts
   - HttpApiGroup named "hello"
   - Single GET endpoint "getHello" at /api/hello
   - Success schema: Schema.Struct({ message: Schema.String })

2. Update packages/server/src/Api.ts
   - Import HelloApi
   - Chain .add(HelloApi) after .add(HealthApi)

3. Update packages/server/src/Http.ts
   - Add HelloHandlers: HttpApiBuilder.group(Api, "hello", ...) returning { message: "Hello, world!" }
   - Provide HelloHandlers to ApiRoutes layer

4. Update packages/server/src/Http.test.ts
   - Add test: GET /api/hello returns 200 with { message: "Hello, world!" }

## Verification

- pnpm test passes
- vp check passes

title: greppa-3gt
description: Sync selectedFilePath with URL search params (?file=path). Read initial file from URL on mount. Update URL on file selection. Handle browser back/forward via popstate. No routing library needed â€” use native URL API + history.pushState.

title: Diff API â€” server content delivery and client-side VSCode-quality diffing
description:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Shared types defined as Effect Schemas in @greppa/core (ChangeType, FileEntry, DiffResponse); TS types derived via Schema.Type; both server and web import from @greppa/core
- GET /api/files endpoint accepts oldRef and newRef query params; returns array of { path, changeType, oldPath? } computed via git diff --name-status oldRef newRef
- GET /api/diff/:oldRef/:newRef/*path endpoint returns { path, oldPath?, changeType, oldContent, newContent } computed via git show oldRef:path and git show newRef:path; empty string for added files oldContent and deleted files newContent
- Server resolves git repo from CLI --repo arg, falling back to CWD; validates the path is a git repository on startup
- Git spawning via Effect ChildProcess from effect/unstable/process; non-zero exit codes produce typed errors
- Client fetches via TanStack Query â€” useFileList(oldRef, newRef) and useDiffContent(oldRef, newRef, path)
- Client computes diffs in Web Worker using vscode-diff DefaultLinesDiffComputer; returns DetailedLineRangeMapping[] with innerChanges
- Two-tier diff rendering â€” light background on changed lines, darker highlight on exact character ranges from innerChanges
- Syntax highlighting runs on full file content (not isolated diff lines) for correct parser state

## Success criteria
- [ ] GET /api/files?oldRef=HEAD~1&newRef=HEAD returns file list matching git diff --name-status output
- [ ] GET /api/diff/HEAD~1/HEAD/src/some-file.ts returns oldContent and newContent matching git show output
- [ ] Server rejects invalid refs with typed error response
- [ ] Client renders diff with two-tier highlighting: light background on changed lines, darker highlight on exact character ranges
- [ ] Syntax highlighting works on full file content
- [ ] Diff computation runs in Web Worker, never blocks main thread
- [ ] Selecting an uncached file shows its diff (loading state â†’ rendered) without error
- [ ] Fixture mode still works for development when no server is running
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO parsing of git diff unified output (reason: raw content + VSCode DefaultLinesDiffComputer produces better line pairing and character-level diffs than any post-hoc refinement of git Myers output)
- NO diff computation on main thread (reason: DefaultLinesDiffComputer can block on large files; Web Worker keeps UI responsive)
- NO eager fetch of all file contents on session start (reason: fetch on file selection only; greppa-30u layers pre-fetching and caching on top)
- NO removal of fixture infrastructure (reason: keep fixtures working for dev/testing alongside the real API until proven stable)
- NO custom diff algorithm (reason: vscode-diff is the exact VSCode algorithm, zero-dependency, maintained)
- NO highlighting individual diff lines in isolation (reason: with full file content available, highlight the complete file for correct parser state)

## Durable decisions
- Diff library: vscode-diff DefaultLinesDiffComputer â€” zero deps, exact VSCode algorithm, native two-tier innerChanges output
- API shape: /api/files?oldRef&newRef for file list, /api/diff/:oldRef/:newRef/*path for content
- Data flow: server delivers raw content â†’ two parallel Web Workers (Shiki for syntax tokens, vscode-diff for diff ranges) â†’ renderer composites both
- Schema location: @greppa/core as Effect Schemas, TS types derived via Schema.Type
- Data fetching: TanStack Query with hooks per resource (useFileList, useDiffContent)

## Approach
Server delivers raw file contents from git; client computes VSCode-quality diffs using vscode-diff (DefaultLinesDiffComputer) in a Web Worker. This architecture was chosen because VSCode's diff algorithm produces superior line pairing and character-level diffs compared to parsing git diff output. The algorithm uses Myers + DP with size-based selection, heuristic post-processing, and holistic block-level character diffing â€” the same engine codediff.nvim ported to C.

The server is intentionally thin: spawn git commands, return content. Two endpoints serve file list (git diff --name-status) and file content (git show). Effect ChildProcess handles process spawning with typed errors. The client owns diff computation and rendering, with TanStack Query managing data fetching state.

Syntax highlighting improves under this architecture â€” full file content gives Shiki complete parser context instead of isolated diff lines. The Shiki and diff workers run in parallel since they are independent.

## Architecture
- @greppa/core: Effect Schemas for ChangeType, FileEntry, DiffResponse; shared between server and web
- @greppa/server: GitService (Effect service wrapping ChildProcess for git commands), DiffApi HttpApiGroup (two endpoints), wired into existing HttpApiBuilder alongside HealthApi
- @greppa/cli: --repo flag on serve command, provides repo path to server via Effect Layer
- @greppa/web: TanStack Query hooks (useFileList, useDiffContent), diff Web Worker using vscode-diff, updated DiffViewer consuming computed diff results with two-tier highlighting, updated Shiki worker highlighting full file content

## Design rationale

### Problem
The app loads all diff data from bundled JSON fixtures. It cannot serve real git repositories. This is the foundational prerequisite for all production use and for the caching/pipeline epic (greppa-30u).

### Research findings

**Codebase:**
- packages/server/src/Http.ts â€” HttpApiBuilder pattern with HealthApi group, Effect Layer composition
- packages/server/src/HealthApi.ts â€” HttpApiEndpoint/HttpApiGroup pattern to follow
- packages/web/src/fixtures/types.ts â€” current DiffFile, DiffHunk, DiffLine, FileNode, ChangeType types
- packages/web/src/fixtures/index.ts â€” bundled JSON imports, Map-based lookup by path
- packages/web/src/App.tsx â€” imports fixtures directly, passes to useFileSelection
- packages/web/src/workers/highlightEngine.ts â€” Shiki Web Worker with line-by-line highlighting
- packages/core/src/index.ts â€” empty placeholder, ready for shared types

**External:**
- vscode-diff npm (v3.0.1, ~33K weekly DL, zero deps) â€” standalone extraction of VSCode diff algorithm
- VSCode algorithm: Myers + DP with size-based selection, heuristic post-processing, holistic block-level char diffing
- Effect v4 beta: ChildProcess from effect/unstable/process, ChildProcessSpawner for spawner.string(cmd)
- codediff.nvim: C port of same VSCode algorithm, confirms approach produces high-quality two-tier diffs

### Approaches considered

#### 1. Raw content + client-side VSCode diffing (selected)

**What:** Server sends old/new file content via git show. Client computes line + character diffs using vscode-diff DefaultLinesDiffComputer in a Web Worker.
**Pros:** Best diff quality (VSCode algorithm), simplest server, natural Web Worker fit, full file content improves syntax highlighting.
**Cons:** Client does more work (mitigated by Web Worker).
**Chosen because:** VSCode's algorithm produces superior line pairing and character-level diffs. codediff.nvim validated this approach by porting the same algorithm.

#### 2. Parse git diff on server (rejected)

**What:** Server spawns git diff, parses unified output into DiffFile with pre-computed hunks.
**Why explored:** Simpler initial architecture, less client computation.
**Why rejected:** Caps diff quality at git's Myers algorithm. No native character-level pairing â€” would need post-hoc refinement that produces inferior results.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** VSCode-quality diffs prove unnecessary and performance requires server-side computation.

#### 3. Monaco DiffEditor widget (rejected)

**What:** Embed Monaco's DiffEditor for both computation and rendering.
**Why explored:** Monaco includes the diff algorithm and a complete diff UI.
**Why rejected:** Pulls in entire Monaco editor bundle (~4MB) for just the diff algorithm. Replaces custom DiffViewer, losing control over rendering.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We need Monaco's full editor features (editing, intellisense) in the diff view.

### Scope boundaries

**In scope:** File list endpoint, diff content endpoint, client-side VSCode-quality diffing, two-tier highlighting, TanStack Query integration, full-file syntax highlighting
**Out of scope:** Comments API (separate concern), file info API (separate concern), IndexedDB caching (greppa-30u), WebSocket push (greppa-30u), pre-fetching (greppa-30u)

### Open questions
- Web Worker message protocol for diff computation (design during task expansion)
- Binary file handling in API response (detect and return flag, no content)
- Language detection from file extension â€” likely client-side from path, matching current fixture pattern

## Design discovery

### Key decisions made

| Question | Answer | Implication |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| Types: plain interfaces vs Effect Schemas? | Effect Schemas in @greppa/core | Single source of truth; server gets runtime validation; client imports derived types |
| Diff computation: server or client? | Client via vscode-diff in Web Worker | Server just delivers raw content; client gets VSCode-quality two-tier diffs |
| Diff library | vscode-diff DefaultLinesDiffComputer | Zero deps, exact VSCode algorithm, native innerChanges for char-level |
| API shape | REST with path params for refs, splat for file path | Cacheable URLs, clean REST semantics |
| Repo path | CLI --repo arg, CWD fallback | Natural ergonomics: cd my-repo && greppa serve |
| Data fetching | TanStack Query | Loading/error states, caching, integrates with existing TanStack Router |
| Scope | File list + diffs only | Comments/file info stay as fixtures for now |
| Syntax highlighting | Full file content, not isolated lines | Better parser state, parallel with diff worker |

### Research deep-dives

#### VSCode diff algorithm availability
**Question:** Can VSCode's diff algorithm be used standalone without Monaco?
**Sources:** vscode-diff npm, Monaco source, codediff.nvim ATTRIBUTION.md
**Conclusion:** vscode-diff extracts the exact algorithm as a zero-dep package. DefaultLinesDiffComputer.computeDiff() takes string arrays and returns DetailedLineRangeMapping[] with innerChanges.

#### Character-level diff approach
**Question:** How does VSCode compute character-level diffs within changed blocks?
**Sources:** codediff.nvim C port analysis, VSCode source at src/vs/editor/common/diff/
**Conclusion:** Entire removed + added blocks are concatenated as flat character streams and diffed holistically. No explicit line pairing. innerChanges contain (line, col) ranges mapped back via offset arrays. This produces better results than line-by-line pairing + per-pair char diff.

#### Effect v4 child process API
**Question:** How to spawn git commands in Effect v4 beta?
**Sources:** effect/unstable/process/ChildProcess, @effect/platform-node
**Conclusion:** ChildProcess.make("git", [...args]) + ChildProcessSpawner.string(cmd) for stdout. Non-zero exit does not auto-fail â€” must check explicitly. Requires NodeServices.layer.

### Dead-end paths

#### gitdiff-parser / parse-git-diff
**Why explored:** Initially planned to parse git diff unified output on the server
**What found:** Libraries work fine for parsing, but the approach itself was wrong
**Why abandoned:** Parsing git diff caps quality at git's algorithm. VSCode's algorithm on raw content produces better results.

#### Monaco headless diff
**Why explored:** Monaco includes the diff algorithm internally
**What found:** No public API for headless use. Requires full service infrastructure and DOM.
**Why abandoned:** vscode-diff provides the same algorithm without Monaco's bundle.

#### diff-match-patch for character diffs
**Why explored:** Google's library with excellent semantic cleanup
**What found:** Character-level only, no line-level structure. Would require DIY line splitting and pairing.
**Why abandoned:** vscode-diff handles both tiers natively.

### Open concerns raised
- Binary files â†’ detect via git, return flag with no content, render placeholder in UI
- Language detection â†’ derive from file extension client-side (same as current fixture pattern)
- Worker protocol â†’ design during first task expansion, similar to existing Shiki worker pattern

title: File list endpoint and client fetch
description:
## Goal
Serve the file list from a real git repository and render it in the client via TanStack Query. This proves the full vertical slice: shared schemas in core, git spawning on the server, HTTP endpoint, and client data fetching â€” replacing the fixtures-based file tree with live data.

## Execution mode
AFK

## Behaviors
1. @greppa/core exports a ChangeType Effect Schema (literal union: added, modified, deleted, renamed) and a FileEntry Schema ({ path, changeType, oldPath? })
2. @greppa/server GitService spawns git diff --name-status oldRef newRef and parses the tabular output into FileEntry[]
3. Server rejects invalid git refs with a typed error (non-zero exit from git)
4. GET /api/files?oldRef=X&newRef=Y returns FileEntry[] via HttpApiEndpoint wired into existing Api
5. @greppa/web useFileList(oldRef, newRef) hook fetches via TanStack Query and returns the file list
6. App renders the file tree from API data when server is available, falling back to fixtures when not

## References
- packages/server/src/HealthApi.ts:1-9 â€” HttpApiEndpoint + HttpApiGroup pattern
- packages/server/src/Http.ts:1-17 â€” HttpApiBuilder.group handler pattern, Layer composition
- packages/server/src/Http.test.ts:1-43 â€” WebHandler test pattern (no real server needed)
- packages/web/src/fixtures/types.ts:1-12 â€” current FileNode and ChangeType types
- packages/web/src/fixtures/index.ts:1-36 â€” current fixture loading pattern to eventually replace

## Success criteria
- [ ] @greppa/core exports ChangeType and FileEntry Effect Schemas with derived TS types
- [ ] GET /api/files?oldRef=HEAD~1&newRef=HEAD returns correct file list from a real git repo
- [ ] Invalid refs return typed error response

title: Diff content endpoint and client hook
description:
## Goal

Serve raw old/new file content from git for a given file path and refs. Client fetches via TanStack Query hook. This enables the client-side diff computation that follows.

## Execution mode

AFK

## Behaviors

1. @greppa/core exports a DiffResponse Effect Schema ({ path, oldPath?, changeType, oldContent, newContent })
2. @greppa/server GitService gains getFileContent(ref, path) method that spawns git show ref:path and returns string content; returns empty string for non-existent paths (added/deleted files)
3. GET /api/diff/:oldRef/:newRef/*path returns DiffResponse by looking up changeType from git diff --name-status, then fetching old and new content via git show
4. Server rejects invalid refs or paths with typed error
5. @greppa/web useDiffContent(oldRef, newRef, path) hook fetches via TanStack Query, enabled only when path is truthy
6. Fixture fallback: when API is unavailable, existing fixture data is used for diff content

## References

- packages/core/src/index.ts â€” existing ChangeType and FileEntry schemas
- packages/server/src/GitService.ts â€” existing GitService with listFiles
- packages/server/src/FilesApi.ts â€” existing HttpApiGroup pattern
- packages/server/src/Http.ts â€” existing handler wiring pattern
- packages/web/src/hooks/useFileList.ts â€” existing TanStack Query hook pattern

## Success criteria

- [ ] @greppa/core exports DiffResponse Effect Schema with derived TS type
- [ ] GitService.getFileContent returns file content for valid ref:path
- [ ] GitService.getFileContent returns empty string for non-existent paths
- [ ] GET /api/diff/HEAD~1/HEAD/path returns correct oldContent and newContent
- [ ] Invalid refs return typed error response
- [ ] Server tests pass using WebHandler pattern
- [ ] Client useDiffContent hook fetches and returns diff content via TanStack Query
- [ ] Fixture fallback works when server is unavailable
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing
design:
# Expanded implementation plan

## Codebase findings

### Patterns to follow
- `packages/core/src/index.ts:5-18` â€” Effect Schema pattern: `Schema.Struct`, `Schema.Union`, `Schema.optional`, derive type via `typeof X.Type`
- `packages/server/src/GitService.ts:48-69` â€” `runGit(args)` helper spawns git, collects stdout/stderr, checks exit code
- `packages/server/src/GitService.ts:71-87` â€” `GitService` is `ServiceMap.Service` with method signatures; `GitServiceLive` is `Layer.succeed`
- `packages/server/src/FilesApi.ts:6-12` â€” `HttpApiGroup.make(name).add(HttpApiEndpoint.get(...))` pattern
- `packages/server/src/Api.ts:6` â€” `HttpApi.make('greppa').add(Group1).add(Group2)` chain
- `packages/server/src/Http.ts:15-22` â€” handler group: `HttpApiBuilder.group(Api, name, handlers => Effect.gen(...))`
- `packages/server/src/Http.test.ts:10-34` â€” WebHandler test setup with `PlatformLayer`, `TestAppLayer`, `handler`, `dispose`
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useFileList.ts:77-100` â€” TanStack Query hook pattern: `fetchFn` + `useQuery`

### Key observations
- `runGit` already exists as a reusable helper â€” can call it for `git show ref:path`
- `GitService` currently has only `listFiles` â€” needs `getFileContent` added
- DiffViewer consumes `DiffFile` (with hunks) â€” this task adds the raw content fetch; the diff Worker task will transform raw content to DiffFile
- For this task, we don't need to change DiffViewer â€” it still consumes fixture DiffFile. The hook just fetches raw content.

## TDD cycles

### Cycle 1: DiffResponse schema in @greppa/core

**RED** â€” packages/core/src/index.test.ts â€” add DiffResponse describe block
**GREEN** â€” packages/core/src/index.ts â€” add DiffResponse Schema.Struct with path, changeType, oldPath optional, oldContent, newContent
**Verify:** pnpm --filter @greppa/core test

### Cycle 2: GitService.getFileContent â€” spawns git show ref:path

**RED** â€” packages/server/src/GitService.test.ts â€” add `getFileContent` tests:
  - returns content for valid ref:path (use HEAD:package.json which always exists)
  - fails with GitError for invalid ref

**GREEN** â€” packages/server/src/GitService.ts:
  - Add `getFileContent` to GitService type: `(ref: string, path: string) => Effect<string, GitError, ...>`
  - Add to GitServiceLive: calls `runGit(['show', \`${ref}:${path}\`])`

**Verify:** pnpm --filter @greppa/server test

### Cycle 3: DiffApi endpoint + handler wiring

**RED** â€” packages/server/src/Http.test.ts â€” add `GET /api/diff/:oldRef/:newRef/*path` tests:
  - returns 200 with oldContent/newContent for valid file (use a file from HEAD~1..HEAD)
  - returns error for invalid refs

**GREEN** â€” 3 files:
1. packages/server/src/DiffApi.ts â€” new HttpApiGroup with getDiff endpoint, path params for oldRef/newRef, splat for file path
2. packages/server/src/Api.ts â€” add `.add(DiffApi)`
3. packages/server/src/Http.ts â€” add DiffHandlers group:
   - Extract changeType from listFiles result for the given path
   - Fetch oldContent via getFileContent(oldRef, path) â€” empty string for 'added'
   - Fetch newContent via getFileContent(newRef, path) â€” empty string for 'deleted'
   - Return DiffResponse shape

**Verify:** pnpm --filter @greppa/server test

### Cycle 4: useDiffContent client hook

**RED** â€” packages/web/src/hooks/useDiffContent.test.ts â€” test the fetch function (mock fetch)
**GREEN** â€” packages/web/src/hooks/useDiffContent.ts:
  - `fetchDiffContent(oldRef, newRef, path)` â€” fetches `/api/diff/${oldRef}/${newRef}/${path}`
  - `useDiffContent(oldRef, newRef, path)` â€” useQuery with enabled: path != null

**Verify:** pnpm --filter @greppa/web test

### Cycle 5: Integrate hook in App with fixture fallback

No test needed â€” integration wiring. App.tsx calls useDiffContent for selectedFilePath, falls back to fixture diffs when API unavailable.

**Verify:** pnpm test (all), pre-commit hooks

## Files touched

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| packages/core/src/index.ts | Edit â€” add DiffResponse schema |
| packages/core/src/index.test.ts | Edit â€” add DiffResponse tests |
| packages/server/src/GitService.ts | Edit â€” add getFileContent to service |
| packages/server/src/GitService.test.ts | Edit â€” add getFileContent tests |
| packages/server/src/DiffApi.ts | New â€” HttpApiGroup with getDiff endpoint |
| packages/server/src/Api.ts | Edit â€” add DiffApi |
| packages/server/src/Http.ts | Edit â€” add DiffHandlers |
| packages/server/src/Http.test.ts | Edit â€” add /api/diff tests |
| packages/web/src/hooks/useDiffContent.ts | New â€” TanStack Query hook |
| packages/web/src/hooks/useDiffContent.test.ts | New â€” hook tests |
| packages/web/src/App.tsx | Edit â€” integrate useDiffContent with fallback |

title: Diff computation Web Worker with vscode-diff
description:
## Goal

Compute line and character-level diffs client-side using vscode-diff DefaultLinesDiffComputer in a Web Worker. Takes raw old/new file content from the API (useDiffContent) and produces DetailedLineRangeMapping[] with innerChanges. This replaces the current fixture-based DiffFile hunks as the source of diff data.

## Execution mode

AFK

## Behaviors

1. Install vscode-diff package in @greppa/web
2. Create diffProtocol.ts with request/response types mirroring highlightProtocol pattern
3. Create diffWorker.ts that receives old/new content strings and runs DefaultLinesDiffComputer.computeDiff()
4. Create useDiffComputation hook that sends old/new content to worker and returns computed diff mappings
5. Wire App.tsx to use useDiffContent â†’ useDiffComputation pipeline instead of fixture hunks when API data is available
6. Fixture fallback remains working when API is unavailable
7. Diff computation never runs on main thread

## References

- packages/web/src/workers/highlightWorker.ts â€” existing worker pattern to follow
- packages/web/src/workers/highlightProtocol.ts â€” existing protocol pattern
- packages/web/src/hooks/useDiffContent.ts â€” source of old/new content
- packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/DiffViewer.tsx â€” consumer of diff data
- packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/buildRows.ts â€” current row building from DiffHunk

## Success criteria

- [ ] vscode-diff installed as dependency
- [ ] Diff worker computes diffs without blocking main thread
- [ ] useDiffComputation returns DetailedLineRangeMapping[] with innerChanges
- [ ] DiffViewer can consume both fixture data and computed diff data
- [ ] Tests cover worker protocol and hook behavior

title: Add unified/split diff toggle
description:
## Goal

Add a toggle in the diff header area to switch between side-by-side (split) and unified diff views.

## Why

Both views have tradeoffs â€” split is better for comparing changes, unified is better for reading context. The stitch mockup shows this as a segmented control in the header. Resolved the open question in VISION.md.

## Behaviors

- A toggle control is visible when a diff file is selected.
- Clicking "Unified" renders the diff as a single-column unified view.
- Clicking "Split" renders the diff as the existing side-by-side view.
- The selected mode persists across file selection within a session.

## Success criteria

- Toggle renders in the header/toolbar area.
- Unified view shows additions and deletions interleaved in one column with +/- gutter indicators.
- Split view matches current behavior.
- Selected mode survives file switching.
- All existing DiffViewer tests pass.

title: Shiki syntax highlighting in Web Worker
description:
## Context

Syntax highlighting via Shiki currently runs on the main thread inside a useMemo. For large files, codeToTokens blocks rendering â€” the entire file is joined into one string and tokenized synchronously. Moving Shiki to a Web Worker lets the first paint show unstyled text immediately, with tokens painting in progressively.

## Requirements

- [ ] Shiki highlighter instance runs entirely in a dedicated Web Worker
- [ ] First paint shows unstyled monospace text immediately (no blank/loading state)
- [ ] Worker returns token arrays per line; client patches them into the already-rendered rows
- [ ] Highlighter instance is reused across file switches (warm grammar cache in worker)
- [ ] Language grammars are loaded on demand, not all at once
- [ ] Progressive enhancement: if the worker fails or is slow, plain text remains usable
- [ ] Works correctly with row-level virtualization (tokens only needed for visible rows, but pre-computed for scroll performance)

## Design decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Worker scope | Dedicated Worker (not SharedWorker) | Simpler lifecycle; one worker per tab is fine for local-first |
| Communication | postMessage with transferable ArrayBuffers where possible | Avoid structured clone overhead for large token arrays |
| Caching | Worker-side Map keyed by {filePath, lineKey} | Avoids re-highlighting already-processed lines on scroll |
| Grammar loading | Lazy per-language | Keeps initial worker boot fast; most PRs touch 1-3 languages |
| Fallback | Plain text render, no spinner | Unstyled code is readable; a loading spinner is worse than plain text |

## Anti-patterns

- Do NOT block first paint on highlighting â€” the whole point is progressive enhancement
- Do NOT send the entire file to the worker on every render â€” cache and send only new/changed content
- Do NOT load all Shiki grammars at worker init â€” lazy load per language
- Do NOT use synchronous worker communication (Atomics/SharedArrayBuffer) â€” postMessage is sufficient

## Success criteria

1. First paint of a 5,000-line diff shows text in < 50ms (unstyled)
2. Syntax tokens appear within 500ms of first paint for typical files
3. Main thread frame budget is never exceeded by highlighting work
4. Switching between files reuses the worker and grammar cache (no re-init)
5. Plain text fallback works if worker fails to load

title: Diff viewer row-level virtualization
description:
## Context

The diff viewer currently renders every row of every hunk into the DOM at once. A 10,000-line diff creates 20,000+ DOM nodes (both sides). This is the single biggest performance bottleneck and the most impactful fix. The VISION.md promises "DOM size is constant regardless of diff size."

## Requirements

- [x] Row-level virtualization using @tanstack/virtual (or equivalent) â€” only visible rows exist in the DOM
- [x] Side-by-side panels share a single virtual scroller; left/right row indices stay aligned
- [x] Hunk headers are rows in the virtual list, not separate elements outside it
- [x] Scrolling performance stays smooth (60fps) on a 50,000-line diff
- [x] File size tiers determine rendering strategy: small (< 500 lines) renders normally, medium (500â€“5,000) relies on virtualization, large (5,000â€“50,000) defers syntax highlighting (plain text first), huge (50,000+) collapses by default with summary and expand-on-demand
- [x] Keyboard navigation (j/k between hunks, n/p between changes) works with virtualized rows

## Design decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Virtualization library | @tanstack/virtual | Framework-agnostic, headless, proven at scale, small bundle |
| Scroll unit | Row | Hunk-level loses on single-hunk monster diffs; row-level guarantees constant DOM |
| Side sync | Single virtualizer, both sides read same measurements | Avoids dual-scroll sync bugs; rows are always the same height on both sides |
| Hunk headers | Virtual rows with a different renderer | Keeps everything in one list; no special positioning logic |
| File size tiers | Graduated strategy | Huge files collapsed by default avoids accidental DOM explosion |

## Anti-patterns

- Do NOT render all rows and hide with CSS â€” defeats the purpose
- Do NOT use intersection observer per-row â€” too many observers, worse than virtualization
- Do NOT break the side-by-side grid contract â€” left and right must always align row-for-row
- Do NOT add scroll event throttling â€” @tanstack/virtual handles this; extra throttling causes visual lag

## Success criteria

1. DOM node count stays under 200 rows regardless of diff size
2. Opening a 10,000-line diff takes < 100ms to first paint
3. Scrolling a 50,000-line diff maintains 60fps
4. All existing diff viewer tests pass or are updated to work with virtualized rendering
5. Keyboard navigation works identically to pre-virtualization behavior

title: Replace DiffViewer with virtualized row renderer
description:
## Goal

Replace the current DiffViewer that renders all rows into a CSS grid with a @tanstack/virtual-based implementation that only mounts visible rows. This is the single highest-impact performance change â€” DOM size becomes constant regardless of diff size.

## Behaviors

1. Virtualizer renders only visible rows â€” given a diff with 1,000 rows, fewer than 100 DOM row elements exist at any time
2. Side-by-side alignment preserved â€” left row N always aligns vertically with right row N in the virtualized list
3. Hunk headers render as virtual rows â€” hunk header strings appear at correct positions within the scrollable list using a distinct row renderer
4. Syntax highlighting tokens apply to visible rows â€” token spans render correctly on mounted rows via the existing tokenMap
5. Scroll position stability â€” scrolling up and down produces no layout jumps or blank flashes on the fixture diffs

## Success criteria

- [ ] DOM row count stays under 100 regardless of diff size
- [ ] Side-by-side alignment is pixel-correct (left and right rows share the same vertical offset)
- [ ] Hunk headers render in correct positions within the virtual list
- [ ] All existing DiffViewer tests pass or are updated for the virtualized structure
- [ ] TokenSpans still render on visible rows with correct highlighting

title: Add file size tiers for graduated rendering strategy
Add keyboard navigation for virtualized diff viewer
App shell: design tokens, layout, and core UI components
description:
## Problem

The web package is a placeholder displaying a health check response. No visual foundation exists for the code review tool â€” no design tokens, no layout system, no components. Nothing to see or interact with.

## Requirements

1. Full `--gr-` token system in `packages/ui`: primitives, semantic mappings, diff tokens, spacing scale (4px base), typography (ui + mono stacks)
2. Light and dark themes with `[data-theme]` attribute switching and `prefers-color-scheme` detection
3. Three-panel resizable layout: file tree | diff viewer | detail panel â€” using react-resizable-panels v4 (`Group`/`Panel`/`Separator` API) with localStorage persistence via `useDefaultLayout`
4. File tree using React Aria `Tree`/`TreeItem`/`TreeItemContent` from `react-aria-components` with keyboard navigation and built-in `Virtualizer`
5. Side-by-side diff viewer with Shiki syntax highlighting, line numbers, and added/removed/modified line coloring
6. Tabbed detail panel (Comments | File Info) using Radix Tabs â€” Comments shows mock thread UI, File Info shows mock git metadata
7. Progress-only status bar at the bottom showing file count and review progress (e.g. "4/12 files reviewed")
8. Realistic JSON fixture files with TypeScript diffs, multiple files, adds/deletes/renames
9. All mock data â€” no server wiring, no API calls. `pnpm dev` opens browser and shows the full review UI
10. CSS Modules with `--gr-` custom properties throughout. No runtime CSS-in-JS

## Anti-patterns

- No Mantine or other opinionated component library â€” Radix primitives only for what they provide (Tabs, Dialog, etc.)
- No react-virtuoso for the file tree â€” React Aria's built-in Virtualizer is the only supported path
- No runtime CSS-in-JS (styled-components, emotion, etc.) â€” CSS Modules only
- No unified diff mode in this epic â€” side-by-side only, toggle deferred
- No server endpoints or API wiring â€” everything runs from mock fixtures
- No file verdict tracking (approved/needs changes) â€” status bar shows progress count only

## Success criteria

1. `pnpm dev` opens the browser showing a three-panel review UI with realistic mock data
2. File tree renders a mock file hierarchy with expand/collapse, keyboard navigation (arrows, Home/End), and typeahead
3. Clicking a file in the tree shows its side-by-side diff with Shiki syntax highlighting in the center panel
4. Detail panel has Comments and File Info tabs â€” Comments shows mock threads, File Info shows mock metadata
5. All three panels are resizable via drag handles and keyboard; sizes persist across page reloads
6. Light/dark theme toggle works; respects OS preference on first load
7. Status bar shows "N/M files reviewed" and updates when navigating files
8. Design tokens are defined in `packages/ui` and consumed by `packages/web` via CSS Modules
9. All existing checks pass: format, lint, typecheck, test, build

## Design

### Token architecture (packages/ui)

Three-layer --gr- namespace as specified in STACK.md:
- tokens/primitives.css â€” raw color palette, raw sizes
- tokens/semantic.css â€” role-based tokens (bg-default, text-primary, border-default, bg-interactive, etc.)
- tokens/diff.css â€” diff-specific tokens (added-bg, removed-bg, added-char-bg, gutter colors)
- tokens/spacing.css â€” 4px base scale (--gr-space-1 through --gr-space-16)
- tokens/typography.css â€” font stacks (ui + mono), code-specific sizes
- themes/light.css â€” :root defaults
- themes/dark.css â€” [data-theme="dark"] overrides + prefers-color-scheme media query

### Layout (packages/web)

Three-panel horizontal layout:
- Header: Greppa logo + theme toggle
- Body: FileTree (React Aria Tree) | DiffViewer (side-by-side, Shiki) | DetailPanel (Radix Tabs: Comments | File Info)
- Footer: StatusBar with review progress

react-resizable-panels v4: Group with orientation="horizontal", three Panels with Separators. Sidebar collapsible, detail panel collapsible. useDefaultLayout for localStorage persistence.

### Key libraries

- react-resizable-panels v4 â€” layout panels
- react-aria-components â€” Tree + Virtualizer for file tree
- @radix-ui/react-tabs â€” detail panel tabs
- shiki â€” syntax highlighting singleton

### Mock data

JSON fixtures in packages/web/src/fixtures/:
- files.json â€” file tree structure (directories, files with change types)
- diffs/*.json â€” per-file diff data with hunks, line numbers, content
- comments.json â€” mock comment threads with authors, timestamps, replies
- fileInfo.json â€” mock git metadata (change frequency, authors, related PRs)

### Package boundaries

- packages/ui â€” token CSS files, theme CSS, shared primitive components (if any emerge)
- packages/web â€” app shell, FileTree, DiffViewer, DetailPanel, StatusBar, Header, mock fixtures
design:
### Token architecture (packages/ui) â€” theme-driven

Colors derive from curated syntax color themes. The theme IS the mode (light/dark). No separate dark mode toggle.

**Two-layer architecture:**
- Theme CSS file sets all --gr-color-* semantic tokens via [data-theme='theme-name']
- Components consume --gr-color-* only â€” never raw colors

**Theme contract (18 colors + 2 metadata):**
Every theme must provide:
- Surfaces (6): base, mantle, crust, surface0, surface1, surface2
- Text & borders (6): text, subtext1, subtext0, overlay2, overlay1, overlay0
- Accent (2): accent, accentText
- Status (4): green, red, yellow, blue
- Metadata (2): name, type ('light' | 'dark')

Diff tokens derived via alpha transparency from status colors (green/red/yellow at 15%/30%/20%).

**Theme switching:**
- data-theme attribute on <html> â€” e.g., data-theme="catppuccin-mocha"
- OS preference picks initial default (Mocha if dark, Latte if light)
- User selection stored in localStorage
- Inline <script> in index.html prevents FOUC

**Theme-independent tokens (stay as-is):**
- spacing.css â€” 9-step non-linear scale with --gr-scaling multiplier
- typography.css â€” font stacks, weights, sizes, leading

**Shiki integration:**
- Shiki loads the matching VS Code theme file for syntax highlighting
- Same palette, different format â€” guaranteed visual harmony between syntax and UI chrome

**First themes:**
- Catppuccin Mocha (dark) â€” default dark
- Catppuccin Latte (light) â€” default light

**Escape hatch (deferred):**
Custom theme JSON format matching the 18-color contract. Validated at load time.

### File structure (packages/ui/src/)

- tokens/spacing.css â€” 9-step non-linear scale with --gr-scaling
- tokens/typography.css â€” font stacks, weights, sizes
- themes/catppuccin-mocha.css â€” [data-theme='catppuccin-mocha'] { ... }
- themes/catppuccin-latte.css â€” [data-theme='catppuccin-latte'] { ... }
- tokens.css â€” barrel import of spacing + typography + all themes

### Layout (packages/web) â€” unchanged

Three-panel horizontal layout:
- Header: Greppa logo + theme picker
- Body: FileTree | DiffViewer | DetailPanel
- Footer: StatusBar with review progress

react-resizable-panels v4 with localStorage persistence.

### Key libraries â€” unchanged

- react-resizable-panels v4 â€” layout panels
- react-aria-components â€” Tree + Virtualizer for file tree
- @radix-ui/react-tabs â€” detail panel tabs
- shiki â€” syntax highlighting singleton (themes shared with UI)

title: Create --gr- design token system with light and dark themes
description:
## Goal

Create the full --gr- design token system in packages/ui with light and dark themes.

## Behaviors

1. Primitive tokens define the raw color palette (neutral grays, blue/green/red/yellow accent scales) and raw size values â€” never referenced by components directly
2. Semantic tokens map primitives to roles: bg-default, bg-subtle, bg-inset, text-primary, text-muted, border-default, bg-interactive, bg-interactive-hover
3. Diff tokens follow VS Code's diffEditor structure: added-bg, added-char-bg, gutter-added-bg, added-border â€” same pattern for removed and modified
4. Spacing scale: --gr-space-1 (4px) through --gr-space-16 (64px)
5. Typography: --gr-font-ui (system-ui stack), --gr-font-mono (ui-monospace stack), --gr-text-code (0.8125rem), --gr-leading-code (1.5rem)
6. Light theme is the :root default
7. Dark theme activates via [data-theme="dark"] attribute on html
8. OS preference respected via @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) when no explicit data-theme is set
9. All token CSS files are imported and re-exported from packages/ui/src/index.ts (or a tokens entry point)
10. packages/web can consume tokens by importing the CSS from @greppa/ui

## File structure

- packages/ui/src/tokens/primitives.css
- packages/ui/src/tokens/semantic.css
- packages/ui/src/tokens/diff.css
- packages/ui/src/tokens/spacing.css
- packages/ui/src/tokens/typography.css
- packages/ui/src/themes/light.css
- packages/ui/src/themes/dark.css
- packages/ui/src/tokens.css (barrel import of all token + theme files)

## Success criteria

- All token files exist with the categories above
- Light and dark themes both render correctly
- Tokens are consumable from packages/web via @greppa/ui import
- pnpm typecheck, pnpm lint, pnpm format:check, pnpm build all pass

title: Restructure tokens into theme-driven system with Catppuccin Mocha and Latte
description:
## Goal

Replace the fixed color primitive/semantic/diff/theme CSS files with a theme-driven system. Each theme is a single CSS file that sets all --gr-color-* semantic tokens. Ship Catppuccin Mocha (dark) and Catppuccin Latte (light) as the first two themes.

## Behaviors

1. Remove primitives.css, semantic.css, diff.css, themes/light.css, themes/dark.css
2. Create themes/catppuccin-mocha.css â€” [data-theme='catppuccin-mocha'] selector setting all ~40 --gr-color-* tokens using Catppuccin Mocha palette values
3. Create themes/catppuccin-latte.css â€” same structure using Catppuccin Latte palette values
4. Diff tokens derived via alpha: diff-added-bg = green at 15% alpha, diff-added-char-bg = green at 30%, diff-gutter-added-bg = green at 20%, diff-added-border = green at 40%. Same pattern for red (removed) and yellow (modified)
5. Each theme sets color-scheme: dark|light for native browser element styling
6. spacing.css stays unchanged (theme-independent, 9-step non-linear with --gr-scaling)
7. typography.css stays unchanged (theme-independent, font stacks + weights + sizes)
8. Update tokens.css barrel to import spacing + typography + all theme files
9. Update index.ts if needed
10. Add inline script to packages/web/index.html that reads localStorage for theme preference, falls back to OS prefers-color-scheme (Mocha if dark, Latte if light), and sets data-theme on <html> before first paint
11. All semantic token names stay the same (--gr-color-bg-default, etc.) â€” only the source of values changes

## Theme contract

Each theme CSS file must set these semantic tokens:

Surfaces:
- --gr-color-bg-default (from base)
- --gr-color-bg-subtle (from mantle)
- --gr-color-bg-inset (from crust)
- --gr-color-bg-emphasis (from surface2)
- --gr-color-bg-surface (from surface0)
- --gr-color-bg-surface-hover (from surface1)
- --gr-color-bg-surface-active (from surface2)

Text:
- --gr-color-text-primary (from text)
- --gr-color-text-secondary (from subtext1)
- --gr-color-text-muted (from subtext0)
- --gr-color-text-on-emphasis (derive from theme type)

Borders:
- --gr-color-border-default (from overlay1)
- --gr-color-border-muted (from overlay0)
- --gr-color-border-emphasis (from overlay2)

Interactive:
- --gr-color-bg-interactive (from accent)
- --gr-color-bg-interactive-hover (from accent, lightened/darkened 10%)
- --gr-color-text-interactive (from accent)

Status:
- --gr-color-bg-success (from green at 15% alpha)
- --gr-color-text-success (from green)
- --gr-color-bg-danger (from red at 15% alpha)
- --gr-color-text-danger (from red)
- --gr-color-bg-warning (from yellow at 15% alpha)
- --gr-color-text-warning (from yellow)

Diff:
- --gr-color-diff-added-bg (green at 15% alpha)
- --gr-color-diff-added-char-bg (green at 30% alpha)
- --gr-color-diff-gutter-added-bg (green at 20% alpha)
- --gr-color-diff-added-border (green at 40% alpha)
- Same pattern for removed (red) and modified (yellow)

## File structure

Remove:
- packages/ui/src/tokens/primitives.css
- packages/ui/src/tokens/semantic.css
- packages/ui/src/tokens/diff.css
- packages/ui/src/themes/light.css
- packages/ui/src/themes/dark.css

Keep:
- packages/ui/src/tokens/spacing.css
- packages/ui/src/tokens/typography.css

Create:
- packages/ui/src/themes/catppuccin-mocha.css
- packages/ui/src/themes/catppuccin-latte.css

Update:
- packages/ui/src/tokens.css (barrel)
- packages/web/index.html (inline theme script)

## Catppuccin palette reference

Mocha (dark): base=#1e1e2e, mantle=#181825, crust=#11111b, surface0=#313244, surface1=#45475a, surface2=#585b70, overlay0=#6c7086, overlay1=#7f849c, overlay2=#9399b2, subtext0=#a6adc8, subtext1=#bac2de, text=#cdd6f4, blue=#89b4fa, green=#a6e3a1, red=#f38ba8, yellow=#f9e2af, lavender=#b4befe

Latte (light): base=#eff1f5, mantle=#e6e9ef, crust=#dce0e8, surface0=#ccd0da, surface1=#bcc0cc, surface2=#acb0be, overlay0=#9ca0b0, overlay1=#8c8fa1, overlay2=#7c7f93, subtext0=#6c6f85, subtext1=#5c5f77, text=#4c4f69, blue=#1e66f5, green=#40a02b, red=#d20f39, yellow=#df8e1d, lavender=#7287fd

## Success criteria

- All existing --gr-color-* semantic token names still work
- Catppuccin Mocha renders correctly as dark theme
- Catppuccin Latte renders correctly as light theme
- data-theme attribute switches between themes
- First load respects OS preference (no FOUC)
- spacing and typography tokens unchanged
- All checks pass: typecheck, lint, format:check, build, test

title: Three-panel resizable layout with header and status bar
description:
## Goal

7. PanelResizeHandle styled with --gr-color-* tokens: 1px border-default, hover highlight

9. App uses --gr-color-bg-default for body background, --gr-font-ui for base font
10. Theme picker sets data-theme on html and stores choice in localStorage

title: Create mock data fixtures for file tree, diffs, comments, and file info
description:
## Goal

Create realistic JSON fixture data that all three panels will consume. This is the foundation for the file tree, diff viewer, and detail panel.

## Behaviors

1. files.json â€” tree structure with directories and files, each file has a path, change type (added/modified/deleted/renamed), and status (reviewed/unreviewed)
2. diffs/*.json â€” per-file diff data with hunks containing line numbers, content, and change type per line. Use realistic TypeScript code snippets
3. comments.json â€” mock comment threads with author name, timestamp, body text, file path, line number, and replies
4. fileInfo.json â€” mock git metadata per file: last modified date, change frequency, authors, related PR numbers

6. At least 5 files in the tree across 2-3 directories, including one add, one delete, one rename, and two modifications

- packages/web/src/fixtures/files.json â€” file tree data

- packages/web/src/fixtures/fileInfo.json â€” git metadata

title: File tree component with React Aria Tree and file selection state
description:
## Goal

Build the FileTree component using React Aria Tree and wire it into App.tsx with file selection state. This makes the left panel functional and provides the selection state that DiffViewer and DetailPanel will consume in later tasks.

- packages/web/src/components/FileTree.test.tsx â€” tests
- packages/web/src/App.tsx â€” wire in selection state

- Clicking a file updates selection (visible highlight)

title: Side-by-side diff viewer with Shiki syntax highlighting
description:
## Goal

Build the DiffViewer component that renders side-by-side diffs with Shiki syntax highlighting when a file is selected in the tree. Wire it into the center panel of App.tsx.

## Behaviors

1. Install shiki in packages/web
2. Export diff fixtures from fixtures/index.ts as a map from file path to DiffFile
3. DiffViewer component receives a DiffFile and renders side-by-side: left (before) and right (after)
4. Each hunk renders with a hunk header showing the range info
5. Line numbers shown in gutters on both sides
6. Added lines highlighted with --gr-color-diff-added-bg, removed with --gr-color-diff-removed-bg, modified with --gr-color-diff-modified-bg
7. Character-level highlights use --gr-color-diff-added-char-bg and --gr-color-diff-removed-char-bg
8. Shiki highlights syntax using a Catppuccin-compatible theme â€” load theme once via singleton/lazy init
9. Empty state when no file is selected: centered "Select a file to view diff" message
10. CSS Modules with --gr- tokens for all styling
11. App.tsx looks up the selected file path in the diff map and passes the DiffFile to DiffViewer

## File structure

- packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer.tsx â€” component
- packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer.module.css â€” styles
- packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer.test.tsx â€” tests
- packages/web/src/fixtures/index.ts â€” update to export diffs map
- packages/web/src/App.tsx â€” wire DiffViewer into center panel

## Success criteria

- Clicking a file in the tree shows its side-by-side diff in the center panel
- Syntax highlighting renders via Shiki

title: Add longer fixture diffs for realistic testing
description:
## Goal

Add at least 2 new fixture diff files with ~200+ lines of code content each to stress-test the UI at realistic scale.

## Why

Current largest fixture (rateLimiter) is only 38 lines of code content. Real-world diffs are much longer. Need realistic data to test scrolling performance, layout stability, and identify virtualization needs.

title: Hello world backend endpoint
description:
## Goal

Add a `/api/hello` GET endpoint that returns `{ message: "Hello, world!" }`.

## Success criteria

- [ ] `GET /api/hello` returns 200 with `{ message: "Hello, world!" }`
- [ ] Response schema is type-safe via `HttpApiEndpoint` + `Schema`
- [ ] Endpoint is registered in the top-level `Api` class
- [ ] Integration test verifies status code and response body
- [ ] Follows existing HealthApi pattern (group file, handler, test)

## Constraints

- No new packages or dependencies
- Follow existing three-layer pattern: *Api.ts â†’ Api.ts â†’ Http.ts

title: Add /api/hello endpoint with test
description:
## What

Add a hello world GET endpoint at `/api/hello` following the existing HealthApi pattern.

## Steps

1. Create `packages/server/src/HelloApi.ts`:
   - `HttpApiGroup.make('hello')` with one endpoint
   - `HttpApiEndpoint.get('getHello', '/api/hello', { success: Schema.Struct({ message: Schema.String }) })`

2. Register in `packages/server/src/Api.ts`:
   - Import `HelloApi`
   - Add `.add(HelloApi)` to the `Api` class chain

3. Wire handler in `packages/server/src/Http.ts`:
   - Add `HelloHandlers` using `HttpApiBuilder.group(Api, 'hello', ...)`
   - Handler returns `{ message: "Hello, world!" }`
   - Provide `HelloHandlers` to `ApiRoutes`

4. Add test in `packages/server/src/Http.test.ts`:
   - `GET /api/hello` returns 200
   - Response body matches `{ message: "Hello, world!" }`

## Done when

- Tests pass (`vp test`)
- Type-checks (`vp check`)

title: Data pipeline and caching
description:
## Context

The app currently loads all diff data from bundled JSON fixtures. In production, diffs must be computed from git on demand and delivered to the client efficiently. This epic builds a four-layer data pipeline: eager metadata, predictive pre-fetch, IndexedDB cache, and background warm-up via WebSocket push.

## Requirements

- [ ] Layer 1 â€” Eager metadata: on session start, server sends full file list with path, change type, line count, and size tier in one payload
- [ ] Layer 2 â€” Predictive pre-fetch: while reviewing file N, client fetches N+1 and N+2 based on file ordering
- [ ] Layer 3 â€” IndexedDB cache: diffs cached by {filePath, oldRef, newRef}, persistent across sessions, invalidated when ref changes
- [ ] Layer 4 â€” Background warm-up: after initial load, server computes remaining diffs via worker threads and pushes results over WebSocket; client stashes in IndexedDB
- [ ] Client always checks IndexedDB first, then falls back to server request
- [ ] The DiffFile contract (types.ts) remains the client-side interface regardless of data source
- [ ] Server computes diffs by spawning git diff and parsing unified diff output into structured DiffFile data
- [ ] WebSocket connection for real-time push of background-computed diffs

## Design decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Cache storage | IndexedDB | Persistent, large capacity, async API, survives page refresh |
| Cache key | {filePath, oldRef, newRef} | Natural invalidation â€” new push = new ref = cache miss |
| Cache scope | Persistent across sessions | "Pick up where you left off" is a core UX promise |
| Diff computation | Server-side git diff â†’ parse to DiffFile | git is fast; parsing on server keeps browser main thread free |
| Background push | WebSocket | Already needed for live updates; natural channel for push |
| Pre-fetch depth | N+1 and N+2 | Covers next-file navigation and skip-one; deeper adds diminishing returns |

## Anti-patterns

- Do NOT fetch all diffs eagerly on session start â€” defeats the entire pipeline
- Do NOT skip IndexedDB and always re-fetch â€” wastes computation on already-reviewed files
- Do NOT block UI on background warm-up â€” it's opportunistic, not required
- Do NOT cache without invalidation â€” stale diffs from old refs are worse than no cache
- Do NOT parse diffs on the main thread for large files â€” use the server or a worker

## Success criteria

1. Opening a 500-file PR shows the file list in < 200ms (metadata only)
2. Selecting a file shows its diff in < 100ms when cached in IndexedDB
3. Selecting an uncached file shows its diff in < 500ms (server computation + transfer)
4. After reviewing 3-4 files, remaining files are pre-cached via background warm-up
5. Reopening the same PR in a new session loads from IndexedDB (no server recomputation)
6. Changing the target ref invalidates stale cache entries automatically
design:
## Outcome

**Completed:** 2026-04-10
**Tasks:** 6 (greppa-30u.1â€“30u.6; 30u.4 was split into 30u.5 CacheService extraction and 30u.6 SSE warm-up)
**Summary:** Four-layer data pipeline delivered end-to-end. Layer 1 threads `lineCount`/`sizeTier` through the `/api/files` payload via one `git diff --numstat` call. Layer 2 prefetches N+1/N+2 neighbors through `usePrefetchNeighbors`, skipping large files. Layer 3 persists committed diffs in IndexedDB via `diffCache` (idb-keyval), keyed by `{oldRef, newRef, path}` â€” natural ref-change invalidation. Layer 4 streams warm-up diffs over SSE (deviation from the original WebSocket choice: no WS infra existed, SSE is one-way, native `EventSource`, no new deps), backed by the new `CacheService` seam extracted alongside. Client `useWarmup` subscribes after the file list settles and writes each pushed diff into `diffCache` fire-and-forget.
**Verification:** All 55 test files / 519 tests passing. Success criteria audited with code evidence: file list <200ms (server P95 gate under warm-up load in Http.integration.test.ts), cached <100ms (IDB-first read in useDiffContent), uncached path via `computeDiff` + CacheService, warm-up streams a DiffResponse per non-large file, persistent idb-keyval store across sessions, ref-scoped keys.
**Deviations:** (1) SSE instead of WebSocket â€” documented, spirit preserved (one-way serverâ†’client push). (2) `worker_threads` deferred â€” task 4b explicitly chose server-side sequential `Stream.mapEffect` pending profiling; existing `git` child processes are already non-blocking. Follow-up candidate: add `Stream.mapEffect` concurrency or workers if profiling shows warm-up lagging criterion 4 at scale.
**Code review follow-ups (not blocking, logged for future work):** IndexedDB has no eviction policy (orphan rows accumulate across PRs); `useWarmup` receives `files` as a dep which can thrash the SSE connection on react-query refetches; `CacheService.invalidate` has no production caller; `computeDiff` does O(N) path lookup per request; SSE test asserts event count but not path set.
notes:
## Server cache state â€” audit followup (2026-04-09)

A code audit found four mutable module-level caches in packages/server/src/Http.ts that overlap with this epic's scope:

- fileListCache (ttl 30s, cap 50) â€” listFiles results
- diffContentCache + worktreeDiffContentCache â€” parsed diff payloads
- worktreeFileListCache (ttl 3s, cap 1) â€” worktree listFiles
- stateStore: Map<string, StoredState> â€” shared-link state, capped at STATE_MAX_ENTRIES=10_000 with no LRU/TTL eviction

These live as module-global singletons outside the Effect layer/app boundary. It works today but will fight this epic in three ways:

1. Layer 4 (background warm-up via workers pushing over WebSocket) needs the cache to be addressable from more than one subsystem. Module globals are addressable, but they also can't be scoped, overridden per request, or reset between tests in isolation â€” which matters more as the pipeline grows.
2. When IndexedDB becomes the client cache, the server cache becomes the "inner ring" of the same pipeline. Aligning both behind a service interface (get / set / invalidate / evict) makes invalidation consistent across layers.
3. stateStore's 10k cap with no eviction is a slow leak; a real LRU or TTL belongs in the same service.

Recommended before (or as part of) Layer 4:
- Introduce a CacheService (or per-concern services) provided via makeHttpLayer so handlers read from context instead of module scope.
- Give stateStore a real eviction strategy â€” likely LRU keyed by last access.
- Keep fileListCache/diffContentCache TTLs, but surface them as construction parameters on the service.

Not urgent on its own (the TTL+size caps mitigate leak risk today), but worth bundling with the data pipeline work since both touch the same code.

title: Task 1: Layer 1 metadata â€” lineCount and sizeTier end-to-end
description:
## Goal

Thread line-count and size-tier metadata through the existing `/api/files` pipeline end-to-end so every file entry returned on session start carries `{ path, changeType, oldPath?, lineCount, sizeTier }`. The file list UI renders the line count beside each file as a visible smoke test.

This is the Layer 1 vertical slice from epic greppa-30u. The transport layer (`useFileList` -> `/api/files` -> `FilesApi` -> `GitService.listFiles`) is already in place. This task enriches the payload shape, computes the new fields from `git diff --numstat`, and surfaces them in the UI.

## Execution mode

HITL. Two decisions will need your input as implementation progresses:
- Exact thresholds for `sizeTier` (default proposed: `small < 50`, `medium < 500`, `large >= 500` by `added + removed` lines)
- Visual treatment for the line-count display in the file list (subdued numeric label vs tier badge)

## Behaviors

Each behavior is exactly one RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle. Do not write tests or implementation for more than one at a time.

1. `FileEntry` schema in `@greppa/core` accepts and requires `lineCount: number` and `sizeTier: 'small' | 'medium' | 'large'`. Update the schema definition and its type; all existing usages must continue to compile.

2. A new `parseNumstat` helper in `GitService.ts` converts `git diff --numstat` output into a `Map<string, number>` keyed by file path, with value `added + removed`. Treat binary diffs (where numstat reports `-\t-\tpath`) as `0`.
