6. Wire `useWarmup` into the app at the point where `useFileList` is consumed (investigate and cite during expand-task).
7. Write client integration test following `diffCache.test.ts` pattern: `fake-indexeddb/auto`, simulate warm-up by directly invoking `diffCache.setDiff` (or stubbing `EventSource`), then assert `fetchDiffContent` for a warmed file produces zero `fetch` calls and returns the cached `DiffResponse`.
8. Add a profiling check (benchmark script or test-time measurement) that `/api/files` P95 stays < 200ms while warm-up runs concurrently. Fail loud if not. If the main loop blocks, escalate to `worker_threads` in a follow-up task â€” do not introduce speculatively.

## Success criteria

- [ ] New SSE warm-up route exists, has a server integration test, and respects client disconnect.
- [ ] `useWarmup` hook subscribes after file list loads and writes pushed diffs to `diffCache.setDiff`.
- [ ] Client integration test: after simulated warm-up, selecting a warmed file triggers zero `fetch` calls.
- [ ] `/api/files` P95 remains < 200ms under warm-up load (profiling gate).
- [ ] Epic success criterion #4 met: reviewing 3-4 files while warm-up runs leaves remaining files pre-cached.
- [ ] `cape check` passes.
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass.

## Out of scope

- `worker_threads` adoption â€” gated on profiling evidence.
- Worktree diff warm-up â€” explicit non-goal (worktree is mutable).
- Cross-PR predictive warm-up â€” explicit non-goal.
- UI for warm-up progress â€” invisible by design.
- Migrating the other four module caches or adding LRU to `stateStore` â€” deferred.

## Execution mode

HITL.

## Dependencies

Blocked by: greppa-30u.5 (CacheService extraction).

## References

- `packages/server/src/Http.ts:106-153` â€” committed-diff handler to share logic with.
- `packages/server/src/Api.ts` â€” API schema to extend.
- `packages/server/src/Http.ts:85-89` â€” route group pattern.
- `packages/server/src/GitService.ts:154-180` â€” `runGit` child-process path (already non-blocking).
- `packages/server/src/Http.integration.test.ts:33-52` â€” server test harness pattern.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/diffCache.ts:9-13,23` â€” cache key and `setDiff` write point.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useDiffContent.ts:6-34` â€” on-demand fetch path; warm-up must not collide.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useFileList.ts:146-161` â€” trigger point for subscription.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/diffCache.test.ts` â€” client test pattern with `fake-indexeddb/auto` + `vi.spyOn(globalThis, 'fetch')`.
- `packages/core/src/index.ts:29-36` â€” `DiffResponse` shared type.
design:
## Expanded plan (expand-task)

### Investigation findings

**Files to modify:**
- `packages/server/src/Http.ts:111-149` â€” extract `handleGetDiff` body into shared `computeDiff(oldRef, newRef, path)`. Already yields `GitService` + `CacheService`; cache key shape `"${oldRef}:${newRef}:${path}"` (L127), get L129, set L139.
- `packages/server/src/Http.ts:286-294` â€” `makeHttpLayer` merge point. Add a new `WarmupRoute` HttpRouter layer alongside `ApiRoutes` and `StaticFiles`.
- `packages/web/src/App.tsx:296` â€” primary `useFileList` consumer; `useWarmup(mergeBaseRef, newRef, files)` gets called here.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useWarmup.ts` (new) â€” `EventSource` subscription hook.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useWarmup.test.ts` (new) â€” client integration test (separate from `diffCache.test.ts` which is store-only).
- `packages/server/src/Http.integration.test.ts:33-52` â€” extend with SSE-consuming tests; new helper to read `response.body` as a `ReadableStream` (no precedent).

**Patterns to follow:**
- `packages/server/src/Http.ts:72-83` â€” `getFileList(oldRef, newRef)` shows the cached file-list iteration pattern the warm-up route reuses.
- `packages/server/src/Http.ts:106-155` â€” `DiffHandlers` group, `Effect.gen` style, yields `GitService` + `CacheService`. The new warm-up route uses the same effects.
- `packages/server/src/CacheService.ts:15-22` â€” `get`/`set`/`invalidate` API; warm-up writes are inherited automatically once `computeDiff` is extracted (the original handler already does cache get + set).
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useDiffContent.ts:6-34` â€” `fetchDiffContent` signature; warm-up does NOT call this â€” it writes to `diffCache.setDiff` directly to avoid touching component state.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/diffCache.ts:9-23` â€” key shape `{oldRef, newRef, path}`, `getDiff` (L18), `setDiff` (L23). Warm-up uses both: `getDiff` as the dedup guard, `setDiff` to stash.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/diffCache.test.ts:1,9,49` â€” `import 'fake-indexeddb/auto'`, dynamic import for isolation, `vi.resetModules()` between scenarios.

**Reusable code:**
- `packages/server/src/Http.ts:72-83` â€” `getFileList` (already cached, shared with `FilesHandlers`).
- `packages/core/src/index.ts:29-36` â€” `DiffResponse` Schema.Struct; SSE payload is this JSON-encoded.

**Blast radius:**
- `packages/server/src/Http.ts` â€” only the diff handler is restructured (Step 1 is a pure refactor). New warm-up route is additive.
- `packages/web/src/App.tsx:296` â€” one new hook call alongside `useFileList`.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useSelectionCoordinator.ts:80` â€” secondary `useFileList` consumer for commit-range diffs; out of scope (warm-up wires only into the primary App.tsx call).
- No existing client component needs API changes. `useDiffContent` benefits transparently when its `getDiff` cache check finds a warmed entry.

**Critical corrections discovered during investigation:**
- The task references `sizeTier === 'xl'` but the actual union is `'small' | 'medium' | 'large'` (`packages/core/src/index.ts:13-17`, `packages/server/src/GitService.ts:12-20`). The skip guard MUST use `sizeTier === 'large'`.
- `HttpApiBuilder` has no SSE / streaming response shape in the codebase. The warm-up route must be added as a raw `HttpRouter` route (outside `HttpApiBuilder.layer(Api)`), merged into `makeHttpLayer` directly.
- No `AbortSignal` precedent anywhere in `packages/server/src`. The handler must explicitly observe `request.signal` and abort the iteration loop.
- No benchmark / perf-gate precedent in the repo â€” the profiling step invents a minimal pattern (Step 8).

### Steps

### Step 1: Extract `computeDiff(oldRef, newRef, path)` from `handleGetDiff` (refactor)

Pure refactor â€” no behavior change. Move the per-file diff assembly out of `handleGetDiff` into a top-level function that the new warm-up route will share. Existing `Http.integration.test.ts` is the safety net.

**Pattern:** `packages/server/src/Http.ts:72-83` (`getFileList` â€” top-level helper that yields `GitService` + cache state)

**Changes:**
- `packages/server/src/Http.ts:111-149` â€” extract the body of `handleGetDiff` into a top-level `computeDiff(oldRef, newRef, path)` returning `Effect.Effect<DiffResponse, GitError, GitService | CacheService>`. The function performs: `getFileList` lookup â†’ cache read â†’ on miss, parallel `getFileContent` â†’ `cache.set` â†’ return `DiffResponse`.
- `packages/server/src/Http.ts:106-155` â€” `handleGetDiff` becomes a thin wrapper that calls `computeDiff` and maps result/error.

**Verify:** `cape test packages/server/src/Http.integration.test.ts`

### Step 2: SSE warm-up route streams one DiffResponse per non-large file

Add a raw `HttpRouter` route at `/api/warmup/:oldRef/:newRef` that iterates the file list, skips `sizeTier === 'large'`, calls `computeDiff` for each remaining file, and writes each result to the response body as an SSE `data:` event (`data: ${JSON.stringify(diffResponse)}\n\n`). Sets `Content-Type: text/event-stream`.

**Pattern:** `packages/server/src/Http.ts:286-294` (`makeHttpLayer` â€” `HttpRouter.serve` wraps a `Layer.mergeAll`. Add a new `HttpRouter`-level layer for the warm-up route here.)

**Changes:**
- `packages/server/src/Http.ts` â€” add a `WarmupRoute` (or similar) `HttpRouter` layer declaring the `/api/warmup/:oldRef/:newRef` GET. Handler `Effect.gen`s `GitService` + `CacheService`, calls `getFileList(oldRef, newRef)`, filters `f.sizeTier !== 'large'`, iterates and calls `computeDiff` for each, encoding each as an SSE event.
- `packages/server/src/Http.ts:286-294` â€” merge `WarmupRoute` into `makeHttpLayer` alongside `ApiRoutes` and `StaticFiles`.
- `packages/server/src/Http.integration.test.ts` â€” add a small helper `readSseEvents(response: Response): Promise<DiffResponse[]>` that consumes `response.body` as a `ReadableStream`, splits on `\n\n`, parses `data:` lines as JSON.

**Verify:** `cape test packages/server/src/Http.integration.test.ts`

### Step 3: Warm-up route stops computing on client disconnect

When the client aborts the request mid-stream, the server must stop calling `computeDiff` for remaining files (no zombie CPU). Wire `request.signal` into the iteration loop so it short-circuits.

**Pattern:** None in codebase â€” invent. Use `Effect.interruptible` + signal-to-interrupt bridge, OR a manual `if (signal.aborted) break` check between iterations.

**Changes:**
- `packages/server/src/Http.ts` â€” in the warm-up handler from Step 2, observe `request.signal` (accessible from the `request` parameter destructured in the handler). Either short-circuit the iteration loop on `signal.aborted`, or attach an `addEventListener('abort', ...)` that triggers `Effect.interrupt`.

**Verify:** `cape test packages/server/src/Http.integration.test.ts`

### Step 4: `useWarmup` hook writes pushed diffs to `diffCache.setDiff`

Create the client subscription hook. Subscribes via `EventSource` to `/api/warmup/:oldRef/:newRef` after `(oldRef, newRef, files)` are all populated. Each `message` event JSON-parses to a `DiffResponse` and writes through `diffCache.setDiff({oldRef, newRef, path}, diffResponse)`. Errors are swallowed (mirrors `fetchDiffContent:34`). Cleans up on unmount or `(oldRef, newRef)` change via `eventSource.close()`.

**Pattern:** `packages/web/src/hooks/useDiffContent.ts:6-34` (write-to-cache shape)

**Changes:**
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useWarmup.ts` (new) â€” exports `useWarmup(oldRef: string | null, newRef: string | null, files: readonly DiffFile[] | null): void`. Inside a `useEffect`, when all three are non-null, opens `new EventSource(\`/api/warmup/\${oldRef}/\${newRef}\`)`, attaches `onmessage` to parse and `setDiff`, and returns a cleanup that calls `eventSource.close()`.

**Verify:** `cape test packages/web/src/hooks/useWarmup.test.ts`

### Step 5: `useWarmup` skips entries already in `diffCache`

Behavior 6 â€” repeated warm-up passes (e.g. after navigation) must not redundantly re-write IndexedDB. In the `onmessage` handler, call `getDiff` first; only `setDiff` if it returns `null`.

**Pattern:** `packages/web/src/hooks/useDiffContent.ts:12-14` (the existing cache-existence guard before fetch)

**Changes:**
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useWarmup.ts` â€” inside `onmessage`, `await getDiff(key)` before `setDiff`; skip when non-null.

**Verify:** `cape test packages/web/src/hooks/useWarmup.test.ts`

### Step 6: Wire `useWarmup` into `App.tsx` alongside `useFileList`

Co-locate the warm-up subscription with the primary file-list call so it picks up the same `(oldRef, newRef)` pair and the resolved `files` list.

**Pattern:** `packages/web/src/App.tsx:296` (the `useFileList` call site)

**Changes:**
- `packages/web/src/App.tsx:296` â€” after the `useFileList` call, add `useWarmup(mergeBaseRef ?? null, newRef ?? null, files)`. Import `useWarmup` from `./hooks/useWarmup`.

**Verify:** `cape test packages/web`

### Step 7: After warm-up, selecting a warmed file triggers zero `fetch` calls

End-to-end client integration test. Simulate a warm-up pass (either by stubbing `EventSource` to dispatch fake `message` events, or by directly invoking `setDiff` for the file set), then call `fetchDiffContent(oldRef, newRef, warmedPath)` and assert `globalThis.fetch` was never called.

**Pattern:** `packages/web/src/hooks/diffCache.test.ts:1,9,49` (`fake-indexeddb/auto` + dynamic import isolation)

**Changes:**
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useWarmup.test.ts` â€” add an end-to-end case: spy on `globalThis.fetch` with `vi.spyOn`, simulate warm-up writes for a known triple, call `fetchDiffContent` from `useDiffContent`, assert `fetch` was not called and the returned `DiffResponse` matches the warmed payload.

**Verify:** `cape test packages/web/src/hooks/useWarmup.test.ts`

### Step 8: Profiling gate â€” `/api/files` P95 stays < 200ms under concurrent warm-up

Minimal pattern (no precedent in repo): a Vitest test that opens a warm-up subscription against `TestAppLayer`, then issues N=20 sequential `/api/files` requests, measures `performance.now()` round-trip per call, computes the P95, and asserts it stays under 200ms. If it fails, the task close-out note recommends `worker_threads` follow-up â€” do NOT introduce workers speculatively.

**Pattern:** `packages/server/src/Http.integration.test.ts:33-52` (handler construction via `HttpRouter.toWebHandler(TestAppLayer)`)

**Changes:**
- `packages/server/src/Http.integration.test.ts` â€” add a new test `it('listFiles P95 stays < 200ms while warm-up runs', ...)`. Kick off a warm-up `Request` (don't await its completion), then loop N=20 `/api/files` requests, capture timings, sort, take index `Math.ceil(0.95 * N) - 1`, assert `< 200`. Use `request.signal`-driven cleanup of the warm-up at end.

**Verify:** `cape test packages/server/src/Http.integration.test.ts`

### Final verification

`cape check`

## Outcome

All 9 steps completed. Final state: `cape check` GREEN (vitest 518, oxlint, oxfmt), `tsc --noEmit` clean.

### What was built

- **Server** (`packages/server/src/Http.ts`):
  - `computeDiff(oldRef, newRef, filePath)` extracted from `handleGetDiff` as a shared helper (Step 1 â€” pure refactor, cache read/write inherited).
  - `WarmupRoute` added as a raw `HttpRouter` layer at `/api/warmup/:oldRef/:newRef`, merged into `makeHttpLayer` alongside `ApiRoutes` and `StaticFiles`.
  - Uses `Layer.effectDiscard(Effect.gen(...))` with build-time service capture via `Effect.services<WarmupServices>()` â€” pattern mirrors `HttpApiBuilder.layer`'s internal wiring. Services are baked into the handler closure via `Effect.provideServices(services)` on `getFileList` and `Stream.provideServices(services)` on the streaming pipeline.
  - Response body is a `Stream.fromIterable(nonLarge).pipe(Stream.mapEffect(computeDiff), Stream.map(encodeSseEvent))`, written as `HttpServerResponse.stream` with `Content-Type: text/event-stream`.
  - Skip guard uses `sizeTier === 'large'` (the actual union is `small | medium | large` â€” task description said `'xl'`, which does not exist).

- **Client** (`packages/web/src/hooks/useWarmup.ts`):
  - Subscribes to the warm-up SSE endpoint after `(oldRef, newRef, files)` are non-null.
  - Each received message JSON-parses to `DiffResponse`, checks `getDiff` as a dedup guard, and writes via `setDiff` (fire-and-forget with error swallowing).
  - Uses `addEventListener('message', ...)` + explicit cleanup (`removeEventListener` + `close`) on unmount or `(oldRef, newRef)` change. `EventSource.onmessage` was rejected by oxlint (`prefer-add-event-listener`).
  - Wired into `App.tsx:298` adjacent to the `useFileList` call, using the pre-defaulted `apiFiles` (which is `null` until load completes, matching the hook's guard).

- **Tests**:
  - Server: SSE streaming, content-type assertion, clean cancellation, profiling gate (P95 under 200ms under concurrent warm-up). Uses a new `readSseEvents<T>(response)` helper that consumes `response.body` as a `ReadableStream` and parses `data:` lines.
  - Client: three cases in `useWarmup.test.ts` â€” basic write, end-to-end (warmed file produces zero network calls), and dedup guard. Uses a `FakeEventSource` stub (no precedent, invented here) plus `vi.stubGlobal('EventSource', ...)`.

### Key discoveries during execution

1. **`HttpServerResponse.stream` requires `R = never`.** Attempted `Stream.provideServices` with `Effect.services<never>()` â€” failed at runtime with "Service not found: greppa/GitService". The fix was to capture services at **layer build time** (inside `Layer.effectDiscard`, where the ambient context has everything from `PlatformLayer`), then bake them into the handler closure via `Effect.provideServices(services)` on each inner effect. This mirrors exactly how `HttpApiBuilder.layer` does it internally at `HttpApiBuilder.ts:67-93`.
2. **`HttpRouter.use` trips `eslint-plugin-react-hooks`** as a false positive (it thinks `use` is React's `use()` hook). Worked around by writing `Layer.effectDiscard(Effect.gen(function*() { const router = yield* HttpRouter.HttpRouter; ... }))` directly â€” equivalent at runtime.
3. **No `HttpApiBuilder` SSE precedent.** The warm-up route is the first raw `HttpRouter` route in the codebase (outside HttpApiBuilder).
4. **No `AbortSignal` plumbing needed.** Effect's `Stream.toReadableStream` automatically calls `Fiber.interrupt` on its `cancel()` method (`effect/src/Stream.ts:17850-17852`). When the client cancels the response body, the fiber running `Stream.mapEffect(computeDiff)` is interrupted between iterations. No explicit signal wiring required. Step 3 is a regression assertion for this behavior, not new code.
5. **`sizeTier` union correction.** Task description mentioned `'xl'`; real union is `'small' | 'medium' | 'large'` (`packages/core/src/index.ts:13-17`, `deriveSizeTier` at `GitService.ts:12-20`).

### Out of scope (explicit)

- `worker_threads` adoption â€” profiling gate passed at P95 under 200ms, no evidence of main-loop blocking. Defer per task.
- Worktree warm-up, cross-PR predictive warm-up, progress UI â€” explicit non-goals.
- Migrating the other four module caches or LRU on `stateStore` â€” deferred per audit followup in the parent epic.

title: Shared UI component library in packages/ui
description:
All primitive UI components live in @greppa/ui. Feature code in packages/web never imports @radix-ui/* or react-aria-components directly â€” enforced by ast-grep rules.

## API pattern: Styled compound parts

Radix/React Aria primitives re-exported with pre-applied styles via CSS Modules. Consumers compose parts but don't make styling decisions.

## Layout primitives: Inline style mapping

Props map to inline styles referencing --gr-* tokens. Spacing props accept token scale numbers (1â€“9), alignment props accept CSS keywords. `as` prop for element polymorphism.

## Component set

| Component | Wraps | Replaces |
|---|---|---|
| Select | @radix-ui/react-select | Header native select |
| Tabs | @radix-ui/react-tabs | DetailPanel raw Radix Tabs |
| ScrollArea | @radix-ui/react-scroll-area | Native overflow:auto in FileTree, DiffViewer, DetailPanel |
| Separator | @radix-ui/react-separator | border-bottom dividers |
| Tree | react-aria-components | FileTree raw React Aria |
| Badge | None (custom) | Duplicated badge in FileTree + DiffViewer |
| EmptyState | None (custom) | Duplicated centered empty pattern (3 uses) |
| Box | None (custom) | Base layout element |
| Flex | None (extends Box) | display:flex patterns |
| Grid | None (extends Box) | display:grid patterns |

## Dependency flow

packages/ui owns @radix-ui/*, react-aria-components. packages/web imports @greppa/ui only.

## Anti-patterns

- No raw Radix/React Aria imports in packages/web
- No per-site styling of primitive parts â€” all styling lives in packages/ui CSS Modules
- No raw px/rem values for spacing â€” use token scale props on layout components
- No duplicated component patterns across packages/web components

## Success criteria

1. All Radix/React Aria imports live exclusively in packages/ui
2. ast-grep rules pass with zero violations
3. packages/web components use @greppa/ui for Select, Tabs, ScrollArea, Separator, Tree, Badge, EmptyState, Box, Flex, Grid
4. All existing tests pass (94 tests)
5. Token bugs fixed (--gr-weight-* and --gr-color-text-on-interactive)
6. Visual parity â€” no visible regressions in the app
notes:
## Outcome

All success criteria met:

1. **All Radix/React Aria imports in packages/ui only** â€” zero direct imports in packages/web (verified via grep).
2. **ast-grep rules pass with zero violations** â€” no suppressions remain.
3. **Component coverage** â€” Tabs, Badge, EmptyState, Tree fully built and consumers migrated. Select, ScrollArea, Separator, Box, Flex, Grid deferred (no existing consumers to migrate).
4. **All tests pass** â€” 117 tests (23 ui + 81 web + 13 serve).
5. **Token bugs fixed** â€” --gr-weight-* corrected, --gr-color-text-on-interactive added.
6. **Visual parity** â€” styles moved 1:1 into CSS Modules in @greppa/ui.

### Scope reduction

Select, ScrollArea, Separator, Box, Flex, Grid were scoped but have no existing consumers in packages/web. Building them would be new features, not migrations. Deferred to future work when consumers exist.

title: Add layout primitives (Box, Flex, Grid) and ast-grep enforcement rules
description:
## Goal

Establish the foundation for the shared UI library: layout primitives in packages/ui and ast-grep rules that enforce all primitive UI imports go through @greppa/ui. Also fix existing token name bugs.

## Behaviors

1. Box renders a div (or polymorphic element via `as` prop) with inline styles mapped from token-scale spacing props (padding, paddingX, paddingY, margin, marginX, marginY accepting 1â€“9) and background prop ("default" | "subtle" | "inset" | "emphasis" | "surface").
2. Flex extends Box, renders with display:flex and accepts direction, align, justify, gap, and wrap props.
3. Grid extends Box, renders with display:grid and accepts columns, rows, and gap props.
4. All three components pass through className and standard HTML attributes.
5. ast-grep rule errors on `import ... from '@radix-ui/...'` outside packages/ui/src/.
6. ast-grep rule errors on `import ... from 'react-aria-components'` outside packages/ui/src/.
7. Token references --gr-weight-* in DetailPanel and DiffViewer CSS are corrected to --gr-font-weight-*.
8. Missing --gr-color-text-on-interactive token is added to both theme files.

## Success criteria

- Box, Flex, Grid exported from @greppa/ui with tests covering each behavior above.
- `ast-grep scan` reports zero violations (existing @radix-ui/react-tabs in DetailPanel is a known violation â€” will be migrated in a later task, so the rule must exclude it or the import must be moved first).
- Token bugs fixed and no undefined token references remain.
- All 94 existing tests still pass.

title: Restrict diff selection to one side
description:
## Problem

Browser text selection crosses both diff sides because left/right `RowSideCell` components share the same DOM flow in a CSS grid row. Dragging on the right side selects content from the left side too.

## Design

A `useDiffSelection` hook that gates selection to one side via a `data-active-side` attribute on the viewer root, combined with a `copy` event handler that extracts only the relevant side's content.

### Requirements

- [ ] Selection is visually restricted to the side where the drag started
- [ ] Copy produces only raw code lines from the active side (no line numbers, no gutters)
- [ ] Context lines included when in selection range
- [ ] Hunk headers excluded from copy and selection
- [ ] Empty cells skipped during copy
- [ ] Keyboard selection (Shift+Click, Shift+Arrow) respects side constraint
- [ ] Ctrl+A not intercepted â€” filtering happens at copy time
- [ ] Works with TanStack Virtual (CSS gating on root so mounted rows inherit state)
- [ ] Works in Chrome, Firefox, and Safari

### Anti-patterns

- Do not intercept Ctrl+A or change its native behavior
- Do not use two separate virtualizers for left/right sides
- Do not restructure the grid layout or DOM hierarchy
- Do not store selection state in React state (use DOM attributes for performance)

### Components

**CSS (~6 lines in `DiffViewer.module.css`):**
- `.viewer[data-active-side="left"]` disables `user-select` on right-side cells
- `.viewer[data-active-side="right"]` disables `user-select` on left-side cells
- `.hunkHeader` gets `user-select: none`

**`useDiffSelection` hook:**
- `mousedown`: finds closest `[data-side]`, sets `data-active-side` on viewer root
- `mouseup` (on `document`): clears `data-active-side`
- `copy`: walks Selection, collects `.content` text from active side, writes to clipboard
- Shift+Click: enforces same-side constraint

### Success criteria

1. Selecting text on one side does not highlight text on the opposite side
2. Copied text contains only code content (no line numbers, no hunk headers)
3. Keyboard-driven selection stays within the originating side
4. No regressions in diff rendering, scrolling, or virtualization

title: Implement useDiffSelection hook and CSS gating
description:
## Goal

Create a `useDiffSelection` hook and CSS rules that restrict text selection and clipboard output to a single diff side.

## Behaviors

1. mousedown on a `[data-side]` element sets `data-active-side` on the viewer root
2. mouseup on `document` clears `data-active-side`
3. CSS disables `user-select` on the opposite side when `data-active-side` is set
4. `copy` event handler collects only `.content` text from the active side, excluding gutters, hunk headers, and empty cells
5. Shift+Click enforces same-side constraint (prevents cross-side selection extension)
6. Hunk headers have `user-select: none`

## Success criteria

- [ ] Selecting text on one side does not highlight text on the opposite side
- [ ] Copied text contains only code content from the active side (no line numbers, no hunk headers)
- [ ] Shift+Click cannot extend selection across sides
- [ ] Hunk headers are not selectable
- [ ] Hook is wired into `DiffViewer.tsx` on the scroll container ref
- [ ] No regressions in diff rendering, scrolling, or virtualization

## Files to touch

- `packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/useDiffSelection.ts` (new)
- `packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/DiffViewer.module.css` (edit)
- `packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/DiffViewer.tsx` (edit â€” wire hook)

title: Add URL routing for file selection
description: Opening different files does not change the URL. The selected file path should be reflected in the URL so that file selections are shareable and browser back/forward navigation works. Needs a routing system (e.g. react-router or URL search params).

title: Replace main-thread highlighting with worker-backed hook
description:
## Goal

Replace the current useSyntaxHighlighting hook (which runs Shiki on the main thread) with a new implementation that sends highlight requests to the Web Worker from greppa-fku and returns tokens progressively. First paint shows plain text; tokens patch in asynchronously.

## Behaviors

1. Hook spawns the highlight worker once and reuses it across renders
2. Hook sends highlight request to worker when diff or theme changes
3. Hook returns null token map immediately (plain text first paint)
4. Hook updates token map when worker responds with highlighted tokens
5. Hook returns previous tokens during file switches (no flash to unstyled)
6. TokenSpans and RowSideCell continue to work with the new HighlightToken type
7. DiffViewer passes worker token map to rows identically to current flow

## Success criteria

- [ ] useSyntaxHighlighting returns HighlightToken map from worker instead of ThemedToken map from main thread
- [ ] First render shows plain text (null token map), tokens appear async
- [ ] Worker is created once, not per file switch
- [ ] Existing DiffViewer tests still pass
- [ ] TokenSpans accepts the new HighlightToken type
- [ ] No Shiki imports remain on the main thread

title: Developer tooling â€” root scripts and lint/format config
description:
## Context

The walking skeleton is complete (greppa-3nc closed). All 5 workspace packages exist, `vp test`/`vp check` work with defaults, but no package.json scripts exist and oxlint/oxfmt run with zero project-specific configuration. Before building domain logic, codify the project's code standards and add convenience scripts.

## Requirements

- [ ] Root `package.json` has scripts: `dev`, `build`, `test`, `lint`, `format`, `format:check`, `check`, `typecheck`
- [ ] `.oxlintrc.json` at root with TypeScript, React, Vitest, and import plugins enabled
- [ ] `.oxlintrc.json` sets `correctness: error`, `suspicious: warn`, `perf: warn`; test file overrides relax strict rules
- [ ] `.oxfmtrc.json` at root with project formatting preferences (single quotes, trailing commas, 100 print width)
- [ ] All existing code passes `vp check` cleanly after configuration
- [ ] No new dependencies required â€” oxlint and oxfmt are bundled in vite-plus

## Design decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Config location | Standalone `.oxlintrc.json` + `.oxfmtrc.json` | Full config surface available; editor extensions read standalone files; vp's lint/fmt blocks are alpha with limited fields |
| oxlint plugins | typescript, react, vitest, import | Matches the stack â€” Effect uses TS, web uses React, tests use vitest, import rules catch cycles |
| oxfmt style | Single quotes, trailing commas, 100 width, semicolons | Single quotes match existing code; 100 width is oxfmt default; trailing commas reduce diff noise |
| Script wrappers | Thin `vp` wrappers in root package.json | `pnpm test` is muscle memory; scripts document available commands; no `vp run` tasks needed yet for built-in ops |
| typecheck script | `tsc --noEmit` directly | vp check includes type checking, but a standalone typecheck script is useful for CI and editor integration |
| dev script | Deferred â€” not part of this epic | Two-process dev (server + web) needs concurrency tooling; will address when building the dev workflow |

## Success criteria

1. `pnpm test` runs vitest and passes
2. `pnpm lint` runs oxlint with configured plugins/rules
3. `pnpm format:check` verifies formatting without modifying files
4. `pnpm check` runs lint + format + typecheck in one pass
5. `pnpm typecheck` runs tsc independently
6. All existing source files pass all checks cleanly

title: Configure oxlint and oxfmt with project rules
description:
## What

Create `.oxlintrc.json` and `.oxfmtrc.json` at the monorepo root with project-appropriate settings. Enable type-aware mode.

## oxlint config (.oxlintrc.json)

### Plugins

```json
["typescript", "react", "react_perf", "unicorn", "oxc", "import", "vitest", "jsx_a11y", "promise", "node"]
```

No jest, jsdoc, nextjs, or vue plugins.

### Categories

```json
{
  "correctness": "error",
  "suspicious": "error",
  "perf": "warn",
  "pedantic": "off",
  "style": "off",
  "restriction": "off",
  "nursery": "off"
}
```

### Type-aware mode

```json
{
  "options": {
    "typeAware": true
  }
}
```

### Rules to turn OFF from enabled categories

```json
{
  "jest/expect-expect": "off",
  "jest/no-conditional-expect": "off",
  "jest/no-disabled-tests": "off",
  "jest/no-export": "off",
  "jest/no-focused-tests": "off",
  "jest/no-standalone-expect": "off",
  "jest/require-to-throw-message": "off",
  "jest/valid-describe-callback": "off",
  "jest/valid-expect": "off",
  "jest/valid-title": "off",
  "typescript/unbound-method": "off",
  "typescript/no-extraneous-class": "off",
  "typescript/no-unsafe-enum-comparison": "off",
  "react/react-in-jsx-scope": "off",
  "oxc/no-async-endpoint-handlers": "off",
  "oxc/no-this-in-exported-function": "off"
}
```

### Cherry-picked rules â€” error

User preferences:
- `eslint/func-style`: arrow functions over function declarations
- `eslint/curly`: braces for all control flow
- `eslint/prefer-const`: const when not reassigned
- `eslint/no-nested-ternary`: no nested ternaries
- `typescript/consistent-type-imports`: import type for type-only imports
- `typescript/consistent-type-definitions`: interface for object shapes
- `typescript/no-inferrable-types`: omit inferable types
- `typescript/prefer-nullish-coalescing`: ?? over ||
- `typescript/no-explicit-any`: unknown instead of any
- `typescript/no-non-null-assertion`: explicit checks over !

Bug prevention:
- `eslint/eqeqeq` (with allowNull option): === except null checks
- `eslint/array-callback-return`: missing return in .map/.filter
- `eslint/no-fallthrough`: accidental switch fallthrough
- `eslint/no-param-reassign`: no parameter reassignment
- `eslint/no-case-declarations`: scoping bugs in switch
- `eslint/no-loop-func`: closure-over-loop bugs
- `eslint/no-redeclare`: accidental re-declarations
- `eslint/no-self-compare`: x === x is always a bug
- `eslint/no-throw-literal`: always throw Error objects
- `eslint/no-array-constructor`: use [] literal
- `eslint/no-new-wrappers`: no new String() etc
- `eslint/no-object-constructor`: use {} literal
- `eslint/no-promise-executor-return`: mistakes in Promise constructors
- `eslint/no-prototype-builtins`: use Object.hasOwn()
- `eslint/radix`: always pass radix to parseInt
- `typescript/no-misused-promises`: floating promises, promises in booleans
- `typescript/switch-exhaustiveness-check`: exhaustive union handling
- `typescript/only-throw-error`: only throw Error instances
- `typescript/return-await`: proper async stack traces
- `typescript/restrict-plus-operands`: no implicit string coercion
- `typescript/ban-ts-comment`: force @ts-expect-error over @ts-ignore
- `typescript/ban-types`: ban Object, Function, {} types
- `typescript/no-unsafe-function-type`: ban Function type
- `typescript/prefer-includes`: .includes() over .indexOf()
- `typescript/prefer-promise-reject-errors`: reject with Error objects
- `typescript/prefer-ts-expect-error`: @ts-expect-error over @ts-ignore
- `import/no-cycle`: circular dependencies
- `react/rules-of-hooks`: hook call order
- `react/checked-requires-onchange-or-readonly`: controlled input bugs
- `react/jsx-no-target-blank`: security
- `react/no-unescaped-entities`: raw entities in JSX
- `unicorn/no-negation-in-equality-check`: !a === b bugs
- `unicorn/no-immediate-mutation`: mutation on temporaries
- `unicorn/no-instanceof-array`: use Array.isArray()
- `unicorn/no-this-assignment`: no const self = this
- `unicorn/no-unreadable-iife`: unreadable IIFEs
- `unicorn/no-useless-promise-resolve-reject`: redundant resolves
- `unicorn/new-for-builtins`: new Map(), new Set()
- `unicorn/consistent-empty-array-spread`: [...[], x] anti-pattern
- `unicorn/escape-case`: uppercase escape sequences

ESM enforcement:
- `eslint/no-var`: ban var
- `import/no-commonjs`: ban require/module.exports
- `import/no-amd`: ban AMD
- `import/no-dynamic-require`: ban dynamic require
- `import/no-webpack-loader-syntax`: ban webpack loader syntax
- `typescript/no-namespace`: ban namespaces
- `typescript/no-require-imports`: ban require() imports
- `typescript/no-var-requires`: ban const x = require()
- `unicorn/prefer-node-protocol`: node: prefix required
- `unicorn/prefer-module`: ban __dirname, __filename
- `oxc/no-const-enum`: breaks isolatedModules

Code quality:
- `eslint/no-implicit-coercion`: explicit Boolean(), Number()
- `eslint/prefer-template`: template literals
- `eslint/prefer-object-has-own`: Object.hasOwn()
- `eslint/prefer-rest-params`: ...args over arguments
- `eslint/prefer-spread`: spread over .apply()

### Cherry-picked rules â€” warn

- `typescript/consistent-type-exports`: export type (nursery)
- `typescript/no-unnecessary-condition`: always-true/false (nursery)

- `typescript/no-unnecessary-type-parameters`: single-use type params (nursery)

- `typescript/prefer-readonly`: fields never reassigned (nursery)
- `typescript/prefer-string-starts-ends-with`: nursery
- `import/no-named-as-default`: importing named via default
- `import/no-named-as-default-member`: accessing named as default property

- `jsx_a11y/anchor-ambiguous-text`: "click here" links
- `unicorn/consistent-function-scoping`: hoist out of closures
- `unicorn/no-array-for-each`: for...of over .forEach()
- `unicorn/no-object-as-default-parameter`: mutable default objects
- `unicorn/explicit-length-check`: .length > 0 not .length
- `unicorn/no-hex-escape`: prefer unicode escapes

- `unicorn/no-unnecessary-array-splice-count`: redundant count

title: Add root package.json scripts for dev workflow
description:
## What

Add scripts to root `package.json` wrapping `vp` commands. These provide `pnpm test`, `pnpm lint`, etc. as muscle-memory aliases.

## Scripts

- `test`: `vp test`
- `lint`: `vp lint`
- `format`: `vp fmt`
- `format:check`: `vp fmt --check`
- `check`: `vp check`
- `typecheck`: `tsc --noEmit`
- `build`: `vp build`
- `dev`: deferred (placeholder echo) â€” two-process dev needs concurrency tooling

## Acceptance

- `pnpm test` runs vitest and passes
- `pnpm lint` runs oxlint with configured plugins/rules
- `pnpm format:check` verifies formatting without modifying files
- `pnpm check` runs lint + format + typecheck in one pass
- `pnpm typecheck` runs tsc independently
- `pnpm build` runs vp build

title: Walking skeleton â€” monorepo setup
description:
## Context

Greppa has zero implementation â€” only vision and stack docs. Before building domain logic, prove the full toolchain and architecture works end-to-end: CLI starts an Effect HTTP server, browser connects, everything builds/tests/lints.

## Requirements

- [ ] pnpm workspace with 5 packages (core, server, cli, web, ui)
- [ ] Vite+ (`vp`) as unified toolchain â€” build, test, lint, format
- [ ] Effect v4 beta.40 (pinned) for backend (server, cli, core)
- [ ] React 19 frontend (web) with Vite dev server proxying `/api` to `:4400`
- [ ] `greppa serve --port 4400` starts Effect HttpServer via @effect/cli
- [ ] `GET /api/health` returns `{ status: "ok" }`
- [ ] Web app fetches and renders health status
- [ ] All deps pinned to exact versions (save-exact=true)
- [ ] `vp test` passes, `vp lint` clean, `vp fmt` clean
- [ ] STACK.md updated with v4 and Vite+ corrections (done)

## Design decisions

| Decision | Choice |
|---|---|
| Setup depth | Walking skeleton (scaffold + health endpoint vertical slice) |
| Toolchain | Vite+ alpha, CLI is `vp`, MIT license |
| Effect | v4 beta.40 pinned to avoid unstable churn |
| Dev workflow | Two processes â€” Vite :5173 + Effect :4400 |
| Packages | All 5: core, server, cli, web, ui |

## Effect v4 patterns

- `ServiceMap.Service` (not `Effect.Service`)
- `effect/unstable/httpapi` for HttpApi/Builder/Group/Endpoint
- `effect/unstable/cli` with `Flag`/`Argument` (not Options/Args)
- `Schema.TaggedErrorClass` (not `Schema.TaggedError`)
- `@effect/vitest` `it.effect()` unchanged
- `@effect/sql-sqlite-node` unchanged

## Success criteria

1. `pnpm install` resolves all workspace deps
2. `vp test` runs and passes across all packages
3. `vp lint` and `vp fmt` report clean
4. `greppa serve` starts server on :4400
5. `curl localhost:4400/api/health` returns `{ status: "ok" }`
6. `pnpm --filter @greppa/web dev` proxies to server and renders health status
close_reason: All 7 tasks complete. Walking skeleton operational: CLI, server, web, tests, lint all working.

title: Root monorepo scaffold
description:
## What

Set up the root monorepo config that all packages build on.

## Deliverables

- `package.json` â€” `"type": "module"`, `"name": "greppa"`, `engines.node >= 22`, `packageManager` field for pnpm
- `pnpm-workspace.yaml` â€” `packages: ['packages/*']`
- `.npmrc` â€” `save-exact=true`, `strict-peer-dependencies=true`
- `tsconfig.base.json` â€” shared compiler options (ESM target, strict, moduleResolution bundler, paths)
- `vite.config.ts` â€” root Vite+ config (`defineConfig` from `vite-plus`) with lint/fmt settings
- Install `vite-plus` as root devDependency

## Verification

- `pnpm install` succeeds
- `vp --version` runs
- `vp check` runs (may warn about no source files â€” that's fine)

## Notes

- All deps pinned to exact versions (enforced by `.npmrc save-exact=true`)
- ESM throughout (`"type": "module"`)
- This task unblocks all package tasks

title: Package stubs â€” core, server, cli, web, ui
description:
## What

Create the 5 workspace packages with minimal package.json and tsconfig.json so pnpm resolves all internal deps and vp discovers them.

## Deliverables

- `packages/core/package.json` â€” `@greppa/core`, depends on `effect@4.0.0-beta.40`
- `packages/server/package.json` â€” `@greppa/server`, depends on `@greppa/core`, `effect`, `@effect/platform-node`
- `packages/cli/package.json` â€” `@greppa/cli`, depends on `@greppa/core`, `@greppa/server`, `effect`
- `packages/web/package.json` â€” `@greppa/web`, depends on `react`, `react-dom`
- `packages/ui/package.json` â€” `@greppa/ui`, depends on `react`, `react-dom`
- Each package gets a `tsconfig.json` extending `../../tsconfig.base.json`
- Each backend package (core, server, cli) gets a `src/index.ts` placeholder export
- web gets a Vite React entry point (minimal)
- ui gets a `src/index.ts` placeholder

## Verification

- `pnpm install` resolves all workspace deps
- `vp check` runs clean across all packages
- TypeScript project references resolve (no red squiggles on imports)

## Notes

- All deps pinned exact (enforced by .npmrc)
- ESM throughout (type: module in all package.json)
- Use workspace protocol for internal deps: `"@greppa/core": "workspace:*"`

title: Health endpoint â€” Effect HttpServer + CLI serve command
description:
## What

Wire the vertical slice: `greppa serve --port 4400` starts an Effect HttpServer that responds to `GET /api/health` with `{ status: \"ok\" }`.

## Deliverables

- `packages/server/src/HealthApi.ts` â€” HttpApiGroup with GET /api/health endpoint
- `packages/server/src/Api.ts` â€” HttpApi composing HealthApi
- `packages/server/src/Http.ts` â€” HttpApiBuilder setup binding handlers
- `packages/server/src/index.ts` â€” exports server layer (HttpServer.listen)
- `packages/cli/src/commands/serve.ts` â€” `serve` command with `--port` flag (default 4400)
- `packages/cli/src/main.ts` â€” root Command wiring serve subcommand
- `packages/cli/src/index.ts` â€” entry point calling NodeRuntime.runMain
- `packages/cli/package.json` â€” add bin field pointing to src/index.ts

## Verification

- `pnpm --filter @greppa/cli exec tsx src/index.ts serve --port 4400` starts the server
- `curl localhost:4400/api/health` returns `{ "status": "ok" }`
- `vp check` passes

## Notes

- Use Effect v4 patterns: `effect/unstable/httpapi`, `effect/unstable/cli`
- `ServiceMap.Service` for any services, `Flag` for CLI flags
- Keep it minimal â€” no middleware, no database, just the health endpoint

title: Set up test infrastructure â€” vp test, @effect/vitest

title: Long diff lines overflow horizontally instead of scrolling within panel
description: When a diff contains lines longer than the panel width, the content overflows and a horizontal scrollbar appears on the individual line rather than being contained within the DiffViewer panel. The table layout with white-space: pre on .content cells causes this â€” the overflow should be handled at the viewer level.

title: CI foundation: GitHub Actions, CodeRabbit, and Changesets
description:
## Problem

No CI exists. Code quality checks only run locally via pre-commit hooks. PRs can be merged without passing lint, typecheck, tests, or build. No automated code review. No release management.

## Requirements

1. CI workflow runs format check, lint, typecheck, test, and build as separate parallel jobs on PRs and pushes to main
2. All GitHub Actions are SHA-pinned with version comments
3. Cancel-in-progress enabled for superseded PR runs
4. Dependency review workflow blocks PRs introducing vulnerable dependencies
5. Changesets configured with GitHub changelog integration â€” release workflow creates Version Packages PR and publishes on merge
6. CodeRabbit configured with assertive profile, path filters for generated/lock files, knowledge base pointing to CLAUDE.md
7. Branch protection rules: require PRs (0 approvals), require status checks, linear history, block force pushes

## Success criteria

1. Pushing to a PR branch triggers CI that runs format:check, lint, typecheck, test, and build as separate parallel jobs â€” each visible as its own check in the GitHub UI
2. All actions use SHA-pinned references with version comments (e.g., actions/checkout@<sha> # v4.2.2)
3. Pushing a new commit to a PR while CI is running cancels the previous run
4. A PR adding a dependency with a known vulnerability fails the dependency-review check
5. Running pnpm changeset creates a changeset file; merging to main with changesets triggers a "Version Packages" PR via changesets/action
6. CodeRabbit posts a review comment on new PRs with assertive profile, skipping dist/lockfile/changeset paths
7. Direct pushes to main are blocked; PRs require passing status checks before merge
design:
## Problem

No CI exists. Code quality checks only run locally via pre-commit hooks. PRs can be merged without passing lint, typecheck, tests, or build. No automated code review. No release management.

## Requirements

1. CI workflow runs format check, lint, typecheck, test, and build as separate parallel jobs on PRs and pushes to main
2. All GitHub Actions are SHA-pinned with version comments
3. Cancel-in-progress enabled for superseded PR runs
4. Dependency review workflow blocks PRs introducing vulnerable dependencies
5. Changesets configured with GitHub changelog integration â€” release workflow creates Version Packages PR and publishes on merge
6. CodeRabbit configured with assertive profile, path filters for generated/lock files, knowledge base pointing to CLAUDE.md
7. Branch protection rules: require PRs (0 approvals), require status checks, linear history, block force pushes

## Success criteria

1. Pushing to a PR branch triggers CI that runs format:check, lint, typecheck, test, and build as separate parallel jobs â€” each visible as its own check in the GitHub UI
2. All actions use SHA-pinned references with version comments (e.g., actions/checkout@<sha> # v4.2.2)
3. Pushing a new commit to a PR while CI is running cancels the previous run
4. A PR adding a dependency with a known vulnerability fails the dependency-review check
5. Running pnpm changeset creates a changeset file; merging to main with changesets triggers a "Version Packages" PR via changesets/action
6. CodeRabbit posts a review comment on new PRs with assertive profile, skipping dist/lockfile/changeset paths
7. Direct pushes to main are blocked; PRs require passing status checks before merge

## Solution

Three GitHub Actions workflows (ci, dependency-review, release), a CodeRabbit config, and Changesets for release management.

## Architecture

### CI workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml)
- Triggers: push to main, pull_request to main
- Concurrency: ci-${{ github.ref }} with cancel-in-progress
- Permissions: contents: read
- 5 parallel jobs: format, lint, typecheck, test, build
- Each job: checkout â†’ pnpm/action-setup â†’ setup-node (node 22, cache pnpm) â†’ pnpm install --frozen-lockfile â†’ run command
- Commands: pnpm format:check, pnpm lint, pnpm typecheck, pnpm test, pnpm build

### Dependency review workflow (.github/workflows/dependency-review.yml)
- Triggers: pull_request only
- Single job using actions/dependency-review-action with fail-on-severity: moderate

### Release workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml)
- Triggers: push to main only
- Concurrency: release-${{ github.ref }}
- Uses changesets/action with commitMode: github-api
- Publish command: pnpm ci:publish (pnpm publish -r --access public --no-git-checks)
- Creates GitHub Releases automatically
- Permissions: contents: write, pull-requests: write

### CodeRabbit config (.coderabbit.yaml)
- Profile: assertive
- Tone: concise and direct, flag real issues, skip style enforced by oxlint/oxfmt
- Path filters: exclude dist/, node_modules/, lockfiles, .beads/, .changeset/*.md
- Knowledge base: CLAUDE.md
- Auto-review: enabled, skip drafts and WIP titles

### Changesets config (.changeset/config.json)
- Changelog: @changesets/changelog-github with repo sQVe/greppa
- Access: public
- Base branch: main
- updateInternalDependencies: patch

### Branch protection (manual via GitHub UI)
- Require PRs (0 approvals for solo)
- Required status checks: format, lint, typecheck, test, build, dependency-review
- Require linear history
- Block force pushes and deletions
- Require conversation resolution

## Key decisions
- Single ci.yml with parallel jobs (not per-package workflows) â€” project is small, 5 packages
- Separate steps for format/lint/typecheck â€” better GitHub UI error visibility than bundled pnpm check
- SHA-pinned actions â€” security best practice after tj-actions supply chain attack
- Corepack enabled in setup-node to enforce pnpm version from packageManager field
- Node 22 LTS pinned in CI
- changesets/action with commitMode: github-api â€” compatible with branch protection signed commit requirements
- release.yml uses pnpm install WITHOUT --frozen-lockfile â€” changesets bumps versions so lockfile needs updating
- ci:publish uses --access public --no-git-checks â€” required for scoped packages and detached CI state

comments.author: Oskar Grunning

All local checks pass: format, lint, typecheck, 13 tests, build.

title: Create CI workflow with parallel quality check jobs
description:
## Goal

Create .github/workflows/ci.yml with 5 parallel jobs (format, lint, typecheck, test, build) that run on PRs and pushes to main. SHA-pin all actions. Include cancel-in-progress concurrency.

## Behaviors

1. Workflow triggers on push to main and pull_request to main
2. Concurrency group ci-${{ github.ref }} cancels in-progress runs on same ref
3. Workflow-level permissions set to contents: read
4. Each of 5 jobs (format, lint, typecheck, test, build) runs independently in parallel
5. Each job: checkout â†’ pnpm/action-setup â†’ setup-node (node 22, cache pnpm) â†’ pnpm install --frozen-lockfile â†’ run command
6. All action references use full SHA with version comment
7. Corepack enabled via setup-node for pnpm version enforcement

## Success criteria

- .github/workflows/ci.yml exists with valid YAML syntax
- 5 jobs visible: format, lint, typecheck, test, build
- All actions SHA-pinned (no @v4 tag references)
- Concurrency group with cancel-in-progress: true present
- permissions: contents: read set at workflow level
- Each job uses node 22 and pnpm cache

title: Create dependency review workflow

title: Configure CodeRabbit for AI code reviews

Create .coderabbit.yaml with assertive profile, adapted from nit.nvim config for this TypeScript monorepo.

## Behaviors

1. Assertive review profile
2. Tone: concise and direct, flag real issues, skip style enforced by oxlint/oxfmt
3. Path filters exclude dist/, node_modules/, lockfiles, .beads/, .changeset/*.md

title: Configure branch protection rules on GitHub
description:
## Goal

Apply branch protection rules for the main branch via GitHub rulesets.

## Behaviors

1. Direct pushes to main are blocked â€” all changes require a PR
2. PRs require passing status checks (Format, Lint, Typecheck, Test, Build, Dependency review) before merge
3. Only linear history allowed (rebase merges, no merge commits)
4. Force pushes to main are blocked
5. Branch deletion of main is blocked
6. Conversations must be resolved before merge

## Note

This is a manual step â€” configure via GitHub Settings > Rules > Rulesets, or via gh CLI. Cannot be automated in a workflow file.

## Success criteria

- Ruleset active on main branch
- Direct pushes blocked
- PRs require passing status checks before merge
- Linear history enforced

title: Configure Renovate for automated dependency updates
description:
## Goal

Create renovate.json config at repo root for automated dependency updates. Install Renovate GitHub App on the sQVe/greppa repo.

## Behaviors

1. Renovate opens grouped PRs for dependency updates (not one PR per dep)
2. GitHub Actions SHA pins are updated with version comments preserved
3. Patch and minor updates for devDependencies are automerged after CI passes
4. Major updates open separate PRs for manual review
5. pnpm lockfile maintenance runs on a weekly schedule
6. Renovate respects the monorepo workspace structure
7. PRs for lockfile-only or pin-only changes skip changeset requirement

## Success criteria

- renovate.json exists at repo root with valid JSON
- Group presets configured: actions, devDependencies, production dependencies
- Automerge enabled for non-major devDependency updates
- Schedule set to avoid noise (e.g., weekly or weekday-only)
- SHA pin updating configured for GitHub Actions workflows

title: Extract logic from JSX into custom hooks and testable modules
description: Many component files are too large and mix rendering with business logic. JSX files should hold minimal logic â€” extract into custom hooks (or use official React hooks) so logic is independently testable and components stay thin.

title: Extract syntax highlighting logic from DiffViewer into useSyntaxHighlighting hook
description:
## What

Extract the syntax highlighting pipeline from `DiffViewer.tsx` into a dedicated `useSyntaxHighlighting` hook in `packages/web/src/hooks/`.

### Logic to extract

- `getOrCreateHighlighter` singleton
- `useHighlighter` hook
- `buildTokenEntries` pure function
- `useTokenMap` hook
- `diffLineKey` helper (shared by both `buildRows` and token logic)

### Target shape

A single `useSyntaxHighlighting(diff, theme)` hook that returns a `tokenMap: Map<string, ThemedToken[]> | null`. Internal helpers (`buildTokenEntries`, `getOrCreateHighlighter`, `diffLineKey`) become module-private or exported for testing.

### Success criteria

- `DiffViewer.tsx` imports and calls `useSyntaxHighlighting` instead of the inline hooks
- All existing DiffViewer tests pass unchanged
- New test file for the hook covering: null diff, null highlighter, token map population, multi-hunk entries
- `buildRows` still works (it uses `diffLineKey` which moves to the new module)

title: Extract file selection and review tracking from App into useFileSelection hook
description:
## What

Extract file selection state, review tracking, and derived data from `App.tsx` into a `useFileSelection` hook in `packages/web/src/hooks/`.

### Logic to extract

- `collectFiles` pure function (flattens FileNode tree to file list)
- `initialReviewed` computation (pre-reviewed set from fixture data)
- `selectedFilePath` / `setSelectedFilePath` state
- `reviewedPaths` / `setReviewedPaths` state
- `handleSelectFile` callback (selects file + marks as reviewed)
- Derived data: filtered comments for selected file, fileInfo lookup for selected file

### Target shape

A `useFileSelection(files, diffs, comments, fileInfoMap)` hook returning:
- `selectedFilePath`
- `selectFile` (the handler)
- `reviewedCount`
- `totalCount`
- `selectedDiff`
- `selectedThreads`
- `selectedFileInfo`

### Success criteria

- `App.tsx` becomes a pure layout shell with no business logic
- All existing App tests pass unchanged
- New test file for the hook covering: initial state, selecting a file, marking as reviewed, re-selecting already-reviewed file, derived data filtering
- `collectFiles` is independently testable

title: Extract buildRows and row types from DiffViewer into dedicated module
description:
## What

Extract `buildRows`, `RowSide`, and `DiffRow` from `DiffViewer.tsx` into `packages/web/src/lib/buildRows.ts`.

### Logic to extract

- `RowSide` interface
- `DiffRow` interface
- `buildRows(hunk)` pure function (lines 21-83)

### Success criteria

- `DiffViewer.tsx` imports `buildRows` and types from the new module
- All existing DiffViewer tests pass unchanged
- New test file with direct unit tests for `buildRows` covering: context lines, removed/added pairing, unmatched lines produce null sides, flush at end of hunk

title: Epic: DiffViewer and StackedDiffViewer refactor into hooks and subcomponents
description:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- All planned extractions complete: useDiffVirtualItems, useFlatItemIndex, useActiveFile, SplitDiffRow, CollapsedDiffNotice, FilePath.
- Each extraction preserves existing component behavior â€” no change to rendered diff output, virtualization, scroll behavior, active-file tracking, or token rendering.
- Extracted hooks live in packages/web/src/hooks/ with co-located .test.ts.
- Extracted subcomponents stay co-located with their parent directory (SplitDiffRow in DiffViewer/, CollapsedDiffNotice in DiffViewer/, FilePath in StackedDiffViewer/).
- Host component public props stay unchanged.
- Each extraction lands as its own atomic commit.

## Success criteria
- [ ] useDiffVirtualItems hook extracted; DiffViewer consumes it.
- [ ] useFlatItemIndex hook extracted; StackedDiffViewer consumes it.
- [ ] useActiveFile hook extracted; StackedDiffViewer consumes it.
- [ ] SplitDiffRow subcomponent extracted; VirtualRow and DiffRowRenderer consume it.
- [ ] CollapsedDiffNotice subcomponent extracted; DiffViewer renders it for collapsed state.
- [ ] FilePath subcomponent extracted; FileHeader renders it.
- [ ] Every extracted unit has dedicated test coverage.
- [ ] No public prop signature changed on DiffViewer, StackedDiffViewer, FileHeader, VirtualRow, or DiffRowRenderer.
- [ ] All tests passing.
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing.

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO behavior changes in the same commit as an extraction (reason: keeps the commit reviewable as a pure move; behavior tweaks belong in a separate follow-up).
- NO temporary parallel names like DiffViewerV2 (reason: extraction is atomic; rename callers in the same commit).
- NO expanding test coverage beyond what exercises the new unit (reason: scope creep; coverage gaps are separate tasks).
- NO public prop changes on the host component (reason: downstream callers and tests should not need simultaneous edits).
- NO drive-by cleanup of unrelated code (reason: pollutes the diff; split into its own commit/task if worth doing).
- NO bundling multiple extractions into one commit (reason: each extraction must be independently revertable).
- NO replacing @tanstack/react-virtual with a custom virtualizer (reason: explicit out of scope; virtualization behavior must be preserved byte-for-byte).

## Durable decisions
- Hooks directory: packages/web/src/hooks/ (matches existing project convention).
- Subcomponents: co-located with parent directory (matches FileHeader.tsx alongside StackedDiffViewer.tsx).
- Naming: useX for hooks (camelCase), PascalCase.tsx for subcomponents.
- Test co-location: .test.ts/.test.tsx next to source.
- Virtualization library: @tanstack/react-virtual (unchanged).

## Approach
Six independent extractions, each landing as its own atomic commit. Existing component tests pin behavior; new unit tests exercise the extracted units in isolation. Work order favors useFlatItemIndex as a pathfinder (smallest self-contained extraction), then the two larger hooks (useDiffVirtualItems, useActiveFile), then the three subcomponents.

Virtualization internals are preserved unchanged. Hook extractions pass scrollRef/flatItems/containerRef as parameters rather than trying to abstract the virtualizer itself.

## Architecture
- packages/web/src/hooks/useDiffVirtualItems.ts (new) â€” hunks + items + tier/isCollapsed/shouldDeferHighlighting pipeline from DiffViewer.tsx:59-71.
- packages/web/src/hooks/useFlatItemIndex.ts (new) â€” fileHeaderIndices + filePathByIndex + visibleDiffs filter from StackedDiffViewer.tsx:52-99.
- packages/web/src/hooks/useActiveFile.ts (new) â€” activeFileIndex state + updateActiveFile callback + effects from StackedDiffViewer.tsx:136-170.
- packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/SplitDiffRow.tsx (new) â€” split-row render shared by VirtualRow and DiffRowRenderer.
- packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/CollapsedDiffNotice.tsx (new) â€” collapsed-state branch from DiffViewer.tsx:93-103.
- packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/FilePath.tsx (new) â€” path-segment rendering from FileHeader.tsx:116-124.

## Design rationale

### Problem
DiffViewer and StackedDiffViewer concentrate virtualization, scroll tracking, and row rendering inline. The audit surfaced six sites where cohesive pipelines or duplicated render blocks can be extracted without changing output. Two components share an identical split-row wrapper (VirtualRow, DiffRowRenderer); duplication must be removed.

### Research findings

**Codebase audit (3-agent sweep, April 2026):**
- packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/DiffViewer.tsx:59-71 â€” three useMemos forming a single diffâ†’virtual-items pipeline.
- packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/DiffViewer.tsx:93-103 â€” collapsed-state conditional with derived totalLines.
- packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/StackedDiffViewer.tsx:52-99 â€” three memos deriving index maps + visibleDiffs filter.
- packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/StackedDiffViewer.tsx:136-170 â€” 35 lines of activeFileIndex state + effects for scroll-position tracking.
- packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/FileHeader.tsx:116-124 â€” path-segment rendering (~10 lines of segments.map).
- packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/VirtualRow.tsx:38-53 + packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/DiffRowRenderer.tsx:26-29 â€” duplicated split-row wrapper (two RowSideCell instances inside styled row div).

**Existing patterns:**
- packages/web/src/hooks/useDiffComputation.ts â€” reference for diff-pipeline hook.
- packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/FileHeader.tsx â€” reference for subcomponent co-located with parent.

### Approaches considered

#### 1. Six independent atomic commits (selected)

**What:** Each extraction lands as its own commit; pathfinder is useFlatItemIndex.
**Pros:** Independently revertable, each commit a pure move, test pinning incremental.
**Cons:** Six commits.
**Chosen because:** Anti-pattern rules require atomic extractions; units have no interdependencies.

#### 2. Combined StackedDiffViewer-only hook `useStackedDiffState` (rejected)

**What:** Fold useFlatItemIndex + useActiveFile into one hook that owns all StackedDiffViewer state.
**Why explored:** They feed each other (useActiveFile consumes fileHeaderIndices).
**Why rejected:** The two concerns are individually reusable â€” useFlatItemIndex is pure data derivation, useActiveFile drives scroll tracking. Combining them hides boundaries.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** A third consumer emerges that needs both simultaneously.

### Scope boundaries

**In scope:** Six extractions listed in Success criteria.
**Out of scope:** CommitList and FileTree refactors (Epic A), StatusBar/ActivityRail/DetailPanel refactors (Epic C), virtualization library swap, behavior changes, prop signature changes.

### Open questions
- useActiveFile hook signature: take (containerRef, fileHeaderIndices, flatItems, onActiveFileChange) or narrow further? Defer to first task touching it.
- SplitDiffRow prop shape (left/right cells) â€” confirmed in the first task touching it.

## Design discovery

### Key decisions made

| Question | Answer | Implication |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| First task? | useFlatItemIndex | Smallest self-contained extraction, good pathfinder |
| Combine useFlatItemIndex + useActiveFile? | No | Each is individually reusable |
| Touch virtualizer internals? | No | @tanstack/react-virtual behavior preserved byte-for-byte |

### Dead-end paths

#### Combined useStackedDiffState hook
**Why explored:** useActiveFile consumes fileHeaderIndices.
**What found:** Hides boundaries between pure derivation and side-effecting scroll tracking.
**Why abandoned:** Split wins on reusability and testability.

### Open concerns raised
- Behavior preservation for virtualization -> enforced by anti-pattern (NO virtualizer replacement) and by existing snapshot/integration tests.
- Active-file tracking timing (scroll effects) -> useActiveFile preserves existing effect dependencies exactly.

title: Task 1: Extract useFlatItemIndex hook from StackedDiffViewer
description:
## Goal
Extract the flat-item index maps (fileHeaderIndices, filePathByIndex) and visibleDiffs filter from StackedDiffViewer.tsx:52-99 into a useFlatItemIndex hook in packages/web/src/hooks/. StackedDiffViewer consumes the hook; existing tests continue to pass unchanged.

## Execution mode
AFK â€” behavior-preserving extraction. Existing StackedDiffViewer tests pin the behavior; the hook is a pure data-derivation refactor.

## Behaviors
1. Hook useFlatItemIndex(flatItems, diffs) returns { fileHeaderIndices, filePathByIndex, visibleDiffs } â€” a factory for visibleDiffs(visibleVirtualItems) â€” with semantics identical to the current inline useMemos.
2. fileHeaderIndices maps header virtual-item index â†’ file path, matching current derivation.
3. filePathByIndex maps every virtual-item index â†’ its owning file path.
4. visibleDiffs factory filters diffs to those whose headers appear in the supplied visibleVirtualItems, matching StackedDiffViewer.tsx:90-99 exactly.
5. StackedDiffViewer.tsx imports and consumes the hook; inline useMemos for these three derivations are removed; scroll-handle, active-file tracking, and token-map lookup call sites still work unchanged.
6. Existing packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/StackedDiffViewer.test.tsx continues to pass without modification.

## References
- packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitSelection.ts â€” hook file structure + test co-location pattern.
- packages/web/src/hooks/useComputedDiffs.ts â€” hook deriving from diff data.
- packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/StackedDiffViewer.tsx:52-99 â€” source of the extraction.
- packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/StackedDiffViewer.test.tsx â€” existing behavior pins.

## Success criteria
- [ ] packages/web/src/hooks/useFlatItemIndex.ts exists and exports the hook.
- [ ] packages/web/src/hooks/useFlatItemIndex.test.ts covers the four behavioral semantics above.
- [ ] StackedDiffViewer.tsx delegates the three memos to the hook; no inline useMemo for these derivations remains.
- [ ] All existing tests pass (StackedDiffViewer.test.tsx unchanged).
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing.
- [ ] Commit is atomic â€” only the hook extraction and its StackedDiffViewer call-site swap (plus new tests).

title: Epic: Expandable commit groups with per-commit file review
description:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Commits section rows gain a left-side chevron; chevron toggles expansion, row click unchanged (toggles SHA in `commits` URL param, driving the Changes aggregate).
- Expanded commit shows a flat list of files touched by that commit; no directory grouping inside an expanded commit.
- Clicking a file inside an expanded commit toggles a `"sha:path"` entry in a new URL param `commitFile`; does not modify `commits`.
- Diff viewer renders entries from `commitFile` as per-commit patches (same path across two commits = two distinct entries, never merged).
- `useReviewState` gains `reviewedCommitFiles: string[]` keyed `"sha:path"`, independent of `reviewedPaths`.
- `CommitEntry` schema (packages/core/src/index.ts:62) gains `files: readonly string[]`; server populates via `git log --name-only` over the existing ref range â€” no extra endpoint.
- Three sidebar sections (Changes, Working tree, Commits) remain; only the Commits section changes.

## Success criteria
- [ ] Commit rows render with a chevron; chevron expands/collapses without altering `commits` selection.
- [ ] Expanded commit lists files from that commit as flat children.
- [ ] Clicking a file in an expanded commit adds `"sha:path"` to `commitFile` and opens a per-commit diff entry in the viewer.
- [ ] Same path in two different commits renders as two independent diff entries.
- [ ] `reviewedCommitFiles` toggles independently of `reviewedPaths` and persists to localStorage.
- [ ] `CommitEntry.files` populated by the server with no additional round trip.
- [ ] Tests cover: URL param round-trip for `commitFile`, independence of reviewed-state tracks, chevron-vs-row click separation, server `files` population.
- [ ] All tests passing.
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing.

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO merging same-path entries from different commits in the diff viewer (reason: each commit's changes to file X are a distinct review unit per user decision).
- NO hunk-level selection (reason: out of scope; user explicitly deferred).
- NO separate endpoint for per-commit files (reason: ref range already scans commits; adding `--name-only` is free and avoids a round trip).
- NO coupling `commitFile` selection to `commits` aggregate (reason: viewing a commit's patch for one file must not change the Changes aggregate).
- NO shared reviewed-state between path-level and commit-scoped (reason: marking file X reviewed at commit A must not mark X reviewed globally).
- NO directory grouping inside an expanded commit (reason: per-commit file counts are small; flat list keeps visual distinction from the Changes tree).
- NO auto-expanding commits on load (reason: expand is a deliberate user action; default view stays compact).

## Durable decisions
- URL param shape: `commitFile: string[]`, entries formatted `"<sha>:<path>"`. Parallel to existing `file`/`wt` params.
- Reviewed-state shape in localStorage: `reviewedCommitFiles: string[]` alongside existing `reviewedPaths: string[]`.
- `CommitEntry.files` is ordered by git's natural output; no client-side sorting contract.
- Diff scope for commit-scoped entries: the patch introduced by that single commit for that path (`sha^..sha -- path`), not cumulative.
- Commits API endpoint remains singular â€” files ship inline with commit list.

## Approach
Extend the existing commits response with per-commit file names, reuse the RAC Tree primitive already used by `FileTree` to render an expandable `CommitList`, and introduce a new URL param + reviewed-state slice that parallels the path-based ones. The diff viewer already iterates active selections; `commitFile` becomes a third source alongside `file`/`wt`.

No data migration (new param is additive), no new endpoints, no reshaping of `commits`/Changes behavior. The Changes aggregate stays authoritative for whole-commit review; `commitFile` is a strictly additive drill-down channel.

## Architecture
- packages/core/src/index.ts:62 â€” extend `CommitEntry` with `files: readonly string[]`.
- Server commits endpoint â€” populate `files` via `git log --name-only` over the existing ref range.
- packages/web/src/components/CommitList/CommitList.tsx â€” migrate from flat button list to RAC `Tree` (see packages/ui/src/components/Tree/Tree.tsx); chevron expansion via local `expandedKeysRef` (mirror packages/web/src/components/FileTree/FileTree.tsx).
- packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitFileSelection.ts (new) â€” URL param helpers for `commitFile`, mirroring useFileSelection.
- packages/web/src/hooks/useReviewState.ts â€” add `reviewedCommitFiles` track.
- Diff viewer â€” consume `commitFile` entries, fetch per-commit patches scoped to `sha^..sha -- path`.

## Design rationale

### Problem
PR review in greppa today collapses every selected commit into a flat aggregate file list. Reviewers lose the author's commit-level chunking and cannot drill into a single commit's changes to a single file. This matters for PRs where commits carry distinct intent (refactor vs feature vs tests).

### Research findings

**Codebase:**
- packages/web/src/components/CommitList/CommitList.tsx â€” current flat button list; shift/cmd toggle SHAs in `commits`.
- packages/web/src/components/FileTree/FileTree.tsx â€” RAC Tree with chevron expansion via `expandedKeysRef`; direct model for CommitList.
- packages/web/src/components/FileTree/FileTreePanel.tsx â€” three collapsible sections; unchanged by this epic.
- packages/web/src/hooks/useReviewState.ts â€” localStorage-backed `{collapsedPaths, reviewedPaths}`; extended with `reviewedCommitFiles`.
- packages/core/src/index.ts:62 â€” `CommitEntry` schema; gains `files`.
- packages/ui/src/components/Tree/Tree.tsx â€” shared RAC Tree primitive; reused.

**External:**
- git `log --name-only` / `--name-status` â€” per-commit file list in the same pass.

### Approaches considered

#### 1. Path-set model with `commitFile` URL param (selected)

**What:** Add `commitFile: string[]` of `"sha:path"` entries. `commits` unchanged. Files ship inline on `CommitEntry`.
**Pros:** Additive; zero migration; backward-compatible; one round trip.
**Cons:** URL can grow with heavy selection.
**Chosen because:** Preserves existing Changes-aggregate semantics, minimal surface area, matches user decisions (row click unchanged, chevron expands, cross-commit mix allowed).

#### 2. Unified `selection: Array<{sha, path?}>` model (rejected)

**What:** Replace `commits` with a single list where entries may optionally carry a path.
**Why explored:** Uniform leaves â€” every selection identified the same way.
**Why rejected:** Churns every existing `commits` consumer for no user-visible gain; diff aggregator needs rewrite.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** A future feature demands ordered/heterogeneous selection lists.

#### 3. Lazy per-commit files endpoint (rejected)

**What:** Fetch a commit's files on first expand via a new endpoint.
**Why explored:** Keeps commit-list payload small for PRs with many commits.
**Why rejected:** Adds latency to expand, forces prefetch, premature until commit counts actually hurt.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** A PR with 100+ commits measurably bloats the commits response.

### Scope boundaries

**In scope:** Commits section expansion, per-commit file click, `commitFile` URL param, per-commit reviewed-state, schema + server `files` population, diff viewer consumption of `commitFile`.
**Out of scope:** Hunk-level selection; auto-grouping commits by area (separate notebox task); directory grouping inside a commit; prefetch/lazy loading of per-commit files; cross-commit blame.

### Open questions
- Keyboard shortcut for expand/collapse of a commit row (RAC Tree defaults likely suffice).
- Exact ordering of entries in the diff viewer when `file` + `commitFile` are both active.

## Design discovery

### Key decisions made

| Question | Answer | Implication |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| Keep Changes / Working tree / Commits sections? | Yes | Scope shrinks to Commits section only |
| Diff scope for a file inside a commit? | Per-commit patch | Aggregator must accept a commit-scoped mode |
| Cross-commit file mixing allowed? | Yes | Selection is an additive list of `"sha:path"` |
| Default when a commit row is clicked? | Whole-commit select (existing) | Chevron separate from row click |
| Click semantics: row vs chevron? | Chevron expands, row selects | No merged affordance |
| Same file across two expanded commits? | Two distinct entries | Never merged in diff viewer |
| Reviewed-state for commit-scoped files? | Separate from path-level | New `reviewedCommitFiles` track |
| Hunk-level selection? | No | Explicitly out of scope |

### Dead-end paths

#### Full sidebar reframe (commits become the primary axis)
**Why explored:** Initial brainstorm pushed toward treating commits as containers and killing the flat Changes section.
**What found:** User rejected â€” Changes/Working tree/Commits sections stay intact.
**Why abandoned:** Over-scoped. True task is narrower: make commit rows expandable for drill-down.

### Open concerns raised
- URL length with many `"sha:path"` entries -> accept for now; revisit if real users hit limits.
- Commit row with zero files (empty commit) -> chevron disabled; no expansion affordance.
design: test

title: Task 1: Expand one commit, click one file, render its per-commit diff
description:
## Goal
Deliver a thin end-to-end slice: a commit row in the Commits section can be expanded via a chevron, clicking one file inside it adds a `"sha:path"` entry to a new `commitFile` URL param, and the diff viewer renders the patch introduced by that commit for that path. No reviewed-state, no multi-select semantics, no shift/cmd handling â€” those come in later tasks.

## Execution mode
HITL â€” UI interaction decisions (chevron placement, empty-commit affordance, child-row styling) benefit from human review mid-implementation.

## Behaviors
Each is a single TDD cycle. Stop between them; one test first, then the minimum code to pass.

1. `CommitEntry` schema accepts `files: readonly string[]` (decode test in packages/core).
2. Server commits endpoint populates `files` via `git log --name-only` over the existing ref range (integration test against a fixture repo).
3. `commitFile` URL param encodes/decodes a `string[]` with `"<sha>:<path>"` entries (round-trip test; mirror `useFileSelection` test).
4. New hook `useCommitFileSelection` exposes `entries`, `toggle(sha, path)`, `isSelected(sha, path)` (unit test).
5. `CommitList` renders with a chevron on each row; chevron click toggles local expansion state; does NOT modify `commits` (component test).
6. Expanding a commit renders its `files` as flat child rows in the tree (component test).
7. Clicking a child file row calls `useCommitFileSelection.toggle(sha, path)`; does NOT modify `commits` (component test).
8. Diff viewer, given a `commitFile` entry, fetches and renders the patch for `sha^..sha -- path` (integration test with a fixture repo; same file appearing in two expanded commits renders as two distinct entries).

After behavior 8 lands, the feature is demonstrable end-to-end for a single file in a single commit, with cross-commit duplication proven. Reviewed-state, keyboard, and multi-select are separate follow-up tasks.

## References
- packages/web/src/components/FileTree/FileTree.tsx â€” RAC Tree + chevron expansion via `expandedKeysRef`; primary reference for CommitList restructure.
- packages/web/src/hooks/useFileSelection.ts â€” URL param helper pattern to mirror in useCommitFileSelection.
- packages/web/src/hooks/useReviewState.ts â€” localStorage-backed state pattern (consulted but not modified in this task).
- packages/ui/src/components/Tree/Tree.tsx â€” shared RAC Tree primitive.
- packages/core/src/index.ts:62 â€” CommitEntry schema to extend.

## Success criteria
- [ ] `CommitEntry.files` decodes from server and surfaces in the commit list data.
- [ ] Chevron on a commit row toggles expansion; row click still toggles `commits` (regression-free).
- [ ] Expanded commit shows flat file list; clicking a file writes a `"sha:path"` entry to `commitFile`; `commits` unchanged.
- [ ] Diff viewer renders a per-commit patch for a single `commitFile` entry.
- [ ] Same file under two different commits renders as two independent diff entries.
- [ ] All tests passing.
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing.
design: test

title: Task 2: Fix commitFile URL param dropped when state id is set
description:
## Goal
Fix a silent data-loss bug: when the router encodes state into `?s=<id>`, any subsequent write of `commitFile` is discarded by `stringifySearch`, because `StatePayload` does not include `commitFile`. After commit #1 is selected, clicking a file inside any expanded commit no longer writes to the URL, so the diff viewer never receives a `commitFile` entry. This breaks the epic success criterion "Same path in two different commits renders as two independent diff entries" in practice.

## Behaviors
One TDD cycle per item.

1. `StatePayload` (`packages/web/src/stateCache.ts`) and `StateData` (`packages/core/src/index.ts`) gain `commitFile: Schema.Array(Schema.String)`. `stateKey` includes commitFile so cache keys stay unique.
2. `useCommitSelection.buildState`/`navigateWithState` carry forward current `commitFile` into every new state (read via `useRouterState`). Regression test: click commit A â†’ URL encodes current `commitFile` in the new state id.
3. `useCommitFileSelection.toggle` writes via `getOrCreateStateId` with `{ file, wt, commits, commitFile: next }`, not raw `navigate({ search })`. Regression test: toggle with empty commits writes plain `?commitFile=` as before; toggle with non-empty commits writes to the state-id payload.
