4. `commitsRoute.beforeLoad` redirect includes `commitFile: state.commitFile` so reload from a shared URL restores selection.
5. Integration test: click commit, click another commit, click a file â†’ `useSelectionCoordinator.commitFileDiffs.diffs.length === 1`; repeated clicks toggle (length â†’ 0).

## Success criteria
- [ ] Clicking a file inside an expanded commit after selecting one or more commits writes the entry to the URL-encoded state and surfaces it in the diff viewer.
- [ ] Reload preserves `commitFile` selection.
- [ ] Existing single-file tests (no commits selected) unchanged.
- [ ] All tests passing.
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing.

## References
- packages/web/src/router.ts:49-66 â€” `stringifySearch` short-circuit to be worked around via state payload.
- packages/web/src/stateCache.ts:3-18 â€” `StatePayload`, `stateKey`.
- packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitSelection.ts:10-14,29-35 â€” `buildState`/`navigateWithState`.
- packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitFileSelection.ts:42-55 â€” `toggle` to rewrite.
- packages/core/src/index.ts:44-49 â€” `StateData` schema.
- packages/server/src/StateApi.ts â€” no code change needed; payload shape comes from core schema.
design:
## Expanded plan (expand-task)

### Investigation findings

**Files to modify:**
- `packages/core/src/index.ts:44-49` â€” `StateData` schema needs `commitFile: Schema.Array(Schema.String)`. `StateSaveRequest`/`StateSaveResponse` inherit via `...StateData.fields`.
- `packages/web/src/stateCache.ts:3-18` â€” `StatePayload` interface gains `commitFile: string[]`; `stateKey` must include it so cache keys stay unique across selections that differ only by `commitFile`.
- `packages/server/src/Http.ts:29-33,272` â€” `StoredState` interface and the `saveState` handler's store write must thread `commitFile` through.
- `packages/web/src/router.ts:23` â€” `commitsSearch` already accepts `commitFile` (no change). `commitsRoute.beforeLoad` redirect at L158 must add `commitFile: state.commitFile`. L116 and L136 cross-section redirects already spread `...state`, so they pick up `commitFile` once `StatePayload` gains the field.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitSelection.ts:10-14,16-17,20,29-35` â€” `buildState` currently returns `{file:[], wt:[], commits:shas}`. It must carry forward the current `commitFile` from router state. `navigateWithState` must include `commitFile` in the final route search so the URL round-trips.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitFileSelection.ts:42-55` â€” `toggle` uses `navigate({ search: (prev) => ({ ...prev, commitFile: next }) })`. When `?s=<id>` is active, `prev` only contains `{s, commits}` because `commitFile` is not in `StatePayload`. Fix: read `file`/`wt`/`commits` from router state, call `getOrCreateStateId({file, wt, commits, commitFile: next})`, navigate with `{ s: id, commits, commitFile: next }`.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useMultiSelect.ts:28-31` â€” `buildState` must include `commitFile: []` to satisfy the new `StatePayload` shape.

**Patterns to follow:**
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitSelection.test.ts:49-54` â€” `commitsUrl` helper seeds `?s=<id>` via `cacheState` and renders hook. Reuse pattern for the `?s=` round-trip regression test.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitSelection.ts:16-17,20` â€” `useRouterState({ select: ... })` selector pattern.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useMultiSelect.ts:68-78` â€” `navigateWithState` composing `getOrCreateStateId` + `navigate`.

**Reusable code:**
- `packages/web/src/toStringArray.ts` â€” already used by both hooks.
- `packages/web/src/stateCache.ts:38-47` `getOrCreateStateId()` â€” mint-id entry point.
- `cacheState(id, state)` â€” seed state in tests.

**Blast radius:**
- `StatePayload`/`StateData` construction sites: `useCommitSelection.buildState`, `useMultiSelect.buildState` (must add `commitFile: []`). No other sites.
- `getOrCreateStateId` callsites: `useCommitSelection:31`, `useMultiSelect:70`.
- Test helpers: `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitSelection.test.ts:50` (`commitsUrl`) constructs a literal; needs `commitFile: []`.
- Server `StoredState` must stay in lockstep or `saveState` drops the field.
- `commitsRoute.beforeLoad` redirect â€” the only redirect that specifically needs `commitFile`.

**Pre-existing issues (out of scope, surface only):**
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitSelection.test.ts:27-33` and `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitList.test.ts:28` construct `CommitEntry` without `files` (added in commit 0359288). Pre-existing typecheck mismatches; do not fix.

### Steps

### Step 1: StateData / StatePayload / StoredState carry commitFile

**Pattern:** `packages/core/src/index.ts:44-49` â€” another `Schema.Array(Schema.String)` field alongside `file`/`wt`/`commits`.

**Changes:**
- `packages/core/src/index.ts:44-49` â€” add `commitFile: Schema.Array(Schema.String)` to `StateData`.
- `packages/web/src/stateCache.ts:3-7` â€” add `commitFile: string[]` to `StatePayload`.
- `packages/web/src/stateCache.ts:17-18` â€” include `state.commitFile` in `stateKey` tuple.
- `packages/server/src/Http.ts:29-33` â€” add `commitFile: string[]` to `StoredState`.
- `packages/server/src/Http.ts:272` â€” spread `commitFile: [...payload.commitFile]` in store write.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitSelection.ts:10-14` â€” `buildState` returns `commitFile: []` (Step 2 makes it carry forward).
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useMultiSelect.ts:28-31` â€” both `buildState` branches return `commitFile: []`.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitSelection.test.ts:50` â€” `commitsUrl` helper literal gets `commitFile: []`.

**Verify:** `pnpm --filter @greppa/core test && pnpm --filter @greppa/web test -- stateCache useCommitSelection useCommitFileSelection && pnpm --filter @greppa/server test`

### Step 2: useCommitSelection carries commitFile through every new state

**Pattern:** `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitSelection.ts:16-17,20` â€” existing selector pattern.

**Changes:**
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitSelection.ts` â€” add `selectCommitFileParams` via `toStringArray(s.location.search.commitFile)`. Read via `useRouterState`. Rework call sites so `navigateWithState` receives a payload with current `commitFile`. In `navigateWithState`, add `commitFile: state.commitFile` to the navigate `search`.
- `clear()` at L37-40 â€” include `commitFile: []` in the search reset.

**Verify:** `pnpm --filter @greppa/web test -- useCommitSelection`

### Step 3: useCommitFileSelection.toggle writes via getOrCreateStateId

**Pattern:** `packages/web/src/hooks/useMultiSelect.ts:68-78`.

**Changes:**
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitFileSelection.ts:23-25` â€” extend `selectParams` to read `file`, `wt`, `commits`.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitFileSelection.ts:27-29` â€” destructure all four arrays.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitFileSelection.ts:42-55` â€” rewrite `toggle`:
  1. compute `next` commitFile list.
  2. `getOrCreateStateId({ file, wt, commits, commitFile: next })`.
  3. `navigate({ to: '/commits', search: { s: id, commits, commitFile: next }, replace: true })`.
- Dep array at L54 includes `file`, `wt`, `commits`.

**Verify:** `pnpm --filter @greppa/web test -- useCommitFileSelection`

### Step 4: commitsRoute.beforeLoad redirect preserves commitFile

**Pattern:** `packages/web/src/router.ts:116,136` â€” cross-section redirects already spread `...state`; only same-section branch needs explicit threading.

**Changes:**
- `packages/web/src/router.ts:158` â€” `throw redirect({ to: '/commits', search: { s: search.s, commits: state.commits, commitFile: state.commitFile } });`

**Verify:** `pnpm --filter @greppa/web test`

### Step 5: Round-trip regression â€” commitFile survives a state-id URL

**Pattern:** `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitFileSelection.test.ts:118-130` "toggle does not modify commits param", but seeded with a `?s=<id>` URL. Assert on the resolved payload from `stateCache.get(newId)`, not the visible URL params.

**Changes:**
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitFileSelection.test.ts` â€” add a new `it` case: `cacheState` an id with `{file:[], wt:[], commits:['aaa'], commitFile:[]}`, render `/commits?s=<id>`, act â†’ `toggle('aaa', 'src/a.ts')`, await, read `router.state.location.search.s` (non-empty string), look up `stateCache.get(newId)`, assert payload `commitFile === ['aaa:src/a.ts']` and `commits === ['aaa']`.

**Verify:** `pnpm --filter @greppa/web test -- useCommitFileSelection`

### Final verification
`cape check`


## Divergence

Step 5 (round-trip regression test) merged with Step 3 during implementation.

The test I added under Step 3 â€” "toggle survives a state-id URL by minting a new state id" in
useCommitFileSelection.test.ts â€” already performs the exact assertion Step 5 described: seed
?s=<id>, toggle, assert new stateCache payload contains both commits and commitFile.

Additional coverage already exists: useSelectionCoordinator.test.ts L112-132 verifies
cross-SHA entries flow through to useCommitFileDiffs (covering task behavior #5's
"same path â†’ two distinct diff entries" intent).

No new Step 5 test is necessary. Verification proceeds with final cape check.

## Outcome

Fix landed across schema, state cache, navigation hooks, and router redirect.

Root cause was a schema-level omission: `StatePayload`/`StateData` lacked `commitFile`. When
any selection wrote via `?s=<id>`, the router's `stringifySearch` short-circuit dropped every
other param, but `stateKey` only hashed `[file, wt, commits]` and `toggle` spread `prev` which
never contained `commitFile`. Adding `commitFile` to the payload shape + threading it through
`useCommitSelection.navigateWithState`, `useCommitFileSelection.toggle` (now via
`getOrCreateStateId`), and the `commitsRoute.beforeLoad` redirect closes the loop.

### Shape changes
- `StateData.fields` (core) and `StatePayload` (web) gained `commitFile: string[]`.
- `stateKey` includes `commitFile` so identical commit selections with different per-commit
  file selections mint distinct ids.
- Server's `StoredState` tracks `commitFile` so persisted state round-trips.
- `useMultiSelect.buildState` zero-inits `commitFile` to satisfy the shape (no behavior change
  â€” path selection never populates commit files).

### Behavior changes
- `useCommitSelection` reads current `commitFile` via `useRouterState` and carries it forward
  into every `navigateWithState` call (plain click, meta+click, shift+range, clear).
- `useCommitFileSelection.toggle` now reads full `{file, wt, commits}` alongside `commitFile`,
  mints a state id via `getOrCreateStateId`, and navigates to `/commits` with
  `{ s, commits, commitFile }`. Works with or without other selections active.
- `commitsRoute.beforeLoad` same-section redirect uses the new
  `buildCommitsRedirectSearch(s, state)` helper to include `commitFile: state.commitFile` so
  reload from a shared URL restores the per-commit file selection.

### Tests added
- `packages/core/src/index.test.ts` â€” `StateData` decodes with/without `commitFile`; rejects
  payloads missing the field.
- `packages/web/src/stateCache.test.ts` (new) â€” `stateKey` uniqueness under `commitFile`
  variation; identical payloads collapse to one id.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitSelection.test.ts` â€” "should carry forward commitFile into
  the new state" covers the buildState â†’ stateCache round-trip.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitFileSelection.test.ts` â€” "toggle survives a state-id URL
  by minting a new state id" covers the primary bug (toggle after `?s=` was active).
- `packages/web/src/router.test.ts` (new) â€” `buildCommitsRedirectSearch` shape test.

### Verification
- Step 1-5 gates passed individually.
- `cape check`: all 563 web + core + server tests pass (vitest âś“, oxlint âś“, oxfmt âś“).
- One pre-existing flaky test (`diffCache.test.ts: propagates getDiff rejections ...`) also
  fails intermittently on the base branch without my changes â€” surfaced, not addressed.

### Surfaced but out of scope
- `useCommitSelection.test.ts:27-33` and `useCommitList.test.ts:28` construct `CommitEntry`
  without `files` (added in commit 0359288); typecheck mismatch pre-existed this task.

### DB note (unrelated to this task's code)
The beads SQLite database was corrupt for the greppa-c4a subtree throughout this session.
`br doctor --repair` and full JSONLâ†’DB rebuild both produced new corruption at Rowid 84+/138+.
Worked around by editing `.beads/issues.jsonl` directly and re-importing. Eventually
`br update` started accepting writes to greppa-c4a.2. Worth an investigation outside this task.

## Revert note

Initial fix (commit 93795ea) caused a render loop / tab hang when interacting
with commits or expanded-commit file rows in the dev server. Reverted in
74a2dfd without a reproduction. The revert restores pre-fix behavior; the
original bug (commitFile dropped when ?s= is active) is still present.

Suspected cause â€” not verified:

- `useCommitSelection` now subscribes to `commitFile` via useRouterState
  and carries it forward in every `navigateWithState`. This adds a second
  subscription that mutates `selectCommit`'s dep array (`commitFileParams`)
  every nav, cascading ref changes through CommitList and into the
  selectedDiffs -> StackedDiffViewer chain.
- Alternatively: `useCommitFileSelection.toggle` now mints a new state id
  via `getOrCreateStateId` on every toggle when any selection is active.
  Each toggle writes a new URL, which re-renders the entire selection
  coordinator, which recomputes entries and diffs. If `useCommitFileDiffs`
  returns a new `{diffs, failedPaths}` object each combine() call, the
  App.tsx `selectedDiffs` useMemo invalidates every render even without
  new data.

Plan for a re-do:

1. Reproduce in the dev server first (chrome-extension was down during the
   original attempt).
2. Narrow the fix â€” only touch `useCommitFileSelection.toggle` and the
   state schema. Do NOT thread commitFile through `useCommitSelection` as a
   first pass. Accept that selecting a new commit clears commitFile
   (matches historical behavior); revisit later if needed.
3. If clearing on commit-select is unacceptable, add carry-forward as a
   second commit with a manual browser test gate BEFORE committing.
4. Verify `commitFileDiffs` returns stable references across renders when
   data hasn't changed (memoize `{diffs, failedPaths}` via useMemo in the
   combine output, or verify useQueries already does this).

Task stays open; verify.json cleared so next attempt starts from step 1.

## Expanded plan v2 (narrow, post-revert)

# Re-attempt greppa-c4a.2: narrow fix for commitFile dropped by ?s= URLs

## Context

When the router encodes URL search into `?s=<id>` (`packages/web/src/router.ts:49-52`), every non-`s` param is dropped from the URL. `StatePayload` (`packages/web/src/stateCache.ts:3-7`) and `StateData` (`packages/core/src/index.ts:44-49`) never included `commitFile`, so toggling a file inside an expanded commit after selecting any commit silently lost the write. The diff viewer never received the entry. This defeats the epic success criterion "Same path in two different commits renders as two independent diff entries."

The first fix (commit `93795ea`) was reverted in `74a2dfd` because the dev tab hung during interaction â€” unreproducible in tests, probably from the combined effect of also threading `commitFile` through `useCommitSelection` (its `selectCommit` useCallback got `commitFileParams` as a dep, re-memoizing on every navigation and cascading prop changes through `CommitList`).

This attempt is narrower: schema + `useCommitFileSelection.toggle` + redirect only. No changes to `useCommitSelection`. Accept that selecting a new commit will reset `commitFile` â€” this matches pre-fix behavior and is NOT a success criterion on the task.

## Approach

Four files change behavior; two packages get new test coverage. No changes to `useCommitSelection` or `useMultiSelect` beyond zero-initing the new field.

**Validation gates:** automated tests at every step, plus a manual browser check in the dev server after the final step (before commit) â€” clicking a commit, then a file inside it, and confirming (a) the file renders in the diff viewer, (b) the tab does not hang, (c) reload preserves the selection.

## Critical files

- `packages/core/src/index.ts:44-49` â€” `StateData` struct, add `commitFile: Schema.Array(Schema.String)`.
- `packages/web/src/stateCache.ts:3-7,17-18` â€” `StatePayload` adds `commitFile: string[]`; `stateKey` includes it.
- `packages/server/src/Http.ts:29-33,272` â€” `StoredState` adds `commitFile: string[]`; save handler threads it.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitFileSelection.ts:23-55` â€” `toggle` branches: plain navigate when no selection, `getOrCreateStateId` when any of file/wt/commits is active.
- `packages/web/src/router.ts:142-160` â€” `commitsRoute.beforeLoad` same-section redirect includes `commitFile`.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitSelection.ts:10-14` â€” `buildState` zero-inits `commitFile: []` (shape only; NO threading).
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useMultiSelect.ts:28-31` â€” `buildState` branches zero-init `commitFile: []` (shape only).
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitSelection.test.ts:50` â€” `commitsUrl` helper literal gets `commitFile: []`.

## Existing patterns to reuse

- `packages/web/src/hooks/useMultiSelect.ts:68-78` â€” `navigateWithState` using `getOrCreateStateId` + `navigate`. Template for the toggle rewrite when any selection is active.
- `packages/web/src/stateCache.ts:38-47` `getOrCreateStateId()` â€” returns an existing id for matching payload (via `findExistingId`), so repeated toggles with the same result don't flood `postState`.
- `packages/web/src/toStringArray.ts` â€” already used for search param coercion.
- `cacheState(id, state)` from `stateCache.ts` â€” for seeding a `?s=<id>` URL in round-trip tests.

## Steps (TDD, verify after each)

1. **Extend schema shape.** Add `commitFile: Schema.Array(Schema.String)` to `StateData`; `commitFile: string[]` to `StatePayload` and `StoredState`; include `commitFile` in `stateKey`; update `saveState` handler to thread `commitFile`; zero-init `commitFile: []` in both `useCommitSelection.buildState` and `useMultiSelect.buildState`; update `commitsUrl` helper in `useCommitSelection.test.ts`. Add schema decode tests in `packages/core/src/index.test.ts`. Add `packages/web/src/stateCache.test.ts` with a `getOrCreateStateId` uniqueness test (two payloads differing only in `commitFile` get distinct ids). **Verify:** `node_modules/.bin/vp test packages/core packages/web packages/server`.

2. **Rewrite `useCommitFileSelection.toggle`.** Extend selector to read `file`, `wt`, `commits`, `commitFile`. Toggle:
   - If `file.length === 0 && wt.length === 0 && commits.length === 0`: plain navigate to `/commits` with `{s: '', commits: [], commitFile: next}` (matches pre-fix plain-param behavior).
   - Else: `getOrCreateStateId({file, wt, commits, commitFile: next})`, navigate with `{s: id, commits, commitFile: next}`.
   Add two tests in `useCommitFileSelection.test.ts`: (a) "toggle with no other selection writes plain commitFile params, not a state id"; (b) "toggle survives a state-id URL by minting a new state id" that seeds `cacheState` with `{file:['src/x.ts'], wt:['src/y.ts'], commits:['aaa'], commitFile:[]}` and asserts `file`/`wt`/`commits` all carry forward in the new payload. **Verify:** `node_modules/.bin/vp test packages/web -- useCommitFileSelection`.

3. **Fix the `/commits` same-section redirect.** Extract `buildCommitsRedirectSearch(s, state)` in `router.ts` returning `{s, commits: state.commits, commitFile: state.commitFile}`. Wire into `commitsRoute.beforeLoad` at line 158. Export the helper for testing. Add `packages/web/src/router.test.ts` asserting the helper's output shape. **Verify:** `node_modules/.bin/vp test packages/web`.

4. **Manual browser validation.** Before committing: start the dev servers (`pnpm --filter @greppa/server run dev & pnpm --filter @greppa/web run dev`). In the browser tab:
   - Click a commit to select it. Confirm no hang. Check DevTools for unbounded renders.
   - Expand the commit (chevron). Click a file row. Confirm the diff appears in the viewer and the URL updates.
   - Reload the tab. Confirm the file stays selected and the diff still renders.
   - Click a different commit. Note: `commitFile` clears (expected with narrow fix). Confirm no hang.
   Only if all four pass: proceed to `cape check` then `cape:commit`.

## Out of scope (deferred)

- Threading `commitFile` through `useCommitSelection.navigateWithState` so selecting a new commit carries `commitFile` forward. This was the suspected cause of the render loop. Not required by any success criterion on greppa-c4a.2. File as a follow-up task only if user-visible feedback demands it.
- Hunk-level selection, auto-grouping, reviewed-state â€” all explicitly deferred by the epic.

## Verification

- **Automated:** `cape check` passes (vitest, oxlint, oxfmt).
- **Manual:** the browser checks above. If the tab hangs at any step, STOP, do not commit, and bisect toggle vs redirect by partially reverting.
- **Success criteria (task):**
  - Clicking a file after selecting commits writes to URL-encoded state and surfaces in the diff viewer (Step 2 test + manual).
  - Reload preserves `commitFile` selection (Step 3 redirect + manual reload).
  - Existing single-file tests unchanged (Step 2 keeps plain-param path for no-selection).
  - All tests passing (`cape check`).
  - Pre-commit hooks passing (committed via `cape commit`).

## Outcome (v2 narrow)

Re-attempted after the v1 revert. The narrow plan landed cleanly.

### Root cause of prior hang
v1 threaded `commitFile` through `useCommitSelection.navigateWithState`, adding
`commitFileParams` as a `selectCommit` useCallback dep. That change alone was
not the hang trigger â€” browser-traced the real issue: RAC `Tree.Collection`
memoizes its items-render output by items reference. Since `commits` is stable
across renders (react-query), child `Tree.Item` onPointerDown handlers are NOT
rebound when the parent re-renders. The first-render `toggle` closure (with
empty `commits`) stays bound to the DOM. When the user clicks a file after
selecting a commit, toggle sees stale `commits: []` and takes the empty-branch,
writing plain `?commitFile=` and dropping the state-id.

v1 likely hung because it also dropped the `(prev) => ...` navigate form, so
every toggle call fired a nav to `/commits?s=<newid>` even when the closure
made the new state identical to the old â€” triggering a tight re-render loop
through CommitList â†’ selectedDiffs â†’ StackedDiffViewer.

### v2 fix (this attempt)
Narrow scope + stable-callback pattern. `useCommitFileSelection.toggle` reads
current state via a `stateRef` synced on each render, so the callback's closure
is stable (only deps: `navigate`) but reads fresh values at call time. No
changes to `useCommitSelection`.

### Shape changes
- `StateData` (core), `StatePayload` (web), `StoredState` (server) gain
  `commitFile: string[]`.
- `stateKey` includes `commitFile` â€” distinct ids per commit-file selection.
- Server `saveState` threads `commitFile`.
- `useCommitSelection.buildState` and `useMultiSelect.buildState` zero-init
  `commitFile: []` (shape only).

### Behavior changes
- `useCommitFileSelection.toggle`:
  - When file/wt/commits all empty: plain navigate `?commitFile=`.
  - Else: mint state id via `getOrCreateStateId({file, wt, commits, commitFile: next})`, navigate `{s, commits, commitFile}`.
- `commitsRoute.beforeLoad` uses new `buildCommitsRedirectSearch(s, state)`
  helper so same-section reload preserves commitFile.

### Tests
- `packages/core/src/index.test.ts` â€” `StateData` decode tests.
- `packages/web/src/stateCache.test.ts` (new) â€” `stateKey` uniqueness under `commitFile`.
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitFileSelection.test.ts` â€” plain-param path and state-id round-trip carrying file/wt/commits forward.
- `packages/web/src/router.test.ts` (new) â€” redirect-target shape.

### Browser validation (against narrow-plan Step 4)
1. Click commit: `?s=<id>` minted, no hang.
2. Expand commit + click file: `?s=<new-id>` minted, state payload has both
   `commits` and `commitFile`, diff viewer shows 13 entries (12 commit files +
   1 commitFile entry for the same path).
3. Reload: router state preserves `commits` AND `commitFile` via the
   `buildCommitsRedirectSearch` redirect.
4. Click a different commit: new state id, `commitFile` cleared to `[]`,
   no hang. Matches narrow-fix expected behavior.

### Known out-of-scope (per plan)
- Commit-select does not carry forward `commitFile`. Not a task success
  criterion.
- Server dev requires manual restart on `@greppa/core` schema changes (tsx
  doesn't HMR).

title: Task 3: Visible selected-state on commit file rows
description:
## Goal
Child file rows in an expanded commit currently render with no visual difference when they are the active `commitFile` entry. Clicking a file already updates the URL and diff viewer, but the row itself gives no feedback â€” users believe the click did nothing. Wire `useCommitFileSelection.isSelected` into the child `Tree.Item` and style the selected state.

## Behaviors
One TDD cycle per item.

1. `CommitList` accepts `selectedCommitFiles: Set<string>` (encoded `"sha:path"` strings) as a prop; passes `aria-selected` to each child Tree.Item.
2. Selected child rows receive a visible CSS treatment (background tint + text color), mirroring the existing committed `FileTree` selected-row pattern in `packages/web/src/components/FileTree/FileTree.module.css`.
3. `App.tsx` threads `new Set(commitFileSelection.entries.map((e) => \`${e.sha}:${e.path}\`))` into CommitList via `FileTreePanel`.
4. Component test: render with `selectedCommitFiles` containing one entry â†’ matching child row has `aria-selected="true"`, other rows have `aria-selected="false"`.

## Success criteria
- [ ] Clicking a file in an expanded commit visually marks that row as selected.
- [ ] Clicking the same file again deselects it and removes the visual state.
- [ ] The selected indicator is distinct from the hover and focus states.
- [ ] Tests passing; pre-commit hooks passing.

## Dependencies
Must land after greppa-c4a.2 â€” the selected state depends on `commitFile` surviving selection transitions.

## References
- packages/web/src/components/CommitList/CommitList.tsx â€” child `Tree.Item` render.
- packages/web/src/components/FileTree/FileTree.module.css â€” existing selected-row CSS pattern to mirror.
- packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitFileSelection.ts â€” `entries`, `isSelected`.
- packages/web/src/App.tsx â€” wiring site.
design: test

title: Task 4: Visual polish pass on commit list rows
description:
## Goal
Three small visual issues on the Commits section make it feel unfinished:
1. Time ("9h ago", "1d ago") wraps to two lines because the meta column is too narrow â€” every commit row is ~2x tall.
2. Child file rows render as bare text with no file-type icon, making them indistinguishable from free-form labels.
3. No visible indent guide for level-2 (child) rows under an expanded commit, so the tree structure is not obvious.

Pure CSS + small JSX in `CommitList.tsx`; no behavior change, no new tests beyond presence checks.

## Behaviors
Bundle into one verification loop (visual).

1. Meta column rendered inline, not stacked. `abbrevSha` + relative time fit on one line at default `file-tree` panel width (240px). Component test: commit row height stays within single-line bounds via `getBoundingClientRect`.
2. Child file rows render a file-type icon (reuse whatever icon `FileTree` child files use, e.g. `IconFile`). Component test: expanded commit's child row contains an `svg` element.
3. Tree indent guide visible on level-2 rows (CSS on `[aria-level="2"]` via `Tree.Indent`). Visual verification only â€” no automated test for pixel-level guide rendering.

## Success criteria
- [ ] Commit row meta ("9h ago", "aaa111") fits on one line at 240px sidebar width.
- [ ] Child file rows display a file icon.
- [ ] Indent guide visible under expanded commits.
- [ ] All tests passing; pre-commit hooks passing.

## Dependencies
Independent of greppa-c4a.2 and .3 â€” ordering is flexible.

## References
- packages/web/src/components/CommitList/CommitList.tsx and CommitList.module.css â€” primary edits.
- packages/web/src/components/FileTree/FileTree.tsx â€” file icon pattern to mirror.
- packages/ui/src/components/Tree/Tree.tsx â€” `Tree.Indent` primitive.
design: ## Expanded plan (expand-task) ### Investigation findings **Files to modify:** - `packages/web/src/components/CommitList/CommitList.tsx` (1â€“143) â€” JSX for the RAC Tree rendering commit rows + child file rows. Needs a file icon on child rows. - `packages/web/src/components/CommitList/CommitList.module.css` (1â€“72) â€” styles for `.subject`, `.meta`, `.hash`, `.time`. `.item`/`.list` blocks are dead (no `className` reference in the tsx); leave alone or delete if clearly dead. The meta-wrap and indent-guide fixes live here. **Patterns to follow:** - `packages/web/src/components/FileTree/FileTree.tsx:90-95` â€” the `FileIcon` usage pattern. Import `FileIcon` from `@greppa/ui` (index.ts:7), pass `name={path.split('/').pop()}` and `baseUrl='/material-icons'`. FileIcon renders an `` (see `packages/ui/src/components/FileIcon/FileIcon.tsx:27`), not an `` via `@greppa/ui`'s `FileIcon`, which is the established pattern in this codebase. The new test asserts on `img`, matching `FileTree.test.tsx:122-128`. - The shared Tree primitive (`packages/ui/src/components/Tree/Tree.module.css:22-38`) already paints a per-level guide via `::before` + `--tree-item-level`, but it's subtle at 0.4 opacity and, at level 2, only a single hairline gradient stop. The task's feedback ("no visible indent guide") reflects that subtlety at 240px width. A locally-scoped `::after` rule keeps the primitive unchanged (so FileTree styling is preserved) and produces a visible 0.6-opacity guide on CommitList children only. - Step 1's CSS-only behavior is not observable in happy-dom (no layout engine), so it was implemented without a new failing test; the existing 16-test suite guards against structural regressions. Step 2's icon presence is testable (RED/GREEN cycle recorded). Step 3 is visual-only per task success criteria. ### Verification - `cape check`: vitest 575 pass / 1 skipped, oxlint pass, oxfmt pass. - `pnpm run typecheck`: clean. - Manual in-browser check deferred to HITL checkpoint (three small, easily-reasoned CSS/JSX changes; no behavior change).

title: Tree styling: row height, selection bar, indent guides
description:
## Goal

Restyle the generic Tree component (packages/ui) to match editor-quality density and selection behavior: 22px rows, full-width selection bar, and indent guides.

## Behaviors

- Tree rows are 22px tall (min-height: 22px, tight padding)
- Selected items use a full-width background fill with no border-radius â€” background spans the entire sidebar width
- Focused items use a subtle background instead of an outline
- Hovered items keep current behavior (bg-surface-hover)
- Indent guides render as subtle vertical lines at each nesting depth via ::before pseudo-element
- Indentation uses --tree-item-level CSS variable on the chevron's padding-left, not on the row
- A new Tree.Indent sub-component provides a spacer for leaf items (no chevron) to align with folder rows
- --gr-tree-indent custom property (20px) controls indent step size

## Success criteria

- [ ] .item has min-height: 22px with reduced vertical padding
- [ ] .item has no border-radius
- [ ] .item[data-focused] uses background instead of outline
- [ ] .item::before draws vertical guide lines using repeating-linear-gradient
- [ ] .chevron padding-left uses calc((var(--tree-item-level, 1) - 1) * var(--gr-tree-indent))
- [ ] Tree.Indent renders a span sized by --tree-item-level for leaf item alignment
- [ ] Existing Tree.test.tsx tests pass
- [ ] New tests verify indent and styling classes
- [ ] Tree.preview.tsx visually confirms all changes

title: Editor-quality file tree sidebar
description: ## Context The file tree sidebar is functional but visually basic compared to VSCode/JetBrains. It lacks file icons, indent guides, compact folders, and uses an outline-based selection instead of a full-width background fill. This epic brings it to editor-quality through 7 visual improvements in 3 phases. ## Requirements - [ ] Tree rows use 22px height matching VSCode density - [ ] Selected/focused items use full-width background fill instead of outline â€” no border-radius, background spans edge-to-edge - [ ] Indent guides render as subtle vertical lines at each nesting depth using --tree-item-level CSS variable - [ ] Indentation lives on the chevron/spacer (inner element), never on the TreeItem row itself - [ ] File type icons render next to each filename via vscode-material-icons (910 icons, rendering) - [ ] Folder icons show open/closed state matching expand/collapse - [ ] Leaf items align with folder items via an Indent spacer where the chevron would be - [ ] Filename text color reflects change type: green (added), red (deleted), yellow (modified), muted (renamed) - [ ] Single-child directory chains flatten into one row (compact folders), using deepest dir path as React Aria id - [ ] Directories inherit the highest-priority changeType from descendants (added > deleted > modified > renamed) - [ ] Directory rows show colored text but no letter badge ## Success criteria 1. All 7 visual improvements render correctly in both Catppuccin Latte and Mocha themes 2. Existing Tree and FileTree tests pass without modification (new tests added alongside) 3. File icons load via tags from static assets â€” no dangerouslySetInnerHTML 4. Compact folders produce correct tree shape: `src/events` as one row when `events` has a single child directory 5. Directory status propagation colors parent folders based on child file statuses 6. pnpm test, pnpm typecheck, pnpm lint all pass ## Anti-patterns - Do NOT put level-based padding on the TreeItem row â€” only on inner elements (chevron, spacer). This breaks full-width selection highlight. - Do NOT use dangerouslySetInnerHTML for icons â€” use with vscode-material-icons URL API - Do NOT compute compact folders or status propagation at render time â€” do it in the data layer (buildFileTree) - Do NOT show letter badges (M, A, D, R) on directory rows â€” only colored text - Do NOT add keyboard shortcuts in this epic â€” UI visual quality only - Do NOT modify the Tree UI component's public API surface beyond adding Indent sub-component ## Approach Three phases, each a separate PR: **Phase 1 â€” Tree styling (CSS-only):** Tighter row height, full-width selection bar, indent guides + chevron indentation. All in packages/ui Tree component. **Phase 2 â€” File icons + colors:** New FileIcon component in packages/ui using vscode-material-icons. Integrate into FileTree with TreeItemContent render function for isExpanded access. Add change type color classes to filename text. **Phase 3 â€” Data transforms:** compactTree() and propagateChangeType() functions in useFileList.ts. Pure data-layer transforms called inside buildFileTree after sortNodes. ## Design decisions | Decision | Choice | Rationale | |---|---|---| | Icon library | vscode-material-icons | Folder open/closed support, 910 icons, rendering vs dangerouslySetInnerHTML | | Indentation | Chevron padding-left | React Aria pattern â€” row stays full-width for selection highlight | | Compact folder identity | Deepest dir path | Stable React Aria id for selection/expansion | | Status propagation | Data layer (buildFileTree) | Pure function, testable, no render-time computation | | Row height | 22px (VSCode standard) | Editor-density familiarity | | Deleted propagation | Yes (unlike VSCode) | Code review tool â€” deleted files matter for review visibility |

title: Serve built web app from greppa serve
description:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- [ ] `greppa serve` serves both API endpoints and the built web frontend on a single port
- [ ] Web assets are bundled into the CLI dist at build time â€” no runtime path guessing
- [ ] SPA fallback: non-API routes serve `index.html` for client-side routing
- [ ] API routes (`/api/*`) continue working unchanged
- [ ] Build order enforced: web builds before CLI
- [ ] `greppa serve` opens to a working web app in the browser at `http://localhost:4400`

## Success criteria

- [ ] `pnpm run build` from root builds web then CLI, with web dist copied into CLI dist
- [ ] `greppa serve` starts, browser at `http://localhost:4400/` loads the web app
- [ ] `GET /api/health` returns `{ status: "ok" }` (API still works)
- [ ] `GET /api/refs` returns refs (API still works)
- [ ] Navigating to `/file/some/path` in browser works (SPA fallback serves index.html)
- [ ] All tests passing, pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- NO runtime path resolution to find web assets outside the CLI dist (reason: fragile, breaks if directory structure changes)
- NO separate static file server or second port (reason: must be single command, single port)
- NO new dependencies â€” use Effect's built-in `HttpStaticServer` from `effect/unstable/http/HttpStaticServer`
- NO changes to the dev flow â€” `pnpm dev` continues using Caddy + Vite dev server

## Durable decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Asset location | Bundled in CLI dist at build time | Fail-proof, no runtime path guessing |
| Static server | `HttpStaticServer.layer({ root, spa: true })` | Built into Effect, composes with existing HttpRouter |
| Build order | `@greppa/web` as devDependency of `@greppa/cli` | `vp run -r build` respects dependency order |
| Copy mechanism | `cp -r ../web/dist dist/web` in CLI build script | Boring, explicit, zero dependencies |
| Path resolution | `resolve(import.meta.dirname, 'web')` | Always correct since assets ship alongside JS |

## Approach

Three changes:

1. **CLI build** â€” update `packages/cli/package.json`: add `@greppa/web` devDependency, change build script to `"vp pack && cp -r ../web/dist dist/web"`
2. **Server** â€” update `packages/server/src/Http.ts`: `makeHttpLayer` gains `webDistPath` parameter, merges `HttpStaticServer.layer({ root: webDistPath, spa: true })` with `ApiRoutes` inside `HttpRouter.serve`
3. **CLI serve** â€” update `packages/cli/src/commands/serve.ts`: resolve `webDistPath` via `import.meta.dirname` and pass to `makeHttpLayer`

## Architecture

- `HttpStaticServer.layer` provides routes to the same `HttpRouter` as `ApiRoutes` â€” they compose via the layer system
- API routes match first (explicit paths), static files are the fallback
- `spa: true` serves `index.html` for paths that don't match a file â€” enables client-side routing
- `FileSystem`, `Path`, `HttpPlatform` dependencies are already provided by `NodeHttpServer.layer`

title: Bundle web assets and serve from single port
description:
## Goal

Make `greppa serve` serve the built web frontend alongside the API on a single port using Effect's `HttpStaticServer`.

## Behaviors

1. `packages/cli/package.json` gains `@greppa/web` as devDependency. Build script changes to `"vp pack && cp -r ../web/dist dist/web"`.
2. `packages/server/src/Http.ts`: `makeHttpLayer` gains a `webDistPath: string` parameter. Inside, `HttpStaticServer.layer({ root: webDistPath, spa: true })` is merged with `ApiRoutes` so the static file layer provides routes to the same `HttpRouter`.
3. `packages/cli/src/commands/serve.ts`: resolves `webDistPath` as `resolve(import.meta.dirname, 'web')` and passes to `makeHttpLayer`.
4. `packages/server/src/Http.integration.test.ts`: update `makeHttpLayer` call or `TestAppLayer` to accommodate the new parameter.

## References

- packages/server/src/Http.ts:126-138 â€” `ApiRoutes` and `makeHttpLayer`
- packages/cli/src/commands/serve.ts:45-52 â€” serve command handler calling `makeHttpLayer`
- packages/cli/package.json â€” build script and dependencies
- effect/unstable/http/HttpStaticServer â€” `layer({ root, spa, prefix })` API
- packages/web/dist/ â€” built web assets (index.html, assets/, material-icons/)

## Success criteria

- [ ] `pnpm run build` from root builds web then CLI, CLI dist contains `web/` subdirectory with index.html
- [ ] `greppa serve` starts, `http://localhost:4400/` serves the web app
- [ ] `GET /api/health` still returns `{ status: "ok" }`
- [ ] Browser navigation to `/file/some/path` serves index.html (SPA fallback)
- [ ] All tests passing, pre-commit hooks passing

title: Create Shiki Web Worker with message protocol
description:
## Goal

Create a dedicated Web Worker that owns the Shiki highlighter instance and responds to highlight requests via postMessage. Define the message protocol types shared between main thread and worker.

## Behaviors

1. Worker initializes Shiki highlighter lazily on first highlight request
2. Worker loads language grammars on demand per request, not all at init
3. Worker receives lines with keys and returns ThemedToken arrays keyed by line key
4. Worker caches highlighted results by {filePath, lineKey} to skip re-work
5. Worker falls back to plaintext tokens for unknown languages
6. Shared TypeScript types define the request/response message protocol

## Success criteria

- [ ] Worker file uses Vite's native `new Worker(new URL(...), { type: 'module' })` pattern
- [ ] Message protocol types are importable from both worker and main thread code
- [ ] Language grammars are loaded lazily, not bundled at init
- [ ] Cache prevents re-highlighting the same line for the same file
- [ ] Unknown languages produce plain text tokens instead of errors
- [ ] No integration with DiffViewer yet (next task)
design:
## Expanded plan

### New files

1. `packages/web/src/workers/highlightProtocol.ts` â€” Shared message types
2. `packages/web/src/workers/highlightEngine.ts` â€” Core highlighting logic (testable without worker)
3. `packages/web/src/workers/highlightEngine.test.ts` â€” Tests for engine
4. `packages/web/src/workers/highlightWorker.ts` â€” Thin worker entry point wiring postMessage to engine

### Message protocol (highlightProtocol.ts)

- HighlightRequest: { type, filePath, language, theme, lines: {key, content}[] }
- HighlightResponse: { type, filePath, tokens: Record<string, {content, color?}[]> }
- WorkerRequest = HighlightRequest; WorkerResponse = HighlightResponse

### Engine logic (highlightEngine.ts)

Exports handleHighlightRequest(request): Promise<HighlightResponse>

State: lazy highlighterPromise, loadedLanguages Set, cache Map<filePath:lineKey, token[]>

Flow:
1. Init highlighter lazily on first call (themes only, no langs)
2. Load language on demand via highlighter.loadLanguage(). On error, fall back to plaintext
3. Filter out cached lines. Join uncached lines, call codeToTokens once (preserves grammar state). Split back by index. Serialize ThemedToken to {content, color}. Store in cache
4. Return response with all tokens (cached + fresh)

### Worker entry (highlightWorker.ts)

Thin: imports handleHighlightRequest, wires self.onmessage to it, posts response back

### TDD cycles

1. Protocol types compile â€” import and use types in test
2. Returns tokens for lines â€” mock shiki, 2 lines in, token map with both keys out
3. Lazy init â€” createHighlighter not called until first request
4. Language on demand â€” request with python triggers loadLanguage
5. Cache hit â€” same request twice, codeToTokens called once
6. Unknown language fallback â€” fakeLang rejected, plaintext tokens returned

title: URL-driven routing for review state
description:
## Context

All selection state (multi-select files, commit ranges, scroll target) lives in React useState hooks with no URL representation. Refreshing the page or sharing a link loses multi-select and commit range state. The current router has three splat routes (`/`, `/file/$`, `/wt/$`) that only track single file selection.

## Requirements

- [ ] Single `/review` route with validated search params replaces all existing routes
- [ ] `/` redirects to `/review`
- [ ] `file` search param: array of committed file paths (single or multi-select)
- [ ] `wt` search param: array of worktree file paths (single or multi-select)
- [ ] `commits` search param: array of commit SHAs (discrete cmd+click) or single `sha1..sha2` (continuous shift+click range)
- [ ] `file`, `wt`, and `commits` are mutually exclusive â€” switching source clears the other params
- [ ] `*` sentinel for select-all (`?file=*`, `?wt=*`) â€” resolves dynamically against current ref pair
- [ ] Hash fragment encodes scroll target file within stacked diffs (`#src/App.tsx`)
- [ ] Hash with line anchor (`#src/App.tsx:L42`) scrolls to specific line
- [ ] File tree expanded section derived from which param is present (no extra URL param)
- [ ] pushState for new intents (first file click, source switch, first commit click)
- [ ] replaceState for refinements (cmd+click toggle, shift+click range, select-all, minimap click)
- [ ] Scrolling through stacked diffs does NOT update the URL
- [ ] Minimap clicks update hash via replaceState
- [ ] Page load with hash fragment triggers scrollToFile after diffs render
- [ ] Old routes (`/file/$`, `/wt/$`) redirect to `/review?file=...` / `/review?wt=...`
- [ ] All existing selection behavior (single click, cmd+click, shift+click, directory click, section header click) preserved

## Approach

Four phases:

1. **Router + search param schema** â€” Replace `router.ts` with single `/review` route using `validateSearch`. Install `@tanstack/zod-adapter` and `zod`. Add redirect routes for `/`, `/file/$`, `/wt/$`. No behavior changes yet â€” just wire the route, existing state hooks still work.

2. **File selection via URL** â€” Replace `useFileSelection`'s navigate-to-splat approach with search param updates. Wire `useMultiSelect` and `useFileSelectionHandlers` to read/write `file`/`wt` search params. Single click = pushState, modifier clicks = replaceState. Source switching clears other params. `*` sentinel for select-all.

3. **Commit selection via URL** â€” Wire `useCommitSelection` to read/write `commits` search param. Discrete SHAs for cmd+click, `sha1..sha2` for shift+click range. Commit selection clears `file`/`wt` params and vice versa.

4. **Scroll target via hash** â€” Add hash fragment management for scroll-to-file. Minimap clicks and file tree clicks in multi-select update hash via replaceState. Page load reads hash and calls `scrollToFile` after render. Line anchor support (`#path:L42`).

### Key design decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| URL shape | Search params on single route | Multi-select needs arrays; path segments can't encode multiple files unambiguously |
| Validation | Zod via @tanstack/zod-adapter | Type-safe search params, fallback defaults, array handling |
| History model | pushState = new intent, replaceState = refinement | Keeps back stack clean â€” ~3-5 entries per session, not 30+ |
| Scroll target | Hash fragment, not search param | Hash changes don't trigger router navigation or re-renders |
| Select-all | `*` sentinel, dynamic resolution | Avoids URL length blowup; resolves at load time against current diff |
| Panel section | Derived from params | No extra URL state; section follows selection source |

## Anti-patterns

- Do NOT create bidirectional URL â†” scroll sync â€” URL drives scroll, never the reverse
- Do NOT push history on every cmd+click toggle â€” floods back stack
- Do NOT encode scroll pixel offset in URL â€” use logical file paths
- Do NOT allow `file` + `wt` + `commits` to coexist in the same URL
- Do NOT read search params and also maintain parallel useState for the same data â€” single source of truth is the URL

## Success criteria

1. Refreshing the page preserves full selection state (multi-select files, commit ranges)
2. Sharing a URL reproduces the exact view (correct files selected, correct section expanded, scrolled to correct file)
3. Browser back/forward navigates between intents (not between individual multi-select toggles)
4. Old bookmarked URLs (`/file/...`, `/wt/...`) redirect correctly to new scheme
5. No infinite render loops from URL â†” state sync
6. All existing tests pass (with adaptation for the new routing)
design: ## docs/ROUTING.md

title: Replace router with single /review route and search param schema
description:
## Goal

Set up the new `/review` route with validated search params and redirects from old routes. No selection behavior changes â€” just the routing foundation.

## Behaviors

- App renders at `/review` with the same UI as before
- `/` redirects to `/review`
- `/file/{path}` redirects to `/review?file={path}`
- `/wt/{path}` redirects to `/review?wt={path}`
- Single file click navigates to `/review?file={path}` (committed) or `/review?wt={path}` (worktree)
- Search params validated via Zod â€” invalid params fall back to empty arrays
- `useFileSelection` reads file/wt from search params instead of splat route matches

## Success criteria

- [ ] App loads at `/review` with identical UI
- [ ] All three redirect routes work (`/`, `/file/$`, `/wt/$`)
- [ ] `router.ts` has single `/review` route with `validateSearch`
- [ ] `useFileSelection` reads from search params, not `useMatch`
- [ ] All existing tests pass (with route path updates)

title: Wire file selection to URL search params
description:
## Goal

Make URL search params the single source of truth for file selection. Replace useMultiSelect's useState with URL-backed state. All selection actions (click, meta+click, shift+click, directory click, select-all) write to file/wt search params.

## Behaviors

- Plain click: pushState with `file=[path]` or `wt=[path]` (clears other params)
- Meta+click (toggle): replaceState toggling the path in the array
- Shift+click (range): replaceState with range of paths
- Directory click: replaceState with `selectAll` of descendant paths
- Select-all: replaceState with `file=*` or `wt=*` sentinel
- Source switch: clears other source params
- `*` sentinel resolves dynamically against current file list
- Reading back: useMultiSelect derives selectedPaths/activeSource from URL params
- anchorPath stays as local useState (ephemeral, not in URL)

## Success criteria

- [ ] useMultiSelect reads from URL, writes via navigate
- [ ] Plain click = pushState, modifier clicks = replaceState
- [ ] `*` sentinel for select-all resolves at read time
- [ ] Source switching clears other params
- [ ] useFileSelection simplified (no longer owns select/deselect navigation)
- [ ] All existing tests pass (adapted for URL-backed behavior)
- [ ] No parallel useState for selection data

title: Wire scroll target to URL hash fragment
description:
## Goal

Wire URL hash fragments as the scroll target for stacked diffs. File tree clicks in multi-select mode update the hash via replaceState. Page load with a hash fragment triggers scrollToFile after diffs render. Line anchor support (`#path:L42`).

## Behaviors

- File tree click in multi-select mode: replaceState with `#filepath` (single-select already navigates, no hash needed)
- Page load with `#filepath`: call `StackedDiffViewer.scrollToFile(path)` after diffs render
- Page load with `#filepath:L42`: scroll to file, then scroll to line 42
- Scrolling through stacked diffs does NOT update the hash (one-directional: URL drives scroll)
- Hash managed outside TanStack Router via `window.history.replaceState`

## Success criteria

- [ ] Page load with `#filepath` scrolls to the correct file after diffs render
- [ ] Page load with `#filepath:L42` scrolls to the correct line
- [ ] File tree click in multi-select updates hash via replaceState
- [ ] Scrolling through diffs does NOT write to the hash
- [ ] Hash uses `window.history.replaceState`, not router navigate
- [ ] All existing tests pass
design:
## Expanded plan

## Implementation plan

### 1. `parseHash` utility (new file)
`packages/web/src/hooks/parseHash.ts`
- Input: hash string (`#src/App.tsx`, `#src/App.tsx:L42`, empty)
- Output: `{ path: string; line: number | null } | null`
- Pure function, strip leading `#`, split on `:L`

### 2. Extend `StackedDiffViewerHandle.scrollToLine`
`packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/StackedDiffViewer.tsx`
- Add `scrollToLine: (path: string, line: number) => void` to handle interface (L23-25)
- Implementation: find file-header index, then scan forward for diff-row where `row.right?.lineNumber === line` (or `row.left?.lineNumber`), call `scrollToIndex`
- Wire into `useImperativeHandle` (L102-111)

### 3. `useHashScroll` hook (new file)
`packages/web/src/hooks/useHashScroll.ts`
- Args: `ref: RefObject<StackedDiffViewerHandle>`, `diffs: DiffFile[]`
- On mount, read `window.location.hash`, parse with `parseHash`
- When diffs become non-empty AND hash is present, call `ref.current.scrollToFile(path)` or `ref.current.scrollToLine(path, line)`
- Use a `hasScrolled` ref to fire only once

### 4. Wire `useHashScroll` in App.tsx
`packages/web/src/App.tsx`
- After L170: call `useHashScroll(stackedDiffRef, selectedDiffs)`

### 5. Hash writing from file tree clicks
`packages/web/src/hooks/useFileSelectionHandlers.ts`
- After modifier clicks (meta, shift, directory), call:
  `window.history.replaceState(null, '', `${window.location.pathname}${window.location.search}#${path}`)`
- Only when already in multi-select (multiSelect.isMultiSelect) or when the action creates multi-select

### TDD order
1. parseHash: test â†’ implement
2. scrollToLine: test â†’ implement
3. useHashScroll: test â†’ implement
4. App.tsx wiring (integration)
5. Hash writing in useFileSelectionHandlers: test â†’ implement

title: Editor shell polish
description:
## Context

Greppa's UI should feel like a code editor, not a web app. The current shell has multiple headers (app header, diff header, detail panel tabs), 1px borders everywhere, and a status bar that only shows review progress. This epic consolidates the shell into a cohesive editor-like experience.

## Requirements

1. **Single unified header** â€” merge the app header (logo, theme picker) and diff file header (file path, change badge, branch indicator) into one header bar. The detail panel tab bar remains as panel-internal navigation.
2. **Tonal separation over borders** â€” remove all 1px solid borders (header, status bar, detail panel tabs/threads/info rows, diff divider, panel separators). Use background shade differences (--gr-color-bg-subtle vs --gr-color-bg-surface vs --gr-color-bg-default) to distinguish regions.
3. **Editor-style status bar** â€” left side: review progress, branch. Right side: file-local metadata (language, encoding, line/col position). Requires adding file metadata to StatusBar props.
4. **Document no-border principle in VISION.md** â€” add a design principle about preferring tonal layering over structural borders.

## Anti-patterns

- Do not add new borders to replace removed ones.
- Do not introduce Tailwind â€” the project uses CSS modules with --gr-* custom properties.
- Do not change component behavior or data flow â€” this is purely visual/structural.

## Success criteria

- Zero 1px solid borders in the app shell (header, status bar, panel separators). Exception: the active tab indicator in DetailPanel may keep its bottom accent.
- One header bar visible at the top of the app.
- StatusBar shows file metadata on the right when a file is selected.
- All panels visually distinct through background shade differences alone.
- VISION.md contains the no-border design principle.
- All existing tests pass.

title: Add no-border design principle to VISION.md
description:
## Goal

Add a design principle to docs/VISION.md under "Design principles" stating that the UI prefers tonal layering (background shade shifts) over structural borders (1px solid lines) to separate regions.

## Why

Sets the ground rule before the visual changes in subsequent tasks. Other tasks in this epic reference this principle.

## Behaviors

- VISION.md design principles section contains a new bullet about tonal separation over borders.

## Success criteria

- New bullet point in the Design principles section of VISION.md.
- Principle explains tonal layering: use --gr-color-bg-subtle, --gr-color-bg-surface, --gr-color-bg-default shade differences rather than borders.
- Concise â€” one bullet, 2-3 sentences max.

title: Remove structural borders and use tonal separation
description:
## Goal

Replace all 1px solid borders in the app shell with background shade differences, implementing the tonal separation principle from VISION.md.

## Why

The no-border design principle (task .1) is documented. This task applies it across the UI so all subsequent tasks (unified header, status bar) build on a border-free shell.

## Scope

Every `border` declaration in the app shell CSS modules except:
- The active tab indicator (`border-bottom: 2px solid` on `Tabs .trigger[data-state='active']`) â€” this is an accent, not a structural border.
- `border-radius` â€” not structural.
- `border: none` / `border-bottom: none` resets â€” harmless.
- The `Separator` in App.module.css â€” this is a resize handle, uses `background` not `border`, keep as-is.

### Borders to remove

| File | Selector | Declaration | Replacement |
|------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Header.module.css:6 | `.header` | `border-bottom: 1px solid var(--gr-color-border-default)` | Remove. Header already has `background: var(--gr-color-bg-subtle)` which contrasts with body `--gr-color-bg-default`. |
| Header.module.css:19 | `.themePicker` | `border: 1px solid var(--gr-color-border-default)` | Remove. Use `background: var(--gr-color-bg-surface)` contrast against header's `--gr-color-bg-subtle`. |
| StatusBar.module.css:5 | `.statusBar` | `border-top: 1px solid var(--gr-color-border-default)` | Remove. StatusBar already has `background: var(--gr-color-bg-subtle)`. |
| DiffViewer.module.css:18 | `.fileHeader` | `border-bottom: 1px solid var(--gr-color-border-muted)` | Remove. fileHeader has `background: var(--gr-color-bg-subtle)`, diff content uses default bg. |
| DiffViewer.module.css:36 | `.side[data-side='right']` | `border-left: 1px solid var(--gr-color-border-default)` | Remove. Left/right sides are already visually distinct via diff coloring. |
| DetailPanel.module.css:10 | `.thread` | `border-bottom: 1px solid var(--gr-color-border-muted)` | Remove border-bottom. Add `margin-bottom: var(--gr-space-sm)` gap + thread items already have `background: var(--gr-color-bg-surface)` on `.comment`. |
| DetailPanel.module.css:71 | `.infoRow` | `border-bottom: 1px solid var(--gr-color-border-muted)` | Remove border-bottom. Rely on row padding for visual separation. |
| Tabs.module.css:3 (packages/ui) | `.list` | `border-bottom: 1px solid var(--gr-color-border-default)` | Remove. Tab list has `background: var(--gr-color-bg-subtle)` contrasting with content area. |

## Behaviors

- No `1px solid` borders remain in app shell CSS (Header, StatusBar, DiffViewer, DetailPanel, Tabs) except the active tab accent.
- Regions remain visually distinct through background shade layering.
- Thread and infoRow separators use spacing instead of lines.

## Success criteria

- Zero `1px solid` border declarations in the listed CSS modules (excluding tab accent and border-radius).
- Visual separation achieved through existing `--gr-color-bg-*` variables.
- All existing tests pass.
- No new CSS custom properties introduced.

title: Merge app header and file header into single unified header
description:
## Goal

Merge the app Header (logo + theme picker) and the DiffViewer fileHeader (change badge + file path + old path) into a single top-level header bar.

## Why

Epic requirement 1: "Single unified header." Currently there are two header bars â€” the app header at the very top and the sticky file header inside DiffViewer. Merging them reduces visual clutter and matches the editor metaphor (one title bar).

## Scope

### Header component changes
- Accept optional props for the currently selected file: filePath, oldPath, changeType (from DiffFile).
- Render left: logo. Center/left-of-center: change badge + file path + old path (when a file is selected). Right: theme picker.
- When no file is selected, center area is empty or shows a subtle placeholder.

### DiffViewer changes
- Remove the .fileHeader div and its CSS entirely.
- DiffViewer no longer renders file path or change badge â€” that moves to Header.

### App.tsx changes
- Pass selectedDiff metadata (path, oldPath, changeType) to Header.
- No change to DiffViewer props â€” it still receives the full diff for rendering hunks.

### CSS changes
- Header.module.css: add styles for the file path area (use --gr-font-mono for paths).
- DiffViewer.module.css: remove .fileHeader rule.
- No borders. Tonal separation only.

## Behaviors

- Single header bar visible at top containing logo, file info, and theme picker.
- File info updates when a different file is selected.
- No file info shown when no file is selected.
- DiffViewer starts directly with hunk content (no sticky file header).

## Success criteria

- One header element in the DOM at the top of the app.
- No .fileHeader in DiffViewer.
- Header shows file path and change badge when a file is selected.
- All existing tests pass (update Header and DiffViewer tests as needed).

title: Add file metadata to editor-style status bar
description:
## Goal

Extend StatusBar to show file-local metadata on the right side when a file is selected: language and encoding.

## Why

Epic requirement 3: "Editor-style status bar â€” left side: review progress, branch. Right side: file-local metadata (language, encoding, line/col position)." This makes the status bar match the editor metaphor (VSCode shows language, encoding, line info in its status bar).

## Scope

### FileInfo type changes (fixtures/types.ts)
- Add language: string (e.g. "TypeScript", "CSS") to FileInfo interface.
- Add encoding: string (e.g. "UTF-8") to FileInfo interface.
- Update fixture data (fileInfo.json) with language and encoding values.

### StatusBar component changes
- Add optional props: language (string | null), encoding (string | null).
- Left side: review progress (existing).
- Right side: language + encoding, shown only when values are non-null.
- Use justify-content: space-between to push left/right apart.

### StatusBar.module.css changes
- Add right-side segment styles matching the existing progress style (same font, size, color).

### App.tsx changes
- Pass selectedFileInfo?.language and selectedFileInfo?.encoding to StatusBar.

### Test updates
- Update StatusBar tests to cover file metadata rendering.
- Update fixture data references if needed.

## Behaviors

- When a file is selected, status bar right side shows "TypeScript  UTF-8" (language and encoding separated by spacing).
- When no file is selected, right side is empty.
- Left side continues showing review progress unchanged.

## Success criteria

- StatusBar renders file language and encoding on the right when a file is selected.
- StatusBar right side is empty when no file is selected.
- FileInfo type includes language and encoding fields.
- All existing tests pass.

title: Diff viewer scroll performance
description:
## Context

Scrolling quickly through large multi-file diffs causes visible lag. Three layers of problems compound:

1. **DOM accumulation**: `LazyDiffViewer` uses a one-way IntersectionObserver latch â€” once a file becomes visible it stays mounted forever. After scrolling through 20 files, all 20 are fully rendered with every DOM node alive (~60kâ€“90k nodes for a 50-file diff). The inner `DiffViewer` row virtualizer (`@tanstack/react-virtual`) is dead in stacked mode because the outer container scrolls, not the inner `.viewer` divs.

2. **Cascading re-renders**: `activeFilePath` state lives in `App` â€” every scroll intersection triggers `setActiveFilePath` â†’ full App re-render. `selectedDiffs` is an unstable array reference. `clearCommitsOnFileSelect` factory produces 6 new function refs at JSX callsites. No `React.memo` on any heavy component.

3. **Per-element style cost**: `TokenSpans` applies inline `style={{ color }}` on every syntax token span (~2,400 live inline-style elements in the viewport), forcing unique computed style objects per element.

## Requirements

- [ ] File-level virtualization: only 3â€“5 files mounted at a time via `useVirtualizer`, with overscan of 1â€“2 files
- [ ] Sticky file headers work within the virtualized list
- [ ] `scrollToFile` imperative handle works with virtualized scrolling
- [ ] Active file detection works with virtualized items
- [ ] `DiffViewer` supports an `embedded` mode (no own scroll container, overflow visible) for stacked use
- [ ] `LazyDiffViewer` component deleted â€” replaced by virtualizer
- [ ] `activeFilePath` state isolated from App â€” scroll events don't re-render the full tree
- [ ] `selectedDiffs` memoized â€” stable reference when inputs unchanged
- [ ] Heavy components (`StackedDiffViewer`, `FileMinimap`, `FileTreePanel`) wrapped in `React.memo`
- [ ] `clearCommitsOnFileSelect` callbacks stabilized â€” no new refs at JSX callsites
- [ ] Token colors applied via CSS classes instead of inline styles
- [ ] `content-visibility: auto` with `contain-intrinsic-size` on file wrapper divs
- [ ] Server: parallel `git show` calls via `Effect.all`
- [ ] Server: diff content cache (TTL 30s, keyed by `oldRef:newRef:path`)

## Approach

Five phases, each building on the previous:

1. **State management fixes** (E, F, G, H) â€” isolated, low-risk, reduce noise for measuring virtualization impact
2. **File-level virtualization** (A) â€” biggest change, replaces LazyDiffViewer with useVirtualizer, adds embedded mode to DiffViewer
3. **CSS token class caching** (D) â€” replace inline style={{ color }} with injected CSS classes
4. **content-visibility** (B) â€” CSS-only optimization on file wrapper divs within the virtualized list
5. **Server parallelization + caching** (J, K) â€” parallelize git show calls, add diff content cache

### Key design decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Virtualization level | File-level in StackedDiffViewer | Avoids flattening all rows into one list (breaks sticky headers, per-file syntax highlighting). Inner row virtualizer stays for standalone mode |
| Virtualizer library | `@tanstack/react-virtual` | Already installed at 3.13.23. `estimateHeight()` already exists |
| Sticky headers | Kept via CSS `position: sticky` within each virtualized file section | User confirmed sticky headers must remain |
| DiffViewer scroll mode | `embedded` prop: when true, no `overflow-y: auto`, height determined by content | Eliminates the nested-scroll-container conflict in stacked mode |
| Token styling | CSS class per unique color, injected stylesheet | Eliminates ~2,400 inline style objects from viewport. Classes are stable across renders |
| activeFilePath isolation | Separate wrapper component or useRef+callback | Only FileMinimap consumes this value â€” no reason to re-render App |
| Server diff format | Keep old+new content (not unified diff) | Client needs full content for char-level highlighting, side-by-side alignment, syntax tokenization |

## Anti-patterns

- Do NOT flatten all files/hunks/rows into a single virtualizer item list â€” breaks sticky headers and per-file syntax highlighting
- Do NOT remove the inner DiffViewer row virtualizer â€” still needed for standalone single-file mode
- Do NOT switch to server-side unified diffs â€” client needs full file contents for char-level highlighting
- Do NOT use `useRef` for activeFilePath without a mechanism to update FileMinimap â€” it must still react to changes
- Do NOT add React.memo without first stabilizing the props â€” memo with unstable props is worse than no memo (false sense of safety)

## Success criteria

1. Scrolling through a 50-file diff maintains < 10k live DOM nodes at any point
2. Fast scrolling shows no visible jank or blank gaps (overscan covers scroll speed)
3. `scrollToFile` navigates to any file in the virtualized list
4. Sticky file headers remain visible while scrolling within a file's diff
5. No cascading App re-renders from scroll-driven state changes (verify with React DevTools profiler)
6. All existing tests pass without modification (or with minimal adaptation for the embedded prop)
notes:
## Revised approach (2026-04-07)

File-level virtualization (task .2) revealed that `position: absolute` + `transform: translateY()` breaks `position: sticky` headers and causes sub-pixel rendering artifacts. IntersectionObserver on dynamically mounted headers is unreliable.

New plan: replace two-level architecture with a **single flat virtualizer** (task .6). See REFACTOR.md for full design. Task .4 (content-visibility) closed as superseded â€” no file wrapper divs in the flat model.

Anti-pattern revision: the original "do NOT flatten" anti-pattern was wrong â€” flattening works when using `rangeExtractor` for sticky headers instead of CSS `position: sticky`.

title: Stabilize props and memoize heavy components
description:
## Goal

Stop cascading re-renders from scroll events and file selection before adding virtualization. Isolate `activeFilePath` state, memoize derived data, stabilize callback references, and wrap heavy components in `React.memo`.

## Context

Four state management problems in App.tsx cause the entire component tree to re-render on every scroll intersection event:

1. `activeFilePath` state (App.tsx:167) lives in App â€” every scroll intersection triggers setActiveFilePath â†’ full App re-render including FileTreePanel, DetailPanel, etc. Only FileMinimap needs this value.
2. `selectedDiffs` (App.tsx:261) is an unmemoized ternary â€” new array reference every render propagates to StackedDiffViewer and FileMinimap.
3. `clearCommitsOnFileSelect` (App.tsx:228-235) is a factory that produces 6 new function refs at JSX callsites (lines 294-299), forcing FileTreePanel to re-render.
4. No `React.memo` on StackedDiffViewer, FileMinimap, or FileTreePanel â€” every App re-render cascades through all heavy components.

## Behaviors

- Extract `activeFilePath` state into a wrapper component around FileMinimap + StackedDiffViewer (or use useRef+callback that only re-renders FileMinimap). Scroll events must not trigger App re-renders.
- Wrap `selectedDiffs` in `useMemo(() => commitSelection.isActive ? commitDiffs : fileDiffs, [commitSelection.isActive, commitDiffs, fileDiffs])`.
- Pre-bind all 6 `clearCommitsOnFileSelect` handlers via `useMemo` so no factory calls happen at JSX callsites. The resulting callbacks must be referentially stable across renders when `commitSelection` hasn't changed.
- Wrap `StackedDiffViewer`, `FileMinimap`, and `FileTreePanel` in `React.memo`. Ensure all props passed to them are stable (callbacks via useCallback, objects via useMemo).

## Success criteria

1. Scrolling through files does NOT re-render FileTreePanel or DetailPanel (verifiable via React DevTools profiler)
2. Changing file selection does NOT re-render unaffected siblings
3. All existing tests pass
4. No behavioral changes â€” the app works identically from the user's perspective

## Files

- `packages/web/src/App.tsx`
- `packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/StackedDiffViewer.tsx`
- `packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/FileMinimap.tsx`
- `packages/web/src/components/FileTree/FileTreePanel.tsx`

title: File-level virtualization in StackedDiffViewer
description:
## Goal

Replace the one-way `LazyDiffViewer` IntersectionObserver latch with file-level `useVirtualizer` so only 3â€“5 files are mounted at a time. Add an `embedded` prop to `DiffViewer` so it renders without its own scroll container in stacked mode.

## Context

`LazyDiffViewer` (StackedDiffViewer.tsx:17-46) mounts files when they scroll into view but never unmounts them. After scrolling through 20 files, all 20 are fully rendered. The inner `DiffViewer` row virtualizer is also dead in stacked mode because the outer container scrolls, not the inner `.viewer` divs â€” so all rows render unvirtualized.

The fix: virtualize at the file level in `StackedDiffViewer` using the already-installed `@tanstack/react-virtual`. `estimateHeight()` at line 12-15 already provides size estimates. Each file section includes its sticky header + DiffViewer. The virtualizer mounts/unmounts entire file sections as they enter/leave the viewport.

`DiffViewer` needs a new `embedded` prop. When `embedded=true`: no `overflow-y: auto` on `.viewer`, no fixed height constraint, the inner row virtualizer is disabled (rows render in full since the outer virtualizer controls visibility). When `embedded=false` (default): current behavior preserved for standalone use.

## Behaviors

- `StackedDiffViewer` uses `useVirtualizer` with `count: diffs.length`, `estimateSize` from `estimateHeight`, `measureElement` enabled, and `overscan: 2`.
- Delete `LazyDiffViewer` component entirely.
- Each virtual item renders: sticky file header + `<DiffViewer diff={diff} embedded />`.
- `scrollToFile(path)` uses `virtualizer.scrollToIndex(index)` where index is found from `diffs.findIndex`.
- Active file detection: use the virtualizer's visible range or an IntersectionObserver on the rendered file headers.
- `DiffViewer` with `embedded` prop: removes `overflow-y: auto` and `height: 100%` from `.viewer`, disables the inner `useVirtualizer` (renders all items directly), removes padding-top/padding-bottom virtual spacers.
- Sticky headers (`position: sticky; top: 0`) work because each file section is a direct child of the virtualizer's offset container.

## Success criteria

1. Scrolling through a 50-file diff maintains < 10k live DOM nodes at any point
2. Fast scrolling shows no visible blank gaps (overscan of 2 covers normal scroll speed)
3. `scrollToFile` navigates correctly to any file
4. Sticky file headers remain visible while scrolling within a file's diff
5. All existing StackedDiffViewer and DiffViewer tests pass (or are adapted for the embedded prop)
6. Standalone (non-embedded) DiffViewer behavior unchanged

## Files

- `packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/StackedDiffViewer.tsx` â€” replace LazyDiffViewer with useVirtualizer
- `packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/StackedDiffViewer.module.css` â€” adjust container styles if needed
- `packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/DiffViewer.tsx` â€” add embedded prop
- `packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/DiffViewer.module.css` â€” conditional overflow/height styles
- `packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/StackedDiffViewer.test.tsx` â€” adapt tests
- `packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/StackedDiffViewer.test.tsx` â€” adapt tests

title: CSS class caching for syntax token colors
description:
## Goal

Replace inline `style={{ color }}` on every syntax token span with CSS classes generated from the theme. Eliminates ~2,400 unique inline style objects from the visible viewport.

## Context

`TokenSpans.tsx:46-51` renders one `<span>` per syntax token with `style={{ color: token.color }}`. With ~30 tokens/line Ă— ~40 visible rows Ă— 2 sides = ~2,400 inline-style spans. Each creates a unique computed style object, forcing per-element style recalc on every paint.

The fix: build a stylesheet mapping unique color hex values to CSS classes (e.g., `.tk-a1b2c3 { color: #a1b2c3 }`), inject it once via a `<style>` element or `CSSStyleSheet`, and apply classNames instead of inline styles.

## Behaviors

- Create a utility that maps color hex strings to stable CSS class names (e.g., `tk-` + hex without `#`).
- When the highlight worker returns token maps, collect all unique colors and inject/update a `<style>` element with the class rules.
- `TokenSpans` applies `className={tokenClassName(token.color)}` instead of `style={{ color: token.color }}`.
- The stylesheet updates when the theme changes (new color set).
- For char-highlight spans that combine a highlight class with a color, apply both the token color class and the highlight class.

## Success criteria

1. No inline `style={{ color }}` on token spans in the rendered DOM
2. A single `<style>` element (or `CSSStyleSheet`) contains all token color rules
3. Theme switching updates the stylesheet correctly
4. Syntax highlighting appearance is identical to before
5. All existing DiffViewer and TokenSpans tests pass

## Files

- `packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/TokenSpans.tsx` â€” replace inline styles with classNames
- `packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/tokenStylesheet.ts` â€” new utility for class name generation and stylesheet injection
- `packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/DiffViewer.tsx` â€” wire stylesheet lifecycle if needed

title: Add content-visibility auto to file wrapper divs
description:
## Goal

Add `content-visibility: auto` with `contain-intrinsic-size` on file wrapper divs in the virtualized StackedDiffViewer. Supplementary CSS optimization that lets the browser skip layout and paint for off-screen files in the overscan range.

## Context

After file-level virtualization (task greppa-isi.2), the overscan range renders 1-2 extra files above and below the viewport. These files are mounted in the DOM but not visible. `content-visibility: auto` tells the browser to skip their layout and paint entirely until they scroll into view.

`estimateHeight()` already exists and provides the value for `contain-intrinsic-size`.

## Behaviors

- Each file section div in the virtualized list gets `content-visibility: auto` and `contain-intrinsic-size: auto <height>px` where height comes from `estimateHeight(diff)`.
- The file header is excluded from `content-visibility` (it needs to remain visible for sticky behavior).
- Only the DiffViewer content area within each file section gets the containment.

## Success criteria

1. Off-screen file diffs in the overscan range have `content-visibility: auto` in the computed styles
2. No visual difference when scrolling â€” files appear correctly as they enter the viewport
3. Sticky file headers still work correctly
4. All existing tests pass

## Files

- `packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/StackedDiffViewer.tsx` â€” apply content-visibility style to DiffViewer wrapper
- `packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/StackedDiffViewer.module.css` â€” add content-visibility class

title: Server: parallelize git show and add diff content cache
description:
## Goal

Parallelize the two sequential `git show` calls in the diff endpoint and add a diff content cache to prevent redundant subprocess spawns.

## Context

`Http.ts:99-102`: the diff handler fetches `oldContent` and `newContent` sequentially via two `yield*` calls. These are independent `git show` commands that can run in parallel.

The file list is cached (TTL 30s, max 50 entries) but individual file diff content is fetched fresh on every request. When navigating between commits or scrolling back to previously viewed files, the same `git show` commands run again unnecessarily.

## Behaviors

- Replace sequential `yield*` for `oldContent`/`newContent` with `Effect.all([git.getFileContent(oldRef, path), git.getFileContent(newRef, path)])` for parallel execution.
- Add a diff content cache alongside the existing file list cache:
  - Key: `${oldRef}:${newRef}:${filePath}`
  - TTL: 30 seconds (same as file list cache)
  - Max entries: 100 (higher than file list since individual files are smaller metadata)
  - Same eviction strategy as `fileListCache`
- Apply the same parallel + cache pattern to the worktree diff handler (Http.ts:164-199) where applicable (worktree content may change, so shorter TTL of 3s matching `WORKTREE_CACHE_TTL_MS`).

## Success criteria

1. Diff endpoint response time reduced (two git show calls run in parallel instead of sequentially)
