2. Repeated requests for the same file diff within 30s return cached content without spawning git processes
3. Cache eviction works correctly â€” stale entries are cleaned up
4. All existing server behavior preserved â€” responses are identical
5. Worktree diff handler also parallelized

## Files

- `packages/server/src/Http.ts` â€” parallelize git show, add diff content cache

title: Single flat virtualizer for StackedDiffViewer
description:
## Goal

Replace the two-level virtualizer architecture (file-level virtualizer + embedded DiffViewer) with a single flat virtualizer that treats all items across all files as one virtual list. Fixes sticky headers, active file tracking, and sub-pixel rendering artifacts.

## Context

Task .2 implemented file-level virtualization using `position: absolute` + `transform: translateY()` for each file block. This breaks:

- **Sticky file headers** â€” `position: sticky` doesn't work inside transformed containers
- **FileMinimap active tracking** â€” IntersectionObserver on dynamically mounted headers is unreliable
- **Sub-pixel rendering** â€” transform-based positioning causes grid-line artifacts between rows

The fix is described in REFACTOR.md: flatten all files into a single `FlatVirtualItem[]` array and virtualize that with one `useVirtualizer`. Use `rangeExtractor` to pin the current file's header (sticky behavior without CSS sticky). Derive active file from the virtualizer's visible items (no IntersectionObserver).

## Behaviors

**Build phase** â€” extend `buildVirtualItems` (or create `buildFlatItems`) to produce a flat array across all files:
- For each file: one `file-header` item, then its hunk-headers and diff-rows, then a `file-separator`
- Type: `{ kind: 'file-header'; diff: DiffFile } | { kind: 'hunk-header'; header: string } | { kind: 'diff-row'; row: DiffRow } | { kind: 'file-separator' }`

**Single virtualizer** in `StackedDiffViewer`:
- `useVirtualizer({ count: flatItems.length, estimateSize })` where estimateSize switches on item kind: `ROW_HEIGHT_ESTIMATE (36)` for diff-rows and hunk-headers, `40` for file-headers, `24` for separators
- One scroll container, standard virtualizer rendering (no `position: absolute` + `transform`)

**Sticky file headers** via `rangeExtractor`:
- Custom `rangeExtractor` that always includes the nearest file-header index above the current scroll position
- The pinned header renders with `position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 1`

**Active file tracking** â€” derived from virtualizer state:
- First visible `file-header` item in `getVirtualItems()` determines active file
- Replaces IntersectionObserver entirely
- `onActiveFileChange` callback fires when active file changes

**scrollToFile** â€” find target file-header index in flat array, call `virtualizer.scrollToIndex(index)`

**Syntax highlighting** â€” `useSyntaxHighlighting` called once per visible file (deduplicate by file path from visible items)

**Remove**:
- `DiffViewer` `embedded` prop and embedded rendering branch (`DiffViewer.tsx:108-132`)
- `DiffPane` component (`DiffPane.tsx`, `DiffPane.module.css`) â€” App.tsx imports StackedDiffViewer directly
- `FileMinimap` component (`FileMinimap.tsx`, `FileMinimap.module.css`, `FileMinimap.test.tsx`) â€” sticky headers + file tree sidebar replace its function
- `DiffRowRenderer` optional props â€” `measureRef` and `dataIndex` become required (no embedded mode)
- `countFileItems` / `estimateFileHeight` / `fileMetrics` in StackedDiffViewer â€” replaced by flat item array
- IntersectionObserver logic in StackedDiffViewer (headerRefs, observerRef, observer effect)

**Update App.tsx**:
- Import `StackedDiffViewer` directly instead of `DiffPane`
- `activeFilePath` state stays in a lightweight wrapper or StackedDiffViewer itself (no App re-renders)
- `scrollToFile` handle still exposed via `StackedDiffViewerHandle`

## Success criteria

1. Scrolling through a 50-file diff maintains < 10k live DOM nodes
2. File headers stick to the top of the viewport while scrolling within that file's diff
3. Active file in FileTreePanel updates correctly during scroll (derived from virtualizer, no IntersectionObserver)
4. `scrollToFile` navigates to any file in the flat list
5. Fast scrolling shows no blank gaps (appropriate overscan)
6. No sub-pixel rendering artifacts between rows
7. Standalone DiffViewer (single-file, non-stacked) still works with its own row virtualizer
8. All existing tests pass or are adapted

## Files

- `packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/StackedDiffViewer.tsx` â€” rewrite with flat virtualizer
- `packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/StackedDiffViewer.module.css` â€” update styles
- `packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/StackedDiffViewer.test.tsx` â€” adapt tests
- `packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/DiffViewer.tsx` â€” remove embedded prop/branch
- `packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/DiffViewer.module.css` â€” remove embedded class
- `packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/DiffRowRenderer.tsx` â€” make measureRef/dataIndex required
- `packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/buildVirtualItems.ts` â€” extend with flat item types or create buildFlatItems
- `packages/web/src/App.tsx` â€” replace DiffPane import with StackedDiffViewer
- `packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/DiffPane.tsx` â€” DELETE
- `packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/DiffPane.module.css` â€” DELETE
- `packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/FileMinimap.tsx` â€” DELETE
- `packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/FileMinimap.module.css` â€” DELETE
- `packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/FileMinimap.test.tsx` â€” DELETE

title: Editor-style UI shell
description:
Refactor the greppa web UI to feel like a dense, polished code editor. Remove the top header, add an activity rail, redesign the status bar, and tighten all spacing/typography to match design-v1.html mockup.

## Requirements
1. Remove the Header component. Replace with a 36px ActivityRail on the left edge (outside react-resizable-panels).
2. ActivityRail has two icons: Files (toggles file-tree panel visibility) and Settings (bottom, opens theme popover via Radix Popover).
3. Active rail icon shows a 2px left accent bar in interactive color.
4. Redesign StatusBar to be context-aware with segmented layout: left segments (review progress, comment count, commit SHA, working-tree label) vary by mode; right segments show mocked keyboard hint badges.
5. StatusBar modes: file-review, commit-review, working-tree, composer-open, review-complete â€” each renders different left/right segments per mockup.
6. Keyboard hint badges are styled kbd elements (mono font, surface bg, subtle box-shadow). Static/mocked for now.
7. Visual polish pass across all components to match mockup density: sidebar section headers (32px height, uppercase xs text, muted color), file rows (24px height, mono sm text), diff file header (36px), status bar (24px).
8. Font consistency: mono font for all data (file names, line counts, commit hashes, stats), UI font for labels and section names.
9. Color hierarchy: muted for secondary info, secondary for file names, primary for active/selected, interactive for accents, success/warning/danger for status badges only.
10. Panel stability: diff-viewer is the only panel that flexes on window resize. Side panels preserve pixel size.

## Success criteria
- Header component deleted, ActivityRail renders in its place.
- Theme picker works from settings popover in rail.
- StatusBar shows correct segments for each mode.
- All spacing, font sizes, heights match design-v1.html mockup values.
- File-tree panel collapses/expands via rail Files icon.
- No visual regressions in diff viewer or detail panel.

## Anti-patterns
- No placeholder/disabled icons in the rail â€” only render what works.
- No top-level header or logo â€” the rail IS the chrome.
- No color on file/directory names in the tree â€” only change badges carry color.
- Don't add keyboard shortcut handlers â€” hints are visual-only for now.
- Don't change any behavior/logic â€” this is purely visual/structural.

## Approach
Five sequential tasks:
1. Remove Header, restructure App shell layout to rail + panels + status bar.
2. Build ActivityRail component with Files icon (toggles file-tree panel) and Settings icon (opens theme popover).
3. Redesign StatusBar with segmented, mode-aware layout and mocked keyboard hints.
4. Visual polish pass â€” tighten spacing, font sizes, heights, colors across FileTreePanel, StackedDiffViewer file headers, DetailPanel, and section headers to match mockup density.
5. Panel resize stability â€” ensure side panels preserve pixel size and only diff-viewer flexes.

title: Remove Header and restructure App shell layout
description:
## Goal
Delete the Header component and restructure App.tsx so the layout is: ActivityRail (left) | resizable panels | StatusBar (bottom). This sets up the shell for subsequent tasks.

## Behaviors
- Delete `packages/web/src/components/Header/Header.tsx` and `Header.module.css`.
- Remove the `<Header />` import and render from `App.tsx`.
- Change the App layout from vertical (header â†’ panels â†’ statusbar) to: a horizontal flex row containing a future ActivityRail slot (empty 36px div for now) and the existing panel Group, with the StatusBar below.
- The ActivityRail slot sits outside react-resizable-panels, to the left of the Group.
- App.module.css: the `.app` remains `flex-direction: column` with `.body` as a horizontal flex row. Add a `.rail` placeholder class (36px wide, bg-inset, flex-shrink: 0).
- The rail placeholder div renders in the `.body` row, before the panel Group.

## Success criteria
- Header component files deleted.
- App renders without a top header bar.
- A 36px-wide placeholder div appears on the left edge (bg-inset color).
- Panel Group and StatusBar remain functional.
- No changes to any other component.

title: Build ActivityRail component with Files and Settings icons
description:
## Goal
Replace the empty rail placeholder div with an ActivityRail component containing two functional icons: Files (toggles file-tree panel visibility) and Settings (opens a theme picker popover via Radix Popover).

## Behaviors
- Create packages/web/src/components/ActivityRail/ActivityRail.tsx and ActivityRail.module.css.
- Install @radix-ui/react-popover as a dependency.
- ActivityRail renders a 36px-wide column with rail-top (Files icon) and rail-bottom (Settings icon) groups, matching the mockup layout.
- Files icon: uses IconFile from @tabler/icons-react (size 15, stroke 1.5). Clicking toggles the file-tree panel collapsed/expanded via react-resizable-panels imperative API. Active when file-tree panel is expanded â€” shows 2px left accent bar in interactive color.
- Settings icon: uses IconSettings from @tabler/icons-react (size 15, stroke 1.5). Wrapped in a Radix Popover trigger. Popover content shows the existing theme options from usePreferences as clickable items.
- Icon buttons: 36x32px, centered, relative position. Muted color default, primary color when active. Active state includes a ::before pseudo-element (2px wide, interactive color, left-aligned accent bar).
- Update App.tsx: replace the placeholder div with ActivityRail, pass a ref or callback to control the file-tree panel collapsed state.
- For panel toggle: use a ref to the file-tree Panel and call collapse()/expand() from react-resizable-panels API.

## Success criteria
- Rail shows Files icon (top) and Settings icon (bottom).
- Clicking Files icon toggles file-tree panel visibility.
- Active Files icon has 2px left accent bar.
- Clicking Settings icon opens a popover with theme options.
- Selecting a theme applies it immediately.
- Rail matches mockup dimensions and colors exactly.
- Existing tests still pass.

title: Redesign StatusBar with segmented mode-aware layout
description:
## Goal
Redesign StatusBar with a segmented, mode-aware layout and mocked keyboard hint badges matching the design-v1.html mockup.

## Behaviors
- Rewrite StatusBar.tsx and StatusBar.module.css to use a segmented layout: left segments, a flexible spacer, and right segments.
- StatusBar accepts a `mode` prop with values: file-review, commit-review, working-tree, review-complete, composer-open.
- Additional props vary by mode: reviewedCount, totalCount, commentCount, commitSha, modifiedCount, selectedFileCount.
- Left segments per mode:
  - file-review: reviewed count (success color) | comment count (warning color, only if > 0)
  - commit-review: commit SHA (interactive color) | reviewed count
  - working-tree: 'working tree' label (warning color) | 'N modified'
  - review-complete: reviewed count (success color, all reviewed)
  - composer-open: reviewed count (success color) | comment count (warning color)
- Right segments (kbd hints) per mode:
  - file-review: Space reviewed, Tab next file
  - commit-review: Space reviewed, Tab next file
  - working-tree: Tab next file
  - review-complete: Enter submit
  - composer-open: Cmd+Enter submit, Esc cancel
- Kbd elements styled as: mono font, xs size, surface bg, sm radius, 1px box-shadow.
- StatusBar height: 24px, bg-subtle, mono font, xs text.
- Remove language/encoding metadata segments (not in mockup).
- Update App.tsx to pass the new mode and relevant props to StatusBar.
- Update StatusBar tests and App tests accordingly.

## Success criteria
- StatusBar renders correct segments for each mode.
- Kbd badges match mockup styling (surface bg, box-shadow, mono font).
- 24px height, mono font, xs text size.
- All tests pass.

title: Visual polish pass matching mockup density
description:
## Goal
Tighten spacing, font sizes, heights, and colors across FileTreePanel, StackedDiffViewer file headers, Tree component, and section headers to match design-v1.html mockup density.

## Behaviors
- FileTreePanel section headers: height 32px, padding 0 var(--gr-space-lg), gap var(--gr-space-md). Title color to muted, letter-spacing 0.06em. Count font to mono.
- Tree items: min-height 24px, font-family mono, font-size sm, label color secondary.
- Remove change-type coloring from file/directory names in FileTree (only badges carry color).
- StackedDiffViewer file header: height 36px, padding 0 var(--gr-space-xl), gap var(--gr-space-lg). File path font to mono, font-size sm.
- Font consistency: mono for all data (file names, line counts, commit hashes, stats), UI font for labels/section names.
- Color hierarchy: muted for secondary info, secondary for file names, primary for active/selected, interactive for accents.

## Success criteria
- Section headers 32px, uppercase xs text, muted color, mono count.
- File rows 24px, mono sm text, secondary color.
- Diff file header 36px with mono file path.
- No color on file/directory names â€” only change badges carry color.
- All tests pass.

title: Panel resize stability â€” side panels preserve pixel size, diff-viewer flexes
description:
## Goal
Ensure side panels preserve their pixel size on window resize and only the diff-viewer panel flexes.

## Behaviors
- In App.tsx, set the file-tree panel and any detail panel to use fixed pixel sizes (not percentages) via react-resizable-panels props.
- The diff-viewer (center) panel should be the only panel that grows/shrinks on window resize.
- Use `defaultSize` in pixels (or minSize/maxSize constraints) so side panels don't shift when the window is resized.
- Verify that collapsing/expanding the file-tree panel via the ActivityRail icon still works correctly after these changes.

## Success criteria
- Resizing the browser window only changes the diff-viewer panel width.
- Side panels maintain their pixel width on window resize.
- File-tree panel collapse/expand via rail icon still works.
- All tests pass.

title: Epic: DetailPanel ActivityRail and StatusBar refactor into subcomponents and helpers
description:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- All planned extractions complete: LeftSegments, RightSegments, ThemePicker, calcPercent.
- Each extraction preserves existing component behavior â€” no change to rendered UI, theme switching, status display, or segment ordering.
- Extracted subcomponents stay co-located with their parent directory.
- calcPercent helper is module-scoped (not exported beyond its source file unless needed).
- Host component public props stay unchanged.
- Each extraction lands as its own atomic commit.

## Success criteria
- [ ] LeftSegments subcomponent extracted; StatusBar renders it as JSX.
- [ ] RightSegments subcomponent extracted; StatusBar renders it as JSX.
- [ ] ThemePicker subcomponent extracted; ActivityRail's popover renders it.
- [ ] calcPercent helper extracted; renderProgressBar and renderReviewed consume it.
- [ ] Every extracted unit has dedicated test coverage (calcPercent's guard behavior pinned by unit tests).
- [ ] No public prop signature changed on StatusBar or ActivityRail.
- [ ] All tests passing.
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing.

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO behavior changes in the same commit as an extraction (reason: keeps the commit reviewable as a pure move).
- NO temporary parallel names like StatusBarV2 (reason: extraction is atomic).
- NO expanding test coverage beyond what exercises the new unit (reason: scope creep).
- NO public prop changes on the host component (reason: downstream callers should not need simultaneous edits).
- NO drive-by cleanup of unrelated code (reason: pollutes the diff).
- NO bundling multiple extractions into one commit (reason: each extraction must be independently revertable).
- NO refactoring DetailPanel, FileInfoContent, or CommentsContent (reason: audit found no warranted extractions there; leave them alone).

## Durable decisions
- Subcomponents: co-located with parent directory (ThemePicker.tsx inside ActivityRail/, LeftSegments.tsx and RightSegments.tsx inside StatusBar/).
- Helpers: co-located with consumer file unless reused across modules.
- Naming: useX for hooks (camelCase), PascalCase.tsx for subcomponents, camelCase.ts for helpers.
- Test co-location: .test.ts/.test.tsx next to source.

## Approach
Four independent extractions, each landing as its own atomic commit. Existing component tests pin behavior; new tests exercise the extracted units. Work order starts with LeftSegments/RightSegments (largest, most characteristic win â€” converts plain render functions to real components that participate in React reconciliation), then the easier ThemePicker and calcPercent wins.

calcPercent is tiny but its duplicated guard (`total > 0 ? (reviewed / total) * 100 : 0`) is the only logic in StatusBar and warrants its own named unit.

## Architecture
- packages/web/src/components/StatusBar/LeftSegments.tsx (new) â€” left-side segment render from StatusBar.tsx:73-...
- packages/web/src/components/StatusBar/RightSegments.tsx (new) â€” right-side segment render from StatusBar.tsx:...-144.
- packages/web/src/components/StatusBar/calcPercent.ts (new) â€” percent-with-zero-guard helper.
- packages/web/src/components/ActivityRail/ThemePicker.tsx (new) â€” popover theme-picker block from ActivityRail.tsx:42-53.

## Design rationale

### Problem
StatusBar contains two plain render functions (LeftSegments/RightSegments) that bypass React reconciliation â€” they cannot be memoized or keyed cleanly. ActivityRail embeds a theme-picker popover inline, making the rail component carry preference-specific data. Audit also flagged a duplicated percent-with-zero-guard in StatusBar worth naming.

### Research findings

**Codebase audit (3-agent sweep, April 2026):**
- packages/web/src/components/StatusBar/StatusBar.tsx:73-144 â€” renderLeftSegments / renderRightSegments plain functions (~40+ combined lines) that should be components.
- packages/web/src/components/StatusBar/StatusBar.tsx:47-65 â€” `total > 0 ? (reviewed / total) * 100 : 0` duplicated across renderProgressBar and renderReviewed.
- packages/web/src/components/ActivityRail/ActivityRail.tsx:42-53 â€” 12-line inline theme-picker block inside Popover.Content.
- packages/web/src/components/DetailPanel/* â€” no findings; leave as-is.

**Existing patterns:**
- packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/FileHeader.tsx â€” reference for subcomponent co-located with parent.

### Approaches considered

#### 1. Four independent atomic commits (selected)

**What:** Each extraction lands as its own commit; start with LeftSegments/RightSegments.
**Pros:** Independently revertable, each commit a pure move.
**Cons:** Four commits for a small epic.
**Chosen because:** Matches the atomic-commit convention used in Epic A and Epic B.

#### 2. Leave plain render functions alone (rejected)

**What:** Keep StatusBar.tsx:73-144 as-is; only extract ThemePicker and calcPercent.
**Why explored:** Plain render functions are valid React idioms.
**Why rejected:** Audit specifically flagged them as bypassing reconciliation at 40+ combined lines. Converting to components enables memoization and keys, and the pattern will grow with future segments.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Performance profiling shows the component-based version is slower.

### Scope boundaries

**In scope:** Four extractions listed in Success criteria.
**Out of scope:** CommitList/FileTree refactors (Epic A), DiffViewer refactors (Epic B), DetailPanel/FileInfoContent/CommentsContent (no findings), theme data model changes, StatusBar segment set changes.

### Open questions
- Should LeftSegments/RightSegments accept `props` and `mode` as-is, or narrow to the specific fields they read? Narrow in the first task â€” pass only what's consumed.
- Does calcPercent warrant a dedicated test file or can it ride alongside StatusBar.test.tsx? Decide in the first extracting task; default to dedicated (match convention).

## Design discovery

### Key decisions made

| Question | Answer | Implication |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| First task? | LeftSegments/RightSegments conversion | Largest, most characteristic win â€” once components, the remaining extractions are trivial |
| Touch DetailPanel? | No | Audit found no warranted extractions; enforced via anti-pattern |
| calcPercent scope? | Named helper + tests | Tiny but its guard is the only logic in StatusBar |

### Dead-end paths

#### Leaving plain render functions as-is
**Why explored:** They are valid React idioms.
**What found:** Bypass reconciliation at 40+ combined lines; audit flagged them for correctness, not cosmetics.
**Why abandoned:** Converting to components enables memoization and keys.

### Open concerns raised
- Prop narrowing for LeftSegments/RightSegments -> resolve in first task; default to passing only consumed fields.
- calcPercent test location -> dedicated .test.ts file to match project convention.

title: Task 1: Convert StatusBar render functions to LeftSegments and RightSegments components
description:
## Goal
Convert the renderLeftSegments and renderRightSegments plain functions at StatusBar.tsx:73-144 into proper React subcomponents (LeftSegments, RightSegments) that participate in reconciliation. StatusBar renders them as JSX; existing tests continue to pass unchanged.

## Execution mode
AFK â€” behavior-preserving extraction. The render output must match the current plain-function implementation byte-for-byte.

## Behaviors
1. LeftSegments subcomponent accepts only the fields renderLeftSegments currently reads from props (narrow, not the whole props object); returns identical JSX.
2. RightSegments subcomponent accepts only the fields renderRightSegments currently reads (incl. mode); returns identical JSX.
3. StatusBar.tsx renders <LeftSegments ... /> and <RightSegments ... /> with stable keys where appropriate; inline render-function calls are removed.
4. Existing packages/web/src/components/StatusBar/StatusBar.test.tsx continues to pass without modification.

## References
- packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/FileHeader.tsx â€” subcomponent co-located with parent pattern.
- packages/web/src/components/StatusBar/StatusBar.tsx:73-144 â€” source of the conversion.
- packages/web/src/components/StatusBar/StatusBar.test.tsx â€” existing behavior pins.

## Success criteria
- [ ] packages/web/src/components/StatusBar/LeftSegments.tsx exists.
- [ ] packages/web/src/components/StatusBar/RightSegments.tsx exists.
- [ ] Both files have co-located .test.tsx files that render the segments with representative inputs and assert the expected segment set appears.
- [ ] StatusBar.tsx consumes them via JSX; no renderLeftSegments/renderRightSegments plain functions remain.
- [ ] Both subcomponents receive only the specific fields they read (narrowed props).
- [ ] All existing tests pass (StatusBar.test.tsx unchanged).
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing.
- [ ] Commit is atomic â€” only the two subcomponent extractions and their StatusBar call-site swap (plus new tests).

title: Multi-diff review workflow
description:
## Overview

Transform the diff viewer from single-file to multi-file review. Three capabilities:

1. **Multi-file diff scrolling** â€” stack multiple diffs vertically, scroll through continuously
2. **Multi-select** â€” click = single select, shift+click = add to selection, click directory = select all children
3. **Commits section** â€” third sidebar section showing commits between oldRef..newRef, click to scope review

## Requirements

### Multi-file diff stacking
- When multiple files are selected, render their diffs vertically in a single scrollable pane
- Sticky file header per diff showing: file path, change badge (M/A/D/R), review button
- Diagonal stripe separator between file diffs
- File navigation bar: "File X of Y" with prev/next arrows
- File minimap: thin vertical strip between sidebar and diff pane, segments sized proportionally to diff length, blue highlight for current scroll position
- Bidirectional sync: scrolling diff highlights current file in tree; clicking file in tree scrolls to its diff

### Multi-select interaction model
- Click = new selection (implicitly clears previous)
- Shift+click = add to selection
- Click directory = select all files under it
- Click section header = select all files in that section
- Selection resets when switching between sections (no cross-section multi-select)
- Single file click (no shift) returns to single-file view

### Commits section
- Third collapsible accordion section alongside Changes and Working tree
- Lists commits between oldRef and newRef (commits in the review)
- Each commit shows: short hash, commit message, relative time
- Flat list (not tree) â€” commits are linear
- Click commit = show that commit's changed files as stacked diffs (parent..commit)
- Shift+click commits = select range, show combined changes across selected commits

### Visual design
- File/directory names in tree use neutral text color â€” only change badges carry color
- Spring animations for accordion sections (existing pattern)
- Mockups at .mockups/multi-diff-and-commits.html and .mockups/multi-select.html

## Approach

Build front-to-back in three phases:

**Phase 1: Multi-file diff stacking.** Create a StackedDiffViewer wrapper that renders multiple DiffViewer instances vertically with sticky headers and separators. Add IntersectionObserver for scroll-to-tree sync. Keep existing single-file view as the default â€” stacked view activates only when multiple files are selected.

**Phase 2: Multi-select.** Extend Tree.Root to support selectionMode="multiple" with shift-click. Update useFileSelection to manage a Set of selected paths instead of a single path. Directory click collects all descendant file paths. Section header click selects all files in section. URL encoding for multi-select uses comma-separated paths or falls back to state-only (no URL for multi).

**Phase 3: Commits section.** Add listCommits to GitService (git log --format). New API endpoints: /api/commits for commit list, /api/commit/:sha/files for per-commit file list, /api/commit/:sha/diff/* for per-commit diff. Add CommitList component to FileTreePanel accordion. Commit selection reuses the same multi-select interaction model.

Each phase delivers independently usable value. Phase 1 works with directory clicks even before shift-click exists. Phase 3 builds on the multi-select from Phase 2.

## Anti-patterns
- Do NOT add checkboxes to tree items or commit items
- Do NOT add a "clear selection" button â€” clicking without shift is the clear
- Do NOT allow cross-section multi-select (e.g., files from Changes + commits)
- Do NOT merge all diffs into one virtual list â€” keep per-file DiffViewer instances for isolation
- Do NOT break single-file view â€” it must remain the default and feel identical to today

## Technical constraints
- DiffViewer takes single DiffFile â€” needs wrapper for stacking, not rewrite
- useFileSelection is single-select via URL â€” needs multi-select state
- Tree.Root uses selectionMode="single" â€” needs "multiple" mode with shift-click
- GitService has no listCommits method â€” must add git log wrapper
- Diff endpoint oldRef is overridden server-side to mergeBaseRef â€” per-commit diffs need new endpoint or explicit ref passthrough
- API returns one diff per request â€” multi-file view fetches in parallel, not batch
- DiffResponse contains raw strings (oldContent/newContent) â€” hunks computed client-side via worker

## Success criteria
- [ ] Can click a directory and scroll through all its files' diffs continuously
- [ ] Can shift+click to build up a custom multi-file selection
- [ ] Can click section header to view all files in that section
- [ ] File minimap shows proportional segments with current position
- [ ] Scrolling diff pane syncs highlighted file in tree
- [ ] Commits section lists commits between oldRef..newRef
- [ ] Can click a commit to view its changes as stacked diffs
- [ ] Can shift+click commits to select a range
- [ ] Single file click still works as today (no regression)
- [ ] Review state (reviewed/unreviewed) works per-file in multi-diff view

title: Create StackedDiffViewer component
description:
## Goal

Create a StackedDiffViewer component that renders multiple DiffViewer instances vertically with sticky file headers and separators. Foundation for multi-file review.

## Behaviors

- When given multiple DiffFiles, renders them vertically in a single scrollable pane
- Each file diff has a sticky header: file path, change badge (M/A/D/R), review button
- Diagonal stripe separator between file diffs
- File navigation bar at top: "File X of Y" with prev/next arrows
- IntersectionObserver on file headers tracks which file is currently in view
- Clicking prev/next scrolls to the adjacent file's header
- When given a single DiffFile, renders identically to current single-file DiffViewer (no visual regression)
- Component wraps existing DiffViewer â€” does not modify its internals

## Success criteria

- [x] StackedDiffViewer renders multiple diffs vertically with sticky headers
- [x] Diagonal stripe separator visible between file diffs
- [x] Navigation bar shows "File X of Y" and prev/next scrolls correctly
- [x] Single-file rendering is visually identical to current behavior
- [x] Tests cover: multi-file rendering, navigation, single-file fallback

title: Multi-select file tree and wire to StackedDiffViewer
description:
## Goal

Enable multi-select in the file tree so multiple files can be selected and their diffs shown in StackedDiffViewer.

## Behaviors

- Extend `useFileSelection` to manage a `Set<string>` of selected paths instead of a single path
- Click (no shift) = single select, clears previous selection, navigates URL to that file
- Shift+click = toggle file in/out of selection set, does not navigate URL
- Click directory = select all descendant file paths under it
- Click section header (Changes / Working tree) = select all files in that section
- Selection resets when switching between sections (no cross-section multi-select)
- Single file selected = single DiffViewer (current behavior); multiple = StackedDiffViewer
- Update `Tree.Root` usage in `FileTree` to support `selectionMode="multiple"`
- Wire `App.tsx` to pass all selected diffs to `StackedDiffViewer`

## References

- `packages/web/src/useFileSelection.ts` â€” current single-select hook
- `packages/web/src/components/FileTree/FileTree.tsx` â€” tree with `selectionMode="single"`
- `packages/web/src/components/FileTree/FileTreePanel.tsx` â€” section callbacks
- `packages/web/src/App.tsx:165` â€” where selected diff is passed to viewer
- `packages/ui/src/components/Tree/Tree.tsx` â€” UI tree wrapper
- Epic anti-patterns: no checkboxes, no "clear selection" button, no cross-section multi-select

## Success criteria

- [ ] Click selects single file, shift+click adds/removes from selection
- [ ] Click directory selects all children
- [ ] Click section header selects all files in section
- [ ] Multiple selected files render in StackedDiffViewer
- [ ] Single file click still works as today (no regression)
- [ ] Switching sections clears previous selection
- [ ] Tests cover: single select, shift+click toggle, directory select, section select, cross-section reset

title: Double-click directory to select, single-click to expand/collapse
description:
## Goal

Change directory click semantics so single-click expands/collapses (standard tree behavior) and double-click selects all children for stacked diff view.

## Context

Current behavior: clicking a directory row fires `onPointerDown` â†’ `selectAll(children)`, hijacking the click that would normally expand/collapse. The only collapse target is the 16px chevron â€” frustrating to hit. This change restores IDE-standard tree navigation.

## Behaviors

- Single-click on directory row â†’ expand/collapse (let React Aria handle natively)
- Double-click on directory row â†’ select all descendant files for multi-diff (`selectAll`)
- Shift+click on directory â†’ toggle all children in selection (unchanged)
- Single-click on file â†’ select file, open single diff (unchanged)
- Shift+click on file â†’ toggle in multi-select (unchanged)
- Section header click â†’ select all files in section (unchanged)

## Implementation

### 1. Split directory interaction in App.tsx selection handlers

In `handleSelectCommittedFile` and `handleSelectWorktreeFile` (lines 127-173):
- Remove the directory branch from the `onPointerDown`/click handler â€” directories should no longer trigger `selectAll` on single click
- Create new `handleDoubleClickCommittedFile` and `handleDoubleClickWorktreeFile` handlers that contain the directory `selectAll` logic

### 2. Add onDoubleClick prop to FileTree

In `packages/web/src/components/FileTree/FileTree.tsx`:
- Add `onDoubleClickFile?: (path: string) => void` prop to `FileTreeProps`
- Attach `onDoubleClick` to `Tree.Item` for directory nodes only
- Keep `onPointerDown` for file selection (single click on files unchanged)

### 3. Wire double-click in FileTreePanel

In `packages/web/src/components/FileTree/FileTreePanel.tsx`:
- Pass the new double-click handlers through to `FileTree`

### 4. Let React Aria handle directory expand/collapse natively

With `onPointerDown` no longer calling `selectAll` for directories, React Aria's built-in expand/collapse on `TreeItem` click takes over. The `expandedKeys` and `onExpandedChange` props already handle this â€” no changes needed to the expand/collapse plumbing.

## Anti-patterns

- Do NOT add a click delay to distinguish single from double click â€” React Aria handles expand/collapse on first click, double-click is a separate event
- Do NOT change shift+click behavior â€” it stays as toggle
- Do NOT modify the `useMultiSelect` hook â€” selection logic is unchanged, only the trigger gesture changes
- Do NOT touch section header behavior â€” it stays as single-click select-all

## Success criteria

- [ ] Single-click on directory expands/collapses it
- [ ] Double-click on directory selects all children â†’ stacked diff
- [ ] Shift+click on directory toggles all children (unchanged)
- [ ] Single-click on file opens single diff (unchanged)
- [ ] Chevron still works for expand/collapse
- [ ] No regression in multi-select file behavior

title: File minimap, scroll-to-tree sync, and review button wiring
description:
## Goal

Complete Phase 1 of the multi-diff review workflow by adding three connected features to StackedDiffViewer: a file minimap between sidebar and diff pane, bidirectional scroll-to-tree synchronization, and wiring the existing "Mark reviewed" button to persisted review state.

## Behaviors

### File minimap
- Thin vertical strip (4px wide) positioned between the sidebar and the diff pane
- One segment per file, sized proportionally to diff length (hunk count as proxy)
- Blue highlight on the segment corresponding to the currently visible file
- Clicking a minimap segment scrolls to that file's diff

### Scroll-to-tree sync
- IntersectionObserver on each file header in StackedDiffViewer tracks which file is in view
- When the visible file changes, StackedDiffViewer calls an onActiveFileChange(path) callback
- App.tsx passes this callback and uses it to highlight the active file in the tree (distinct from selection)
- Clicking a file in the tree while in multi-select scrolls to that file's diff in the stacked viewer

### Review button wiring
- Mark reviewed button in each file header toggles the file path in reviewedPaths (from useReviewState)
- Reviewed files show checkmark + Reviewed text with green accent styling
- StackedDiffViewer receives reviewedPaths: Set<string> and onToggleReviewed: (path: string) => void as props
- App.tsx wires these to useReviewState

## References

- packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/StackedDiffViewer.tsx - current component, button has no handler
- packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/StackedDiffViewer.module.css - styles
- packages/web/src/hooks/useReviewState.ts - persisted review state with reviewedPaths
- packages/web/src/App.tsx - wiring point
- .mockups/multi-diff-and-commits.html - mockup C shows minimap design

## Success criteria

- [ ] File minimap renders proportional segments with blue highlight for current file
- [ ] Scrolling diff pane updates minimap highlight and syncs active file to tree
- [ ] Clicking minimap segment scrolls to that file
- [ ] Clicking tree file in multi-select scrolls to that file's diff
- [ ] Mark reviewed toggles persisted state with visual feedback
- [ ] Tests cover: minimap rendering, scroll sync callback, review toggle

title: Add listCommits to GitService and commits API endpoint
description:
## Goal

- Per-commit file list reuses existing FilesApi by passing `parentSha..commitSha` as refs
- `CommitEntry` schema defined in `@greppa/core`
- Commits ordered newest-first (git log default)

## References

## Success criteria

- [ ] GitService.listCommits returns Effect<CommitEntry[], GitError>
- [ ] GET /api/commits?oldRef=X&newRef=Y returns commit list

title: Review any diff â€” CLI ref selection with merge-base semantics
description:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- [ ] CLI accepts two optional positional args: `greppa serve [old-ref] [new-ref]`
- [ ] No args: auto-detect default branch via `git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD` with fallback to `main` then `master`; new ref defaults to `HEAD`
- [ ] One arg: use as old ref; new ref defaults to `HEAD`
- [ ] Two args: use as old ref and new ref
- [ ] Always resolve diff base via `git merge-base oldRef newRef` â€” no two-dot semantics
- [ ] Both refs validated at startup with `git rev-parse --verify`; server refuses to start if either ref is invalid
- [ ] `RefsConfig` as `ServiceMap.Reference` threads resolved refs from CLI through Effect Layer to HTTP handlers (matches existing `RepoPath` pattern)
- [ ] `GET /api/refs` endpoint returns `{ oldRef, newRef }` â€” the original user-provided names (not SHAs)
- [ ] `useRefs` TanStack Query hook fetches once at startup with `staleTime: Infinity`
- [ ] `App.tsx` replaces hardcoded `'HEAD~1'`/`'HEAD'` with values from `useRefs`; shows loading state while refs fetch
- [ ] Ref resolution is a single clear step in the pipeline so a future `--pr 123` flag can slot in without structural changes

## Success criteria

- [ ] `greppa serve` with no args starts server, `GET /api/refs` returns default branch and HEAD
- [ ] `greppa serve main` starts server, `GET /api/refs` returns `{ oldRef: "main", newRef: "HEAD" }`
- [ ] `greppa serve main feature-branch` returns `{ oldRef: "main", newRef: "feature-branch" }`
- [ ] `greppa serve nonexistent` fails fast with clear error before server starts
- [ ] Browser loads refs from API and renders file list and diffs using those refs
- [ ] No hardcoded ref strings remain in client code
- [ ] All tests passing, pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- NO `..` or `...` delimiter parsing in ref-spec (reason: two positional args enable shell completion and are cleaner)
- NO two-dot diff semantics (reason: merge-base is always correct for review; two-dot adds noise when branches diverge)
- NO storing resolved SHAs in the API response (reason: downstream git commands accept branch names; display stays readable)
- NO lazy ref validation at API call time (reason: fail fast â€” no point starting a server with invalid refs)
- NO scattered `enabled` guards in hooks for ref loading (reason: single loading gate in App.tsx is cleaner)
- NO in-browser ref switching in this slice (reason: that's slice 3; restart server to change refs)

## Durable decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Ref format | Two positional args | Shell completion, cleaner than delimiter parsing |
| Diff semantics | Always merge-base | Two-dot adds noise for review; three-dot matches GitHub PR semantics |
| Ref threading | `RefsConfig` as `ServiceMap.Reference` | Matches existing `RepoPath` pattern; clean Layer composition |
| Default branch detection | `git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD` â†’ `main` â†’ `master` | Standard git convention with sensible fallbacks |
| Ref validation | Fail fast at startup | No point serving invalid refs |
| API response | Original ref names, not SHAs | Readable display; git commands accept names |
| Client loading | Loading gate in App.tsx | Single loading state, not scattered enabled checks |
| Future PR support | `--pr` flag as additive input path | `RefsConfig` is the universal abstraction; no structural changes needed |

## Approach

Thread ref-spec from CLI positional args through Effect's Layer system to an HTTP endpoint the client fetches at startup. Always resolve the actual diff base via `git merge-base` so the diff shows only what the branch introduced (matching GitHub PR "Files changed" semantics).

The server is authoritative for ref resolution â€” the client just asks "what refs am I reviewing?" and uses the answer everywhere. This keeps ref logic in one place and makes future input methods (PR number, URL) a backend concern.

## Architecture

- @greppa/server: `GitService` gains `resolveRef` (rev-parse --verify), `detectDefaultBranch` (symbolic-ref + fallbacks), and `mergeBase` methods. `RefsConfig` as new `ServiceMap.Reference`. `RefsApi` HttpApiGroup with single `GET /api/refs` endpoint.
- @greppa/cli: `serve` command gains two optional positional args, resolves refs, provides `RefsConfig` to Layer.
- @greppa/core: `RefsResponse` Effect Schema for `{ oldRef, newRef }`.
- @greppa/web: `useRefs` hook, `App.tsx` wiring with loading gate.

title: Add resolveRef, detectDefaultBranch, and mergeBase to GitService
description:
## Goal

Add ref resolution methods to GitService: validate refs, detect default branch, and compute merge-base. These are the git primitives that the ref-spec pipeline depends on.

## Execution mode

AFK

## Behaviors

1. `GitService.resolveRef(ref)` runs `git rev-parse --verify <ref>` and returns the ref string (not the SHA). Rejects refs starting with `-`. Fails with `ResolveRefError` if the ref does not exist.
2. `GitService.detectDefaultBranch()` tries `git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD` and strips the `refs/remotes/origin/` prefix. Falls back to checking if `main` ref exists (via resolveRef), then `master`. Fails with `DetectDefaultBranchError` if none found.
3. `GitService.mergeBase(ref1, ref2)` runs `git merge-base <ref1> <ref2>` and returns the SHA string. Fails with `MergeBaseError` if no common ancestor exists.
4. All three methods use `ChildProcess` from `effect/unstable/process`, matching existing `listFiles` and `getFileContent` patterns.
5. Typed error classes follow existing `GitError` pattern in `GitService.ts`.

## References

- packages/server/src/GitService.ts:1-113 â€” existing GitService with listFiles, getFileContent, ChildProcess usage, input validation, RepoPath reference
- packages/server/src/GitService.integration.test.ts:1-80 â€” existing integration test patterns with real git repo, TestLayer, guard-gated describes

## Success criteria

- [ ] `resolveRef` succeeds for valid branch names, tags, SHAs, and `HEAD~N` syntax
- [ ] `resolveRef` fails with `ResolveRefError` for nonexistent refs
- [ ] `resolveRef` rejects refs starting with `-`
- [ ] `detectDefaultBranch` returns the default branch name from the current repo
- [ ] `mergeBase` returns the common ancestor SHA for two refs
- [ ] `mergeBase` fails with `MergeBaseError` when no common ancestor exists
- [ ] Integration tests pass against the real greppa git repo
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

title: Add RefsConfig, RefsResponse schema, and GET /api/refs endpoint
description:
## Goal

Wire ref config from a ServiceMap.Reference through an HTTP endpoint so the client can discover which refs it is reviewing. This is the plumbing layer between GitService (task .1) and the CLI (next task).

## Execution mode

AFK

## Behaviors

1. `RefsConfig` defined as `ServiceMap.Reference` in `packages/server/src/GitService.ts` with shape `{ oldRef: string, newRef: string, mergeBaseRef: string }`. No default value â€” must be provided. Follows existing `RepoPath` pattern.
2. `RefsResponse` schema defined in `packages/core/src/index.ts` as `Schema.Struct({ oldRef: Schema.String, newRef: Schema.String })`. Only exposes user-facing ref names (not merge-base SHA).
3. `RefsApi` defined as `HttpApiGroup.make('refs')` in `packages/server/src/RefsApi.ts` with single `HttpApiEndpoint.get('getRefs', '/api/refs')` endpoint. Success response is `RefsResponse`.
4. `RefsHandlers` in `packages/server/src/Http.ts` reads `RefsConfig` from context and returns `{ oldRef, newRef }`.
5. `Api` in `packages/server/src/Api.ts` gains `.add(RefsApi)`.
6. `ApiRoutes` in `packages/server/src/Http.ts` gains `Layer.provide(RefsHandlers)`.
7. Integration test verifies `GET /api/refs` returns correct JSON when `RefsConfig` is provided in the test layer.

## References

- packages/server/src/GitService.ts:22-24 â€” existing RepoPath pattern for ServiceMap.Reference
- packages/server/src/HealthApi.ts:1-8 â€” simplest HttpApiGroup example
- packages/server/src/Api.ts:1-7 â€” API composition with .add()
- packages/server/src/Http.ts:44-46 â€” handler binding pattern
- packages/server/src/Http.ts:116-126 â€” ApiRoutes and makeHttpLayer composition
- packages/core/src/index.ts:1-27 â€” existing schema definitions
- packages/server/src/Http.integration.test.ts:1-80 â€” integration test patterns

## Success criteria

- [ ] `RefsConfig` exists as `ServiceMap.Reference` with `{ oldRef, newRef, mergeBaseRef }` shape
- [ ] `RefsResponse` schema in core has `{ oldRef, newRef }` shape
- [ ] `GET /api/refs` returns `{ oldRef, newRef }` from the provided `RefsConfig`
- [ ] Integration test provides `RefsConfig` and verifies the endpoint response
- [ ] All tests passing, pre-commit hooks passing

title: Wire CLI positional args through ref resolution to RefsConfig
description:
## Goal

Add two optional positional args to `greppa serve` that flow through ref resolution into `RefsConfig`, which the HTTP layer already consumes via the refs endpoint.

## Execution mode

AFK

## Behaviors

1. `serve` command accepts two optional positional args: `greppa serve [old-ref] [new-ref]`. Uses `Argument.string` with `Argument.optional` from Effect CLI.
2. No args: calls `GitService.detectDefaultBranch()` for oldRef, defaults newRef to `"HEAD"`.
3. One arg: uses it as oldRef, defaults newRef to `"HEAD"`.
4. Two args: uses as oldRef and newRef.
5. Both refs validated via `GitService.resolveRef()` â€” server refuses to start if either is invalid.
6. Merge-base computed via `GitService.mergeBase(oldRef, newRef)`.
7. `makeHttpLayer` gains a `refsConfig` parameter and provides `Layer.succeed(RefsConfig, refsConfig)` internally.
8. The serve command handler resolves refs, builds refsConfig, and passes it to `makeHttpLayer`.

title: Add useRefs hook and wire App.tsx to fetch refs from API
description:

title: Editor-quality file tree sidebar
description: ## Context The file tree sidebar is functional but visually basic compared to VSCode/JetBrains. It lacks file icons, indent guides, compact folders, and uses an outline-based selection instead of a full-width background fill. ## Design summary Bring the file tree to editor-quality through 7 visual improvements in 3 phases: ### Phase 1: Tree styling (CSS-only) 1. **Tighter row height** â€” 22px rows matching VSCode density 2. **Full-width selection bar** â€” replace orange outline with background fill spanning the entire sidebar width. Key: never put level-based padding on TreeItem â€” only on inner elements (chevron/spacer) 3. **Indent guides** â€” subtle vertical lines via ::before pseudo-element using --tree-item-level CSS variable and repeating-linear-gradient ### Phase 2: File icons + change type colors 4. **File/folder icons** â€” vscode-material-icons (910 icons, folder open/closed pairs, rendering). New FileIcon component in packages/ui 5. **Change type colors on filename text** â€” color the label itself per status (green=added, red=deleted, yellow=modified, muted=renamed) using existing design tokens ### Phase 3: Data transforms 6. **Compact folders** â€” flatten single-child directory chains (VSCode's algorithm: loop-based merge, deepest dir path as identity) 7. **Directory status propagation** â€” folders inherit highest-priority changeType from descendants (added > deleted > modified > renamed) ## Key decisions | Decision | Choice | Rationale | |---|---|---| | Icon library | vscode-material-icons | Folder open/closed support, 910 icons, img rendering vs dangerouslySetInnerHTML | | Indentation | Chevron padding-left | React Aria pattern â€” row stays full-width for selection highlight | | Compact folder identity | Deepest dir path | Stable React Aria id for selection/expansion | | Status propagation | Data layer (buildFileTree) | Pure function, testable, no render-time computation | | Row height | 22px (VSCode standard) | Editor-density familiarity | ## Constraints - TreeItemContent render props provide: hasChildItems, isExpanded, level - Tree UI component is generic (packages/ui), FileTree is domain-specific (packages/web) - Must follow TDD, CSS modules, existing design token system - Deleted files DO propagate status (unlike VSCode â€” review tool needs this)
design:
## Context

The file tree sidebar is functional but visually basic compared to VSCode/JetBrains. It lacks file icons, indent guides, compact folders, and uses an outline-based selection instead of a full-width background fill.

## Design summary

Bring the file tree to editor-quality through 7 visual improvements in 3 phases:

### Phase 1: Tree styling (CSS-only)
1. **Tighter row height** â€” 22px rows matching VSCode density
2. **Full-width selection bar** â€” replace orange outline with background fill spanning the entire sidebar width. Key: never put level-based padding on TreeItem â€” only on inner elements (chevron/spacer)
3. **Indent guides** â€” subtle vertical lines via ::before pseudo-element using --tree-item-level CSS variable and repeating-linear-gradient

### Phase 2: File icons + change type colors
4. **File/folder icons** â€” vscode-material-icons (910 icons, folder open/closed pairs, img rendering). New FileIcon component in packages/ui
5. **Change type colors on filename text** â€” color the label itself per status (green=added, red=deleted, yellow=modified, muted=renamed) using existing design tokens

### Phase 3: Data transforms
6. **Compact folders** â€” flatten single-child directory chains (VSCode algorithm: loop-based merge, deepest dir path as identity)
7. **Directory status propagation** â€” folders inherit highest-priority changeType from descendants (added > deleted > modified > renamed)

## Key decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Icon library | vscode-material-icons | Folder open/closed support, 910 icons, img rendering vs dangerouslySetInnerHTML |
| Indentation | Chevron padding-left | React Aria pattern â€” row stays full-width for selection highlight |
| Compact folder identity | Deepest dir path | Stable React Aria id for selection/expansion |
| Status propagation | Data layer (buildFileTree) | Pure function, testable, no render-time computation |
| Row height | 22px (VSCode standard) | Editor-density familiarity |

## Constraints

- TreeItemContent render props provide: hasChildItems, isExpanded, level
- Tree UI component is generic (packages/ui), FileTree is domain-specific (packages/web)
- Must follow TDD, CSS modules, existing design token system
- Deleted files DO propagate status (unlike VSCode â€” review tool needs this)

title: Compact folders and change type propagation
description:
## Goal

Add two pure data-layer transforms to buildFileTree: compactTree() flattens single-child directory chains into one row, and propagateChangeType() bubbles the highest-priority changeType from descendants up to parent directories.

## Behaviors

- compactTree: when a directory has exactly one child and that child is also a directory, merge them into a single row with a joined name (e.g. "src/events"). Use the deepest dir's path as the node path (React Aria id). Recurse through the entire tree.
- propagateChangeType: walk the tree bottom-up. Each directory inherits the highest-priority changeType from its descendants. Priority order: added > deleted > modified > renamed.
- Both transforms are called inside buildFileTree after sortNodes.
- FileNode type gets a displayName field for compact path labels.
- FileTree renders displayName ?? name in the label.

## Success criteria

- [ ] compactTree merges single-child directory chains into one row
- [ ] Compact row uses deepest dir path as id, joined names as displayName
- [ ] propagateChangeType sets changeType on directories from descendants
- [ ] Priority order: added > deleted > modified > renamed
- [ ] Both transforms are pure functions called in buildFileTree
- [ ] FileTree renders displayName when present
- [ ] New tests cover both transforms and rendering
- [ ] pnpm test, pnpm typecheck, pnpm lint all pass

title: Persisted UI state via createPersistedStore factory
description:
## Context

greppa has no systematic state persistence. Theme uses a one-off `useTheme` hook with `useSyncExternalStore` + localStorage. Panel sizes use react-resizable-panels' built-in persistence. Everything else (reviewed files, tree expansion, filters, sort order, view mode, scroll positions) is lost on page refresh.

## Requirements

- [ ] Generic `createPersistedStore(key, schema, defaults)` factory that returns a React hook
- [ ] Uses `useSyncExternalStore` with localStorage (same pub/sub pattern as existing `useTheme`)
- [ ] Validates reads with Effect `Schema`, falls back to defaults on decode failure
- [ ] `usePreferences()` store â€” global UI preferences, single localStorage key `gr-preferences`
- [ ] Theme migrated from standalone `useTheme` into `usePreferences`
- [ ] `useReviewState(sessionId)` store â€” per-review UI state, key `gr-review:<sessionId>`
- [ ] Per-review state: collapsed tree paths, file verdicts, active filters, scroll positions
- [ ] FileTree expansion state persisted via `useReviewState`
- [ ] Reviewed files persisted via `useReviewState`

## Design decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Storage backend | localStorage | Per-browser persistence, no server roundtrip. Vision doc reserves SQLite for review data (comments, sync state) |
| Validation | Effect Schema | Already in the stack (via @greppa/core). Handles schema evolution (new fields get defaults). STACK.md designates Schema as shared across all layers |
| React integration | useSyncExternalStore | Proven pattern in useTheme. No new dependencies needed |
| Architecture | Generic factory + per-concern stores | createPersistedStore(key, schema, defaults) generalizes useTheme pattern. Each concern calls factory with its own key |
| Global vs per-review | Two namespaces | Global prefs (gr-preferences) for theme/view-mode/sort. Per-review state (gr-review:sessionId) for tree expansion, verdicts, scroll positions |
| Review state key | Server-assigned session ID | Decoupled from git refs (which are mutable). Session ID comes from SQLite reviews table. Client stores gr-review:sessionId |
| Panel sizes | Leave with react-resizable-panels | Already persisted by the library. No reason to absorb |
| New dependencies | None | useSyncExternalStore + Schema + localStorage is sufficient |

## Anti-patterns

- Do not add Zustand, Jotai, or any state management library.
- Do not persist review data (comments, verdicts synced to GitHub) client-side â€” that belongs in server-side SQLite.
- Do not absorb panel size persistence from react-resizable-panels.
- Do not create a React Context provider â€” the factory uses module-level singletons like useTheme.

## Success criteria

1. createPersistedStore factory exists with full test coverage
2. Schema validation rejects malformed localStorage data and falls back to defaults
3. usePreferences() hook manages theme (and future global prefs) with localStorage persistence
4. useTheme hook is removed; all consumers use usePreferences().theme
5. useReviewState(sessionId) hook persists tree expansion and reviewed files per session
6. Different session IDs produce isolated state (no cross-contamination)
7. FileTree reads collapsed/expanded state from useReviewState
8. Reviewed files in useFileSelection persist across page refreshes via useReviewState
9. No new runtime dependencies added to packages/web
10. All existing tests pass

title: Build createPersistedStore factory
description:
## Goal

Create a generic `createPersistedStore(key, schema, defaults)` factory function that returns a React hook backed by `useSyncExternalStore` + localStorage. This generalizes the existing `useTheme` pattern into a reusable primitive.

Add `effect` (`4.0.0-beta.40`) as a direct dependency to `packages/web`.

## Behaviors

1. Returns defaults when localStorage key is missing
2. Returns defaults when localStorage contains malformed JSON
3. Returns defaults when localStorage data fails Schema validation
4. Returns valid stored data when localStorage contains conforming JSON
5. `set()` writes partial updates to localStorage and notifies listeners
6. `set()` merges into existing state (does not replace)
7. Multiple stores with different keys are independent (no cross-contamination)
8. `getServerSnapshot` returns defaults

## API shape

```typescript
const useStore = createPersistedStore({
  key: "gr-example",
  schema: ExampleSchema,
  defaults: { theme: "dark", fontSize: 14 },
});

// In a component:
const { state, set } = useStore();
// state is fully typed from Schema
// set merges partial updates: set({ fontSize: 16 })
```

## Files to create

- `packages/web/src/hooks/createPersistedStore.ts`
- `packages/web/src/hooks/createPersistedStore.test.ts`

## Files to modify

- `packages/web/package.json` â€” add `effect: 4.0.0-beta.40` to dependencies

## Success criteria

1. All 8 behaviors have passing tests
2. `pnpm --filter @greppa/web test` passes with no regressions
3. Factory uses Effect Schema for validation, not hand-rolled guards
4. Same `useSyncExternalStore` + listener pub/sub pattern as `useTheme`

title: Build usePreferences and migrate theme from useTheme
description:
## Goal

Create `usePreferences()` hook using `createPersistedStore` for global UI preferences (starting with theme), then migrate all `useTheme` consumers and delete the old hook.

## Behaviors

1. usePreferences returns `{ state, set }` with typed preferences including theme
2. Theme defaults to OS preference (dark/light detection via matchMedia)
3. setTheme via set({ theme }) updates localStorage key `gr-preferences`
4. set({ theme }) also sets `data-theme` attribute on document.documentElement
5. All existing useTheme consumers work identically after migration
6. useTheme.ts and useTheme.test.ts are deleted
7. usePreferences has full test coverage

## Files to create
- packages/web/src/hooks/usePreferences.ts
- packages/web/src/hooks/usePreferences.test.ts

## Files to modify
- packages/web/src/components/Header/Header.tsx â€” use usePreferences instead of useTheme
- packages/web/src/components/Header/Header.test.tsx â€” update mock
- packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/DiffViewer.tsx â€” use usePreferences instead of useTheme
- packages/web/src/components/DiffViewer/DiffViewer.test.tsx â€” update mock
- packages/web/src/App.test.tsx â€” update mock

## Files to delete
- packages/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts
- packages/web/src/hooks/useTheme.test.ts

## Success criteria
1. usePreferences() hook manages theme with localStorage persistence under `gr-preferences` key
2. useTheme is fully removed â€” no imports remain
3. data-theme attribute on documentElement still works
4. All existing tests pass (with updated mocks)
5. No behavioral changes visible to users

title: Build useReviewState hook with dynamic session key
description:
## Goal

Create `useReviewState(sessionId)` hook for per-review persisted state. Unlike `usePreferences` (singleton), this hook manages multiple independent stores keyed by `gr-review:<sessionId>`.

## Design

Cannot reuse `createPersistedStore` directly (designed for singletons). Build a standalone hook using `useSyncExternalStore` + Effect Schema + localStorage with per-key caching. Shared subscriber list across all sessions â€” any mutation notifies all listeners, each re-reads its own key.

## Schema

```typescript
Schema.Struct({
  collapsedPaths: Schema.Array(Schema.String),
  reviewedPaths: Schema.Array(Schema.String),
})
```

Defaults: `{ collapsedPaths: [], reviewedPaths: [] }`

## Behaviors

1. Returns defaults when localStorage key is missing
2. Returns defaults when localStorage data fails Schema validation
3. Returns valid stored data when localStorage contains conforming JSON
4. set() merges partial updates and notifies listeners
5. Different session IDs produce isolated state (no cross-contamination)
6. Same session ID from multiple components shares state

## Files to create

- packages/web/src/hooks/useReviewState.ts
- packages/web/src/hooks/useReviewState.test.ts

## Success criteria

1. All 6 behaviors have passing tests
2. pnpm --filter @greppa/web test passes
3. No integration with FileTree or useFileSelection yet â€” that is a separate task

title: Integrate FileTree expansion and reviewed files with useReviewState
description:
## Goal

Wire `useReviewState` into the component tree so FileTree expansion and reviewed files persist across page refreshes.

## Changes

### FileTree expansion (controlled mode)
- FileTree: accept `expandedKeys` and `onExpandedKeysChange` props (Tree.Root already supports controlled mode via react-aria-components)
- App.tsx: use `useReviewState('default')` to read/write collapsedPaths, derive expandedKeys = allDirectoryIds - collapsedPaths
- On expansion change: compute collapsedPaths = allDirectoryIds - expandedKeys, persist via set()

### Reviewed files persistence
- Extract reviewedPaths from useFileSelection local state into useReviewState
- useFileSelection receives reviewedPaths from caller (or reads from useReviewState directly)
- Auto-mark-as-reviewed on file select still works, but writes to useReviewState

### Session ID
- Use hardcoded `'default'` session ID until server sessions exist

## Success criteria
1. FileTree expansion state survives page refresh
2. Reviewed files survive page refresh
3. All existing tests pass with updated mocks/props
4. No behavioral changes visible to users beyond persistence

title: File icons and change type colors
description: ## Goal Add file type icons via vscode-material-icons and change type color classes to filenames in the FileTree. ## Behaviors - FileIcon component in packages/ui renders tags from self-hosted vscode-material-icons SVGs - FileIcon accepts name (filename/dirname), isDirectory, and isExpanded props - Files show type-specific icons (typescript.svg, react_ts.svg, etc.) resolved via getIconForFilePath - Folders show open/closed icons: getIconForDirectoryPath returns e.g. folder-src, appending -open for expanded state - Filename text color reflects changeType: green (added), red (deleted), yellow (modified), muted (renamed) - Directories show colored text but no letter badge when they have a changeType - FileTree integrates FileIcon, passing isExpanded from TreeItemContent render props - Vite config copies vscode-material-icons SVGs to /material-icons/ in public output ## Success criteria - [ ] FileIcon component renders with correct src for files and directories - [ ] Folder icons toggle between closed and open variants based on isExpanded - [ ] Filename text uses change type color CSS classes - [ ] Directories with changeType show colored text, no Badge - [ ] Files with changeType show both colored text and Badge - [ ] tags load from static assets, no dangerouslySetInnerHTML - [ ] Existing FileTree tests pass - [ ] New tests cover FileIcon and color class rendering - [ ] pnpm test, pnpm typecheck, pnpm lint all pass

title: Execution mode
description: AFK

title: Goal
description: Serve raw old/new file content from git for a given file path and refs. Client fetches via TanStack Query hook. This enables the client-side diff computation that follows.

title: Caddy reverse proxy for local dev
description: Set up Caddy as a reverse proxy so each worktree gets predictable *.greppa.localhost domains with zero manual DNS config.

comments.author: Oskar Grunning
comments.text:
EPIC CONTRACT

## Requirements

1. Each worktree gets three domains resolved via *.localhost (RFC 6761, no /etc/hosts needed):
   - <name>.greppa.localhost -> web app (DEV_PORT)
   - <name>.greppa.localhost/api/* -> API server (API_PORT)
   - playground.<name>.greppa.localhost -> UI playground (PLAYGROUND_PORT)
   - Exception: main worktree uses greppa.localhost (no prefix)

2. worktreeEnv.ts writes a per-worktree Caddy snippet to ~/.config/caddy/greppa/<name>.caddy and runs caddy reload -- fully automated, no manual steps.

3. A root Caddyfile in the repo uses import ~/.config/caddy/greppa/*.caddy to aggregate all active worktrees.

4. Vite dev servers print the Caddy-mapped URL (e.g. https://greppa.localhost) alongside the normal localhost:port output.

5. Remove the Vite /api proxy from packages/web/vite.config.ts -- Caddy handles this now.

6. STACK.md updated to document the Caddy setup.

## Anti-patterns

- No dnsmasq, systemd-resolved, or /etc/hosts modifications
- No manual steps per worktree -- agents must be able to create worktrees and get working URLs automatically
- No hardcoded ports in the Caddyfile -- everything derives from .env.local / worktreeEnv.ts

## Success criteria

- pnpm dev in any worktree writes the Caddy snippet, reloads Caddy, and prints the mapped URLs
- Navigating to greppa.localhost in a browser reaches the web app
- Navigating to greppa.localhost/api/... reaches the API server
- Navigating to playground.greppa.localhost reaches the UI playground
- A second worktree gets its own subdomain without conflicting with the first
- No DNS configuration required on the host machine

title: Extend worktreeEnv to generate Caddy snippet and reload
description:
Extend scripts/worktreeEnv.ts to:

1. Add a buildCaddySnippet function that generates a Caddy config snippet for a single worktree. Takes worktree name, apiPort, devPort, playgroundPort. Produces the three domain blocks (web, /api handle, playground).

2. Write the snippet to ~/.config/caddy/greppa/<name>.caddy (mkdir -p the directory).

3. Write a root Caddyfile to ~/.config/caddy/greppa/Caddyfile that does import ./*.caddy (only if it does not exist yet).

4. Run caddy reload --config ~/.config/caddy/greppa/Caddyfile after writing the snippet. Fail gracefully if caddy is not installed or not running.

5. Add a CADDY_DOMAIN env var to .env.local output (e.g. CADDY_DOMAIN=greppa.localhost or CADDY_DOMAIN=feat-foo.greppa.localhost).

6. Export buildCaddySnippet and add tests for it in worktreeEnv.test.ts.

Domain scheme:
- main -> greppa.localhost, playground.greppa.localhost
- <other> -> <name>.greppa.localhost, playground.<name>.greppa.localhost

title: Remove Vite /api proxy and print Caddy-mapped URL
description:
1. Remove the proxy config from packages/web/vite.config.ts â€” Caddy handles /api routing now.

2. Add Caddy-mapped URL to Vite server output. Read CADDY_DOMAIN from env and print it alongside the normal localhost:port URL.

Covers epic requirements 4 and 5.

title: Document Caddy reverse proxy setup in STACK.md
description:
Add a section to docs/STACK.md documenting:
- The Caddy reverse proxy architecture (*.greppa.localhost domains)
- How worktreeEnv.ts auto-generates snippets
- The root Caddyfile import pattern
- How to install and start Caddy for local dev

Covers epic requirement 6.

title: Epic: CommitList and FileTree refactor into hooks and subcomponents
description:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- All planned extractions complete: useCommitSelectionKeys, useSectionScrollLock, PanelSection, FileTreeItem, handleTreeItemPointerDown helper, commit-row pointer-down helper.
- Each extraction preserves existing component behavior â€” no change to rendered UI, selection state, keyboard nav, or event contracts.
- Extracted hooks live in packages/web/src/hooks/ with co-located .test.ts (match useCommitSelection.ts / useCommitSelection.test.ts).
- Extracted subcomponents stay co-located with their parent (FileTreeItem.tsx inside FileTree/, PanelSection.tsx inside FileTree/).
- Host component public props stay unchanged.
- Each extraction lands as its own atomic commit.

## Success criteria
- [ ] useCommitSelectionKeys hook extracted; CommitList consumes it.
- [ ] useSectionScrollLock hook extracted; FileTreePanel's three sections consume it.
- [ ] PanelSection subcomponent extracted; FileTreePanel renders three instances.
- [ ] FileTreeItem subcomponent extracted; FileTree renders via it recursively.
- [ ] handleTreeItemPointerDown helper extracted.
- [ ] Commit-row pointer-down helper extracted.
- [ ] Every extracted unit has dedicated test coverage.
- [ ] No public prop signature changed on CommitList, FileTree, or FileTreePanel.
- [ ] All tests passing.
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing.

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO behavior changes in the same commit as an extraction (reason: keeps the commit reviewable as a pure move; behavior tweaks belong in a separate follow-up).
- NO temporary parallel names like CommitListV2 or FileTreeNew (reason: extraction is atomic; rename callers in the same commit).
- NO expanding test coverage beyond what exercises the new unit (reason: scope creep; coverage gaps are separate tasks).
- NO public prop changes on the host component (reason: downstream callers and tests should not need simultaneous edits).
- NO drive-by cleanup of unrelated code (reason: pollutes the diff; split into its own commit/task if worth doing).
- NO bundling multiple extractions into one commit (reason: each extraction must be independently revertable).

## Durable decisions
- Hooks directory: packages/web/src/hooks/ (matches useCommitSelection, useCommitFileSelection, useCommitList).
- Subcomponents: co-located with parent (matches FileHeader.tsx alongside StackedDiffViewer.tsx).
- Naming: useX for hooks (camelCase), PascalCase.tsx for subcomponents.
- Test co-location: .test.ts/.test.tsx next to source (matches project convention).

## Approach
Six independent extractions, each landing as its own atomic commit. Existing component tests pin behavior; new unit tests exercise the extracted units in isolation. Work order favors the pathfinder slice (useCommitSelectionKeys â€” familiar territory, already-pinned behavior) and then ships the highest-ratio cleanups (useSectionScrollLock + PanelSection, which together remove ~60 lines of duplication in FileTreePanel).

No runtime behavior changes, no prop changes, no restructuring beyond the listed extractions. The refactor is defensive: tests prove each move is a no-op.

## Architecture
- packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitSelectionKeys.ts (new) â€” selectedKeys memo + expandedKeys state from CommitList.tsx:59-110.
- packages/web/src/hooks/useSectionScrollLock.ts (new) â€” bodyRefs + lockScroll + handleExpandComplete from FileTreePanel.tsx:90-126.
- packages/web/src/components/FileTree/PanelSection.tsx (new) â€” section button + motion.div + content block from FileTreePanel.tsx:130-256.
- packages/web/src/components/FileTree/FileTreeItem.tsx (new) â€” recursive renderItem from FileTree.tsx:47-108.
- handleTreeItemPointerDown â€” helper fn, co-located with FileTreeItem or as separate module under FileTree/.
- Commit-row pointer-down helper â€” helper fn, co-located with CommitList.tsx.

## Design rationale

### Problem
Three components carry state/logic clusters that are hard to test in isolation and harder to reason about inline. The audit surfaced six concrete sites where cohesive behavior can be named and extracted without changing what the UI does. Status quo: testable only through full render trees; duplicated scroll-lock logic copied across three sections of FileTreePanel.

### Research findings

**Codebase audit (3-agent sweep, April 2026):**
- packages/web/src/components/CommitList/CommitList.tsx:59-110 â€” expanded-aware selection state, coherent behavior, currently untestable in isolation.
- packages/web/src/components/CommitList/CommitList.tsx:128-143 â€” commit-row onPointerDown closes over expandedKeysRef and branches across onSelectCommit/onSelectAllFilesInCommit.
- packages/web/src/components/FileTree/FileTree.tsx:58-84 â€” tree-item onPointerDown with three behavioral branches.
- packages/web/src/components/FileTree/FileTree.tsx:47-108 â€” recursive renderItem inside component body; blocks React.memo.
- packages/web/src/components/FileTree/FileTreePanel.tsx:90-126 â€” triple bodyRef + lockScroll + handleExpandComplete concern repeated three times.
- packages/web/src/components/FileTree/FileTreePanel.tsx:130-256 â€” three ~30-line section blocks with identical structure.

**Existing patterns:**
- packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitSelection.ts â€” reference for hook + test co-location.
- packages/web/src/components/StackedDiffViewer/FileHeader.tsx â€” reference for subcomponent co-located with parent.

### Approaches considered

#### 1. Six independent atomic commits (selected)

**What:** Each extraction lands as its own commit, starting with useCommitSelectionKeys as a pathfinder.
**Pros:** Independently revertable, each commit is a pure move, test pinning is incremental.
**Cons:** Six commits for one logical concern.
**Chosen because:** Anti-pattern rules require atomic extractions, and the units have no interdependencies.

#### 2. One refactor mega-commit (rejected)

**What:** Move everything in one commit.
**Why explored:** Fewer commits, single reviewable unit.
**Why rejected:** Violates the NO bundling multiple extractions anti-pattern. A mistake in one extraction would force reverting five unrelated ones.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Future tooling lets us revert sub-hunks of a commit cleanly.

### Scope boundaries

**In scope:** Six extractions listed in Success criteria.
**Out of scope:** DiffViewer and StackedDiffViewer refactors (Epic B), StatusBar/ActivityRail/DetailPanel refactors (Epic C), behavior changes, prop signature changes.

### Open questions
- Should handleTreeItemPointerDown live in FileTree/ as a separate file or co-located inside FileTreeItem.tsx? Defer to first task touching it.

## Design discovery

### Key decisions made

| Question | Answer | Implication |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| Scope split? | Three epics by component area (not one mega-epic) | This epic owns CommitList + FileTree only |
| Include medium/low-value items? | Yes | Pointer-down helpers included even though lower-ratio |
| First task? | useCommitSelectionKeys | Pathfinder â€” code recently touched, tests already pin behavior |

### Dead-end paths

#### Mega-refactor across all components
**Why explored:** Fewer commits, one review.
**What found:** Conflicts with atomic-commit anti-pattern; risk amplifies.
**Why abandoned:** Independent extractions have no ordering constraint â€” splitting wins on risk and reviewability.

### Open concerns raised
- Component prop stability across the epic -> enforced via success criterion + anti-pattern.
- Test coverage scope creep -> enforced via anti-pattern (NO coverage expansion beyond new-unit tests).

title: Task 1: Extract useCommitSelectionKeys hook from CommitList
description:
## Goal
Extract the expanded-aware selection-key derivation from CommitList.tsx:59-110 into a reusable, independently testable useCommitSelectionKeys hook in packages/web/src/hooks/. CommitList consumes the hook; existing CommitList tests continue to pass unchanged.

## Execution mode
AFK â€” behavior-preserving extraction. Existing CommitList.test.tsx pins the behavior; the hook is a pure refactor.

## Behaviors
1. Hook useCommitSelectionKeys({ commits, selectedShas, selectedCommitFiles }) returns { selectedKeys, expandedKeys, setExpandedKeys } with identical semantics to the current inline useMemo + useState.
2. Hook's selectedKeys matches the current implementation for: no selection, single-commit selectedShas collapsed, single-commit selectedShas expanded (all files highlighted), file-level selection drops commit highlight, all-files-selected implies commit selection, file-selection suppresses stale selectedShas from other commits.
3. Hook exposes expandedKeys/setExpandedKeys for the Tree.Root consumer (preserves current behavior of the inline useState).
4. CommitList.tsx imports and consumes the hook; inline selectedKeys memo and expandedKeys useState are removed.
5. Existing packages/web/src/components/CommitList/CommitList.test.tsx continues to pass without modification to assertions.

## References
- packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitSelection.ts â€” hook file structure + test co-location pattern.
- packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitFileSelection.ts â€” hook with URL param + Set-based state pattern.
- packages/web/src/components/CommitList/CommitList.tsx:59-110 â€” source of the extraction.
- packages/web/src/components/CommitList/CommitList.test.tsx â€” existing behavior pins.

## Success criteria
- [ ] packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitSelectionKeys.ts exists and exports the hook.
- [ ] packages/web/src/hooks/useCommitSelectionKeys.test.ts covers the six behaviors above.
- [ ] CommitList.tsx delegates selectedKeys/expandedKeys to the hook; no inline useMemo for selectedKeys remains.
- [ ] All existing tests pass (CommitList.test.tsx unchanged).
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing.
- [ ] Commit is atomic â€” only the hook extraction and its CommitList call-site swap (plus new tests).
